Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance.d: Pays monthly retirement and survivors benefits to eligible workers and their families based on lifetime earnings. Funded primarily through the 6.2% OASDI payroll tax (employer and employee each) on earnings up to the taxable maximum.

Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund.d: Finances Medicare Part A, covering inpatient hospital stays, skilled nursing facility care, hospice, and some home health services for Americans aged 65+ and certain disabled individuals. Funded by the 1.45% Medicare payroll tax on all earnings (employer and employee each) plus a 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax on high earners.

Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance.d: Finances Medicare Part B (physician and outpatient services) and Medicare Part D (prescription drug coverage). Funded approximately 75% by general revenues and 25% by beneficiary premiums.
Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance.b: approximately 62 million Part B enrollees and 52 million Part D enrollees (FY2024)

Interest on the Public Debt.d: Pays interest on all outstanding Treasury securities (bills, notes, bonds, TIPS, and savings bonds) held by the public and intragovernmental accounts. This is a mandatory appropriation that covers the federal government’s borrowing costs.
Interest on the Public Debt.b: not applicable (payments to bondholders and trust funds, not individual program beneficiaries)

Grants to States for Medicaid.d: Provides federal matching funds to states for the Medicaid program, which covers health care for low-income individuals, families, pregnant women, children, elderly, and people with disabilities. The federal share (FMAP) varies by state, ranging from 50% to about 77%.

Refunding Internal Revenue Collections.d: Processes refunds of overpaid federal income taxes, payroll taxes, and related credits (including the Earned Income Tax Credit and Child Tax Credit) to individual and corporate taxpayers. This is a permanent, indefinite appropriation that fluctuates with filing activity and tax law changes.
Refunding Internal Revenue Collections.b: approximately 120 million individual tax refunds issued annually

Federal Disability Insurance Trust Fund.d: Pays monthly benefits to workers who become disabled before reaching full retirement age and to their eligible dependents. Funded by a dedicated 0.9% portion of the OASDI payroll tax (employer and employee each).
Federal Disability Insurance Trust Fund.b: approximately 7.4 million disabled workers plus 1.4 million dependents (FY2024)

Civil Service Retirement and Disability.d: Funds retirement, disability, and survivor annuities for federal civilian employees under the Civil Service Retirement System (CSRS) and the Federal Employees Retirement System (FERS). Financed by employee and agency contributions plus a general fund transfer to cover the unfunded liability.

Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Progra.d: Provides monthly electronic benefits (EBT) to low-income households to purchase food, serving as the nation’s largest domestic anti-hunger program. Eligibility is based on household income generally at or below 130% of the federal poverty level.
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Progra.b: approximately 42 million individuals in 22 million households (FY2024)

Military Personnel (Army, Navy, Marine C.d: Funds pay, allowances (housing, subsistence), bonuses, permanent change of station moves, and other compensation for active-duty military service members across all branches. Also covers Social Security and Medicare tax contributions for uniformed personnel.

Operation and Maintenance (Army, Navy, A.d: Finances day-to-day military operations, training, equipment maintenance, base operations, facilities sustainment, and civilian workforce pay across all service branches. The largest single discretionary appropriation category in the federal budget.
Operation and Maintenance (Army, Navy, A.b: not applicable (operational readiness of the armed forces)

Defense Working Capital Fund.d: A revolving fund that operates on a business-like basis, providing goods and services (supply management, depot maintenance, transportation, logistics) to DoD components on a reimbursable basis. Activities set prices to recover costs, maintaining a self-sustaining cash flow cycle.
Defense Working Capital Fund.b: not applicable (internal DoD supply chain and logistics)

Procurement (Aircraft, Missiles, Weapons.d: Funds the acquisition of major weapons systems, aircraft, ships, vehicles, missiles, ammunition, and other military equipment for all service branches. Appropriations are typically available for three years (obligation) to accommodate multi-year production contracts.

Government Payment for Annuitants, Emplo.d: Pays the government’s share (approximately 72%) of health insurance premiums for federal employees, retirees, and their dependents under the Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) Program. Covers enrollment in a wide range of private health insurance plans.
Government Payment for Annuitants, Emplo.b: approximately 8.2 million federal employees, retirees, and dependents

Tenant-Based Rental Assistance (Section.d: Provides portable housing vouchers to very low-income families, the elderly, and persons with disabilities to rent units in the private market, with HUD paying the difference between 30% of household income and the fair market rent. Administered by approximately 2,200 local public housing agencies.

Supplemental Security Income Program (SS.d: Provides monthly cash assistance to aged, blind, and disabled individuals with limited income and resources, establishing a uniform federal income floor. Funded entirely from general revenues (not the Social Security trust funds).

Federal Pell Grant Program.d: Awards need-based grants to low-income undergraduate students to help pay for college tuition, fees, and living expenses, with no repayment required. Maximum award set annually by Congress (up to $7,395 in award year 2024-25).

Student Financial Assistance (Federal Di.d: Administers the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan Program, issuing subsidized and unsubsidized loans, PLUS loans, and consolidation loans directly to students and parents for postsecondary education. Also includes costs related to income-driven repayment plans and loan forgiveness programs.
Student Financial Assistance (Federal Di.b: approximately 43 million federal student loan borrowers with outstanding balances

Highway Trust Fund (Federal-Aid Highways.d: Finances federal-aid highway construction, maintenance, and safety improvement projects on the National Highway System and other eligible roads, distributed to states via formula and competitive grants. Funded primarily by the 18.4 cents/gallon federal gasoline tax, supplemented by general fund transfers.
Highway Trust Fund (Federal-Aid Highways.b: not applicable (benefits all highway users; funds distributed to 50 states, DC, territories, and tribal governments)

Defense Health Program (TRICARE).d: Provides medical and dental care to active-duty service members, retirees, and their dependents through the Military Health System, including direct care at military treatment facilities and purchased care through the TRICARE managed care network. Covers worldwide health services for the uniformed services community.

Medical Services / Medical Community Car.d: Funds inpatient, outpatient, mental health, long-term care, and community care (including the MISSION Act) health services for eligible veterans through the Veterans Health Administration’s 171 medical centers and 1,100+ outpatient sites. Largest integrated health care system in the United States.
Medical Services / Medical Community Car.b: approximately 9.1 million veterans enrolled in VA health care

Compensation and Pensions.d: Provides tax-free disability compensation payments to veterans with service-connected injuries/illnesses and income-based pension benefits to wartime veterans who are permanently and totally disabled. Also includes dependency and indemnity compensation (DIC) for survivors of service members who died on duty or from service-connected causes.
Compensation and Pensions.b: approximately 6.7 million veterans and survivors receiving compensation or pension

Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHI.d: Provides federal matching funds to states to cover uninsured children in families with incomes too high to qualify for Medicaid but too low to afford private coverage, with enhanced federal matching rates (generally 65%-85%). States design their programs as Medicaid expansions, separate CHIP programs, or combinations.
Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHI.b: approximately 7.4 million children and 370,000 pregnant women enrolled

Science / Exploration / Space Operations.d: Funds NASA’s space science missions (James Webb Space Telescope, Mars exploration, Earth observation), human exploration programs (Artemis/Moon-to-Mars), and space operations (International Space Station, launch services, space communications). Supports the nation’s civil space program and advances scientific understanding.
Science / Exploration / Space Operations.b: not applicable (scientific research and exploration; supports thousands of research institutions, universities, and contractors)

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Bureau of.d: Funds the FBI’s law enforcement, intelligence, and national security operations including counterterrorism, cybercrime, public corruption, and organized crime investigations. Supports approximately 35,000 personnel across 56 field offices and international legal attaché offices.
Salaries and Expenses, Federal Bureau of.b: General public, federal law enforcement community

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison Sy.d: Operates and maintains 122 federal correctional institutions of varying security levels, plus residential reentry centers, housing federal inmates convicted of federal offenses. Covers inmate care, custody, security, education, vocational training, medical services, and community corrections.
Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison Sy.b: approximately 158,000 federal inmates in BOP custody

Salaries and Expenses, U.S. Customs and.d: Funds border security operations including Border Patrol agents, officers at ports of entry, trade enforcement, agricultural inspections, and anti-smuggling operations along the nation’s borders and at 328 ports of entry. Employs the largest federal law enforcement workforce in the United States.
Salaries and Expenses, U.S. Customs and.b: not applicable (border security; approximately 63,000 employees including ~20,000 Border Patrol agents and ~27,000 CBP officers)

Disaster Relief Fund.d: Provides federal assistance following presidential disaster declarations, funding individual assistance (housing, personal property), public assistance (infrastructure repair for state/local governments), and hazard mitigation grants. The primary funding source for federal disaster response and recovery operations.
Disaster Relief Fund.b: varies widely by year; millions of individuals and thousands of state/local government entities and nonprofits affected by declared disasters

National Institutes of Health (NIH â€” c.d: Funds biomedical and public health research through intramural labs and extramural grants to universities, medical schools, and research institutions worldwide, covering basic science, clinical trials, and translational research across all major diseases. The world’s largest public funder of biomedical research.
National Institutes of Health (NIH â€” c.b: not applicable (research enterprise; funds approximately 50,000 competitive grants to over 2,500 institutions)

Centers for Disease Control and Preventi.d: Funds disease surveillance, outbreak investigation, immunization programs, chronic disease prevention, injury prevention, environmental health, and global health security activities to protect the nation from health threats. Provides critical grants and cooperative agreements to state and local health departments.
Centers for Disease Control and Preventi.b: not applicable (public health infrastructure; grants to all 50 states, territories, and hundreds of local health departments)

Forest Service (National Forest System,.d: Manages 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands for multiple uses including timber, recreation, watershed protection, and wildlife habitat, and leads federal wildland firefighting operations. Wildland fire suppression and fuels management constitute the largest share of the Forest Service budget.
Forest Service (National Forest System,.b: not applicable (public lands management; serves approximately 160 million recreational visitors annually)

Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Sec.d: Maintains the safety, security, and reliability of the U.S. nuclear weapons stockpile through the Stockpile Stewardship Program, without underground testing, and manages nuclear weapons production and infrastructure modernization at sites including Los Alamos, Sandia, and Lawrence Livermore. Also supports nuclear nonproliferation, naval reactors, and emergency response.
Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Sec.b: not applicable (national security; operates through seven national laboratories and production plants)

Environmental Management (Defense Enviro.d: Cleans up the environmental legacy of Cold War-era nuclear weapons production and government-sponsored nuclear energy research across the DOE complex, including radioactive waste treatment, spent fuel management, and facility decommissioning. The world’s largest environmental remediation program.
Environmental Management (Defense Enviro.b: not applicable (environmental remediation at 16 major sites across 12 states; protects surrounding communities and ecosystems)

Operations, Federal Aviation Administrat.d: Funds FAA’s air traffic control operations, aviation safety regulation, certification of aircraft and pilots, and maintenance of the national airspace system. Supports over 300 air traffic control facilities and approximately 26,000 controllers.
Operations, Federal Aviation Administrat.b: Commercial airlines, general aviation, flying public

School Improvement Programs (Supporting.d: Provides formula and competitive grants to states and school districts under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA/ESSA) for teacher quality improvement, after-school programs, charter schools, magnet schools, and other school improvement initiatives. Complements the larger Title I grants to local educational agencies.
School Improvement Programs (Supporting.b: approximately 25,000 school districts and 100,000 public schools serving millions of students

Special Education (Grants to States unde.d: Provides formula grants to states to assist in providing free appropriate public education (FAPE) to children and youth with disabilities ages 3-21 under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA). Represents the federal share of the excess cost of educating students with disabilities.
Special Education (Grants to States unde.b: approximately 7.5 million students with disabilities receiving special education services

Community Development Fund (Community De.d: Provides flexible annual formula grants to entitled cities (population 50,000+), urban counties, and states for community development activities directed toward neighborhood revitalization, economic development, and improved community facilities and services benefiting low- and moderate-income persons. At least 70% of funds must benefit LMI populations.
Community Development Fund (Community De.b: approximately 1,200 entitled communities and all 50 states; benefits millions of LMI residents

Operation of Indian Programs / Indian Af.d: Funds trust responsibilities to 574 federally recognized tribes, including tribal governance, natural resources management, law enforcement, social services, and education through 183 BIE-funded schools. Supports tribal self-determination and sovereignty through direct services, contracts, and self-governance compacts.
Operation of Indian Programs / Indian Af.b: approximately 1.9 million American Indians and Alaska Natives who are members of federally recognized tribes

Resource Management, U.S. Fish and Wildl.d: Manages the National Wildlife Refuge System (568 refuges on 850 million acres), administers the Endangered Species Act, enforces federal wildlife laws, manages migratory bird conservation, and operates national fish hatcheries. Also administers grants to states for wildlife and sport fish restoration.
Resource Management, U.S. Fish and Wildl.b: not applicable (wildlife conservation; refuges host approximately 65 million visitors annually)

Operation of the National Park System.d: Operates and maintains 429 units of the National Park System, including national parks, monuments, historic sites, recreation areas, seashores, and battlefields, preserving natural and cultural resources while providing for public enjoyment. Also administers grant and partnership programs for historic preservation and recreation.
Operation of the National Park System.b: not applicable (public lands; approximately 312 million recreational visits annually)

U.S. Army Corps of Engineers â€” Civil W.d: Plans, designs, builds, and operates water resources infrastructure including navigation channels, flood risk management systems (levees, dams, floodwalls), hydropower facilities, aquatic ecosystem restoration, and regulatory permits for wetlands and waterways. Manages over 700 dams and 12,000 miles of inland waterways.
U.S. Army Corps of Engineers â€” Civil W.b: not applicable (water infrastructure; benefits communities nationwide, especially in flood-prone areas and along navigable waterways)

Foreign Military Financing.d: Provides grants (and historically loans) to foreign governments for the purchase of U.S. defense articles, defense services, and military training, with the majority of funding historically going to Israel, Egypt, Jordan, and other strategic partners. Advances U.S. national security objectives through allied capacity building and interoperability.
Foreign Military Financing.b: not applicable (approximately 50+ partner countries; Israel receives approximately $3.3 billion annually under the 2016 MOU)

Economic Support Fund.d: Provides economic assistance to countries of strategic importance to U.S. foreign policy, supporting governance, rule of law, economic reforms, education, health, and stabilization programs. Grants are flexible and can address a wide range of development and diplomatic objectives.
Economic Support Fund.b: not applicable (foreign assistance to approximately 70+ countries and territories)

Global Health Programs (PEPFAR/Global He.d: Funds the President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) and global health initiatives addressing HIV/AIDS, malaria (PMI), tuberculosis, maternal and child health, family planning, nutrition, and pandemic preparedness in developing countries. PEPFAR alone has supported antiretroviral treatment for over 20 million people.
Global Health Programs (PEPFAR/Global He.b: not applicable (global health; PEPFAR supports HIV treatment for approximately 20.1 million people in 50+ countries)

Disaster Loans Program Account, Small Bu.d: Provides low-interest direct loans to businesses, homeowners, and renters to repair or replace property damaged by declared disasters. The primary federal disaster loan program for non-farm private sector losses.

Rural Development Programs (Rural Housin.d: Provides loans, loan guarantees, and grants for rural housing (single-family and multi-family), water and waste disposal systems, electric and telecommunications infrastructure, broadband, and rural business development. Serves communities with populations generally under 35,000.
Rural Development Programs (Rural Housin.b: approximately 60 million people living in rural America; specific program beneficiaries include hundreds of thousands of homeowners and thousands of communities

Federal Transit Administration (Transit.d: Provides formula and competitive grants to state and local transit agencies for capital investments, operations, planning, and safety improvements for public transportation systems including buses, subways, light rail, commuter rail, and ferries. The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law significantly increased authorized funding levels.
Federal Transit Administration (Transit.b: not applicable (public transit systems; approximately 900+ transit agencies serving millions of daily riders in urban and rural areas)

National Railroad Passenger Corporation.d: Provides annual federal grants to Amtrak for the operation, maintenance, and capital improvement of intercity passenger rail service across a 21,000-mile national network serving 500+ stations in 46 states. The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law provided $66 billion over five years for passenger and freight rail.
National Railroad Passenger Corporation.b: approximately 28.6 million Amtrak riders annually (FY2024 ridership)

Hazardous Substance Superfund (EPA Super.d: Funds the investigation and cleanup of the nation’s most contaminated hazardous waste sites (National Priorities List), emergency response to hazardous substance releases, and enforcement actions against responsible parties. The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law provided an additional $3.5 billion for Superfund cleanups.
Hazardous Substance Superfund (EPA Super.b: not applicable (environmental cleanup; approximately 1,340 NPL sites with 73 million Americans living within three miles of a Superfund site)

Research and Related Activities, Nationa.d: Funds fundamental scientific research and education across all non-medical fields of science and engineering through competitive grants to universities and research institutions. Supports approximately 25% of all federally funded basic research at academic institutions.
Research and Related Activities, Nationa.b: University researchers, graduate students, scientific community

Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Sec.d: Maintains the safety, security, and effectiveness of the U.S. nuclear weapons stockpile without underground testing. Funds warhead life extension programs, stockpile stewardship, and nuclear weapons production infrastructure.
Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Sec.b: U.S. national defense and nuclear deterrence infrastructure

Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund.d: Provides subsidized crop insurance to American farmers and ranchers to protect against losses from natural disasters, price declines, and revenue shortfalls. Administered by the Risk Management Agency within USDA.
Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund.b: U.S. farmers, ranchers, and agricultural producers

Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fun.d: Insures private-sector defined benefit pension plans and pays benefits when underfunded plans are terminated. Created by ERISA, it is funded by insurance premiums, investment income, and assets of failed plans.
Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fun.b: Workers and retirees in private-sector defined benefit pension plans

Operations and Support, Customs and Bord.d: Funds CBP’s primary mission of securing U.S. borders at and between ports of entry, including personnel, technology, and infrastructure for border security. Supports trade enforcement, immigration inspection, and agricultural protection.
Operations and Support, Customs and Bord.b: General public, lawful travelers, and U.S. trade community

Shipbuilding and Conversion, Navy.d: Finances the construction, acquisition, and conversion of naval vessels including aircraft carriers, submarines, destroyers, and amphibious ships. Provides long-lead-time materials and detail design for new ship classes.
Shipbuilding and Conversion, Navy.b: U.S. Navy, defense shipbuilding industrial base

State Children’s Health Insurance Progra.d: Provides federal matching funds to states for health insurance coverage of uninsured children in families with incomes too high for Medicaid but too low to afford private coverage. Created by the Balanced Budget Act of 1997.
State Children’s Health Insurance Progra.b: Low-income children and pregnant women not eligible for Medicaid

Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.d: Funds R&D for space-based military capabilities including satellite communications, missile warning, space domain awareness, and GPS modernization. Established when the Space Force was created as the sixth military branch.
Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.b: U.S. Space Force, Department of Defense, space technology industrial base

Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to t.d: Funds concurrent receipt of military retired pay and VA disability compensation for qualifying military retirees with service-connected disabilities. Authorized by the FY2004 NDAA to end the longstanding offset between the two benefits.
Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to t.b: Military retirees with service-connected disabilities rated 50% or higher

Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund, Depart.d: Created by the PACT Act to fund health care and benefits for veterans exposed to burn pits, Agent Orange, and other toxic substances during military service. Provides mandatory funding outside normal VA discretionary caps.
Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund, Depart.b: Veterans exposed to toxic substances during military service

Education for the Disadvantaged (Title I.d: Provides formula grants to school districts with high concentrations of low-income students to help ensure all children meet challenging academic standards. Authorized under Title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act.
Education for the Disadvantaged (Title I.b: Low-income K-12 students, high-poverty school districts

Aircraft Procurement, Air Force.d: Funds procurement of fighter, bomber, tanker, transport, trainer, and special mission aircraft plus modifications and spares for the Air Force. Covers production of platforms such as the F-35A, KC-46, and B-21.
Aircraft Procurement, Air Force.b: U.S. Air Force, aerospace defense industrial base

Temporary Assistance for Needy Families.d: Provides block grants to states for cash assistance, work programs, and support services for low-income families with children. Replaced the AFDC entitlement program under the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Act of 1996.

Project-Based Rental Assistance, Departm.d: Provides rental subsidy payments to private property owners under long-term contracts to maintain affordable housing units for low-income tenants. Contracts are tied to specific housing projects rather than following individual tenants.
Project-Based Rental Assistance, Departm.b: Low-income renters in privately owned subsidized housing

Military Personnel, Marine Corps.d: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for active-duty Marine Corps personnel including basic pay, housing allowances, subsistence, and special/incentive pays. Covers the end strength of approximately 172,000–180,000 active Marines.
Military Personnel, Marine Corps.b: Active-duty U.S. Marines and their dependents

Special Education (IDEA Grants to States.d: Provides formula grants to states and territories to assist in providing free appropriate public education to children with disabilities ages 3–21. Authorized under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act.
Special Education (IDEA Grants to States.b: Children and youth with disabilities, school districts serving them

Aircraft Procurement, Navy.d: Funds the acquisition of fixed-wing and rotary-wing aircraft for the Navy and Marine Corps, including the F-35B/C, MV-22, CH-53K, and E-2D. Includes spare engines, modifications, and support equipment.
Aircraft Procurement, Navy.b: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps aviation, defense aerospace industry

Children and Families Services Programs.d: Funds Head Start/Early Head Start preschool programs for low-income children and the Child Care and Development Block Grant. Provides comprehensive child development services including education, health, nutrition, and parent engagement.
Children and Families Services Programs.b: Low-income children ages 0-5, working families needing child care

Government Payment for Annuitants, Emplo.d: Pays the federal government’s share of health insurance premiums for retired federal civilian employees and their survivors enrolled in the Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) program. Functions as the employer contribution for retiree health coverage.
Government Payment for Annuitants, Emplo.b: Retired federal civilian employees and their survivors

Formula and Bus Grants, Federal Transit.d: Provides formula-based capital and operating assistance to transit agencies for bus and rail systems, including urbanized area formula grants and bus/bus facility programs. The largest FTA program supporting public transportation nationwide.
Formula and Bus Grants, Federal Transit.b: Public transit agencies, transit riders in urban and rural areas

Other Procurement, Navy.d: Funds procurement of equipment and material not covered by dedicated shipbuilding or aircraft accounts, including communications, electronics, ordnance, tactical vehicles, and support equipment. Covers a wide range of Navy combat and logistics systems.
Other Procurement, Navy.b: U.S. Navy fleet operations and readiness

Limitation on Administrative Expenses, S.d: Funds SSA’s operating costs including processing Social Security and SSI claims, maintaining earnings records, issuing Social Security numbers, and administering disability determinations. Became an independent agency account in 1994.
Limitation on Administrative Expenses, S.b: Social Security and SSI beneficiaries, general public using SSA services

Readjustment Benefits, Veterans Benefits.d: Provides education and training benefits to veterans and eligible dependents, primarily through the Post-9/11 GI Bill (Chapter 33) and vocational rehabilitation. Covers tuition, housing allowances, and book stipends.
Readjustment Benefits, Veterans Benefits.b: Post-9/11 veterans, service members, and eligible dependents pursuing education

Federal Family Education Loan Program Ac.d: Manages the legacy portfolio of federally guaranteed student loans originated by private lenders before the program’s termination in 2010. Covers subsidy costs, default claims, and loan consolidation for the remaining portfolio.
Federal Family Education Loan Program Ac.b: Student loan borrowers with legacy FFEL loans

Federal Buildings Fund, General Services.d: A revolving fund that finances the construction, acquisition, renovation, and management of federally owned and leased buildings. Revenue comes from rent collected from tenant federal agencies.

Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers, Civi.d: Finances the Army Corps of Engineers’ reimbursable civil works activities including design, construction, and repair work performed for other federal agencies and non-federal entities. Replenished through payments from customers.
Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers, Civi.b: Federal agencies and non-federal sponsors of civil works projects

Department of Defense Medicare-Eligible.d: Finances TRICARE health benefits for Medicare-eligible military retirees and their dependents through an accrual-based fund established by the FY2001 NDAA. Ensures long-term funding of retiree health obligations.
Department of Defense Medicare-Eligible.b: Medicare-eligible military retirees, dependents, and survivors

Diplomatic and Consular Programs, Depart.d: Funds the Department of State’s core diplomatic operations including staffing and operating approximately 270 embassies, consulates, and diplomatic missions worldwide. Covers personnel, diplomatic security, information technology, and public diplomacy.
Diplomatic and Consular Programs, Depart.b: U.S. foreign policy establishment, American citizens abroad

Global Health Programs, Department of St.d: Funds the President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief and other global health initiatives combating HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, malaria, and pandemic preparedness. PEPFAR is credited with saving over 25 million lives in sub-Saharan Africa and other regions.
Global Health Programs, Department of St.b: Populations in developing countries affected by HIV/AIDS and infectious diseases

Operation and Maintenance, Marine Corps.d: Funds the Marine Corps’ day-to-day operating costs including training, equipment maintenance, base operations, logistics, and civilian personnel. Ensures readiness for expeditionary and amphibious combat operations.
Operation and Maintenance, Marine Corps.b: U.S. Marine Corps operational forces and installations

National Guard Personnel, Army.d: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Army National Guard members during drill weekends, annual training, and active-duty periods. Covers approximately 325,000–336,000 Guard members in 54 states and territories.
National Guard Personnel, Army.b: Army National Guard members and their dependents

Medical Support and Compliance, Veterans.d: Funds administrative, compliance, quality management, and support functions for VA health care facilities including finance, human resources, security, and regulatory compliance. Supports the operational infrastructure of the VA health system.
Medical Support and Compliance, Veterans.b: Veterans receiving VA health care through VHA facilities

Operating Expenses, United States Coast.d: Funds Coast Guard operations including search and rescue, maritime law enforcement, drug interdiction, port security, environmental protection, and aids to navigation. Supports the operation of cutters, aircraft, and shore units.
Operating Expenses, United States Coast.b: Maritime community, coastal populations, general public

Operations and Support, Transportation S.d: Funds TSA’s aviation security screening operations at approximately 440 federally regulated airports, including passenger and baggage screening. Created after 9/11 within the Aviation and Transportation Security Act.
Operations and Support, Transportation S.b: Air travelers, aviation industry, general public

Community Development Fund (CDBG), Depar.d: Provides flexible formula grants to states and local communities for housing rehabilitation, infrastructure improvements, public services, and economic development in low- and moderate-income areas. Authorized under Title I of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974.
Community Development Fund (CDBG), Depar.b: Low- and moderate-income communities, local governments

Commodity Credit Corporation Fund, USDA.d: Finances USDA farm price and income support programs, commodity purchases, export subsidies, and conservation payments to agricultural producers. Functions as a revolving fund with borrowing authority from the U.S. Treasury.

General Operating Expenses, Veterans Ben.d: Funds VBA’s administrative costs for processing veterans’ disability compensation, pension, education, home loan, insurance, and burial benefit claims. Supports approximately 25,000 VBA employees across regional offices.
General Operating Expenses, Veterans Ben.b: Veterans and dependents applying for VA benefits

Foster Care and Permanency Payments (Tit.d: Provides federal matching payments to states for foster care maintenance, adoption assistance, and kinship guardianship assistance for eligible children removed from their homes. Authorized under Title IV-E of the Social Security Act.
Foster Care and Permanency Payments (Tit.b: Children in foster care, adoptive families, kinship caregivers

Other Procurement, Army.d: Funds Army procurement of tactical and commercial vehicles, communications/electronics equipment, night vision devices, chemical defense equipment, and other support items. Covers a broad range of equipment not included in dedicated weapon system accounts.
Other Procurement, Army.b: U.S. Army combat and support units

Operation and Maintenance, Army National.d: Funds the day-to-day operating costs of Army National Guard units including training, equipment maintenance, facility operations, and civilian technician pay. Supports readiness of Guard units for both federal and state missions.
Operation and Maintenance, Army National.b: Army National Guard units in 54 states and territories

Operations and Support, Immigration and.d: Funds ICE’s immigration enforcement, detention operations, removal proceedings, and Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) programs. Covers detention bed space, transportation, and investigations of transnational crime.

Exchange Stabilization Fund, Department.d: Provides the Treasury Secretary with resources to intervene in foreign exchange markets to stabilize the value of the dollar. Established by the Gold Reserve Act of 1934, also used for emergency financial stabilization.

Science, Energy Programs, Department of.d: Funds basic research in physics, chemistry, biology, environmental sciences, and advanced computing through DOE’s national laboratory system. Supports 17 national laboratories and is the largest federal sponsor of basic research in the physical sciences.
Science, Energy Programs, Department of.b: Scientific research community, national laboratories, universities

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison Sy.d: Funds operation of the federal prison system including staff salaries, inmate care, food, medical services, and facility maintenance at over 120 federal correctional institutions. Manages approximately 155,000 federal inmates.
Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison Sy.b: Federal inmates, correctional staff, public safety

Missile Procurement, Army.d: Funds procurement of Army missile systems including Patriot, THAAD, Javelin, Stinger, GMLRS, and associated ground support equipment. Covers production, modification, and initial spares for air and missile defense and tactical missile systems.
Missile Procurement, Army.b: U.S. Army air defense and tactical missile units

Procurement, Marine Corps.d: Funds procurement of Marine Corps ground combat and support equipment including amphibious combat vehicles, ground vehicles, communications systems, and individual combat equipment. Covers items not procured through Navy aviation or shipbuilding accounts.
Procurement, Marine Corps.b: U.S. Marine Corps ground combat forces

Defense Nuclear Nonproliferation, NNSA.d: Funds programs to detect, prevent, and reverse the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction, including securing vulnerable nuclear materials worldwide. Supports nuclear forensics, arms control verification, and international nonproliferation cooperation.
Defense Nuclear Nonproliferation, NNSA.b: Global nuclear security, international nonproliferation regime

Special Supplemental Nutrition Program f.d: Provides supplemental nutritious foods, nutrition education, and health care referrals to low-income pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children up to age five. Serves approximately 6–7 million participants monthly.
Special Supplemental Nutrition Program f.b: Low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children under 5

Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of.d: Funds the operation of Bureau of Indian Affairs programs including tribal government support, law enforcement, social services, natural resources management, and education on Indian reservations. Serves 574 federally recognized tribes.
Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of.b: Federally recognized American Indian and Alaska Native tribes and members

Military Construction, Army.d: Funds planning, design, and construction of Army facilities including barracks, training ranges, maintenance shops, headquarters buildings, and installation infrastructure. Covers both stateside and overseas construction projects.

Military Construction, Navy and Marine C.d: Funds construction of Navy and Marine Corps shore facilities including piers, hangars, training facilities, and operational support buildings. Supports the Navy’s infrastructure requirements at bases worldwide.
Military Construction, Navy and Marine C.b: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps installations and personnel

Military Construction, Air Force.d: Funds planning, design, and construction of Air Force facilities including runways, aircraft shelters, munitions storage, and mission support buildings. Supports the infrastructure needs of Air Force bases worldwide.
Military Construction, Air Force.b: U.S. Air Force installations and personnel

Salaries and Expenses, Drug Enforcement.d: Funds DEA’s enforcement of federal controlled substances laws, including drug trafficking investigations, diversion control, intelligence, and international operations. Supports approximately 10,000 employees including special agents in domestic and foreign offices.

Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Alcohol.d: Funds ATF’s law enforcement operations including firearms trafficking investigations, explosives regulation, arson investigations, and alcohol/tobacco tax enforcement. Administers federal firearms licensing and the National Tracing Center.

Salaries and Expenses, United States Mar.d: Funds the oldest federal law enforcement agency’s operations including fugitive apprehension, judicial security, witness protection, prisoner transport, and asset forfeiture. Protects approximately 2,700 federal judges and judicial facilities.
Salaries and Expenses, United States Mar.b: Federal judiciary, justice system, general public

Operations and Support, United States Se.d: Funds the Secret Service’s dual mission of protecting national leaders and investigating financial crimes including counterfeiting, cyber fraud, and identity theft. Protects the President, Vice President, and other designated officials.
Operations and Support, United States Se.b: Protected officials and families, financial system integrity

Federal Emergency Management Agency Gran.d: Funds FEMA’s non-disaster grant programs including Homeland Security Grant Program, State Homeland Security Program, Urban Area Security Initiative, and firefighter assistance grants. Strengthens state and local preparedness capabilities.
Federal Emergency Management Agency Gran.b: State and local governments, first responders, emergency management agencies

Hazardous Substance Superfund, Environme.d: Funds EPA’s cleanup of the nation’s most contaminated hazardous waste sites on the National Priorities List. Authorized under CERCLA (Superfund law) to address legacy pollution threats to human health and the environment.
Hazardous Substance Superfund, Environme.b: Communities near contaminated sites, public health and environment

State and Tribal Assistance Grants, Envi.d: Provides grants to states and tribes for environmental programs including clean water and drinking water state revolving funds, categorical grants, and brownfields cleanup. The largest EPA grant program supporting state environmental infrastructure.
State and Tribal Assistance Grants, Envi.b: State and tribal environmental agencies, communities with water infrastructure needs

National Cancer Institute, NIH.d: The largest NIH institute, funding cancer research including basic science, clinical trials, cancer prevention, and treatment development. Supports intramural research and extramural grants to universities and research centers nationwide.
National Cancer Institute, NIH.b: Cancer patients, biomedical research community, public health

National Institute of Allergy and Infect.d: Funds research on infectious, immunologic, and allergic diseases including HIV/AIDS, emerging infectious diseases, biodefense, and vaccine development. Played a central role in COVID-19 vaccine and therapeutic development.
National Institute of Allergy and Infect.b: Patients with infectious and immune diseases, global health

National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institut.d: Funds research on cardiovascular, lung, and blood diseases and disorders, including clinical trials and population studies. Supports landmark studies such as the Framingham Heart Study.
National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institut.b: Patients with heart, lung, and blood disorders; biomedical researchers

National Institute of General Medical Sc.d: Funds basic biomedical research that is not targeted at specific diseases, including cell biology, genetics, pharmacology, and computational biology. Supports research training and institutional development grants.
National Institute of General Medical Sc.b: Basic science research community, future biomedical workforce

National Forest System, Forest Service,.d: Funds management and protection of 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands, including wildfire prevention, recreation, timber, watershed, and wildlife habitat management. Supports approximately 30,000 Forest Service employees.
National Forest System, Forest Service,.b: Forest visitors, rural communities, timber and recreation industries

Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service.d: Funds wildfire suppression, hazardous fuels reduction, and fire preparedness activities on national forests and adjacent lands. One of the fastest-growing federal expenditures due to increasing wildfire severity.
Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service.b: Communities in wildland-urban interface, forest ecosystems

Management of Lands and Resources, Burea.d: Funds BLM’s management of approximately 245 million acres of public lands, including rangeland management, energy/mineral leasing, recreation, and conservation. Manages more surface land than any other federal agency.
Management of Lands and Resources, Burea.b: Ranchers, energy producers, recreationists, conservation interests

Resource Management, United States Fish.d: Funds FWS operations including endangered species conservation, migratory bird management, national wildlife refuge system operations, and law enforcement. Manages over 560 national wildlife refuges.
Resource Management, United States Fish.b: Endangered and threatened species, wildlife refuge visitors, conservation community

Operation of the National Park System, N.d: Funds the operation, maintenance, and management of over 420 units in the National Park System, including visitor services, resource protection, law enforcement, and facility maintenance. Hosts over 300 million visits annually.
Operation of the National Park System, N.b: Park visitors, cultural and natural resource preservation

Operations, Research, and Facilities, Na.d: Funds NOAA’s core operations including the National Weather Service, ocean and fisheries research, satellite operations, and coastal zone management. Provides weather forecasts, climate data, and marine resource management.
Operations, Research, and Facilities, Na.b: General public, mariners, aviation, agriculture, fisheries industry

Periodic Censuses and Programs, Census B.d: Funds the decennial census, American Community Survey, economic census, and other periodic surveys conducted by the Census Bureau. Constitutionally mandated for congressional apportionment and widely used for federal fund distribution.
Periodic Censuses and Programs, Census B.b: Federal, state, and local governments; researchers; businesses

Salaries and Expenses, United States Pat.d: Funds the examination and issuance of patents and trademarks, operating almost entirely on user fees from applicants. Processes approximately 600,000 patent applications and 900,000 trademark applications annually.

Water and Related Resources, Bureau of R.d: Funds operation, maintenance, and construction of water infrastructure projects in the 17 western states, including dams, reservoirs, canals, and hydropower facilities. Manages water delivery to over 31 million people and 10 million acres of farmland.
Water and Related Resources, Bureau of R.b: Western farmers, municipalities, hydropower consumers, recreation users

Bonneville Power Administration Fund.d: A self-financing federal power marketing administration that markets wholesale electricity from 31 federal dams and one nuclear plant in the Pacific Northwest. Operates approximately 15,000 miles of high-voltage transmission lines.
Bonneville Power Administration Fund.b: Pacific Northwest electric utilities, ratepayers, and industries

Tennessee Valley Authority Fund.d: A federally owned corporation providing electricity generation, flood control, navigation, and economic development across the Tennessee Valley region covering parts of seven states. One of the nation’s largest public power providers.
Tennessee Valley Authority Fund.b: Tennessee Valley residents and businesses across seven states

Postal Service Fund.d: Accounts for the revenues and expenditures of the United States Postal Service, an independent federal establishment that operates primarily from postal rate revenues. Delivers to approximately 167 million addresses.

Railroad Retirement Board, Benefit Payme.d: Administers retirement, disability, unemployment, and sickness benefits for railroad workers and their families under a separate system from Social Security. Covers approximately 500,000 beneficiaries.
Railroad Retirement Board, Benefit Payme.b: Retired and disabled railroad workers and their families

Foreign Military Financing Program, Depa.d: Provides grants and loans to foreign governments to purchase U.S. defense equipment, services, and training to support U.S. national security objectives. Israel and Egypt are historically the largest recipients.
Foreign Military Financing Program, Depa.b: Allied and partner foreign governments, U.S. defense industry

Economic Support Fund, Department of Sta.d: Provides economic assistance to countries of strategic importance to the United States, funding governance, economic growth, education, and stabilization programs. Supports U.S. foreign policy objectives in key regions.
Economic Support Fund, Department of Sta.b: Developing countries and fragile states of strategic importance to the U.S.

International Disaster Assistance, USAID.d: Funds U.S. government humanitarian assistance for international disasters including emergency food, shelter, medical care, and disaster risk reduction. Responds to natural disasters, complex emergencies, and refugee crises worldwide.
International Disaster Assistance, USAID.b: Populations affected by international disasters and humanitarian crises

Peace Corps.d: Sends American volunteers to developing countries for two-year service assignments in education, health, agriculture, environment, and community development. Has sent over 240,000 volunteers to more than 140 countries since inception.
Peace Corps.b: Communities in developing countries, American volunteers

Operating Expenses, United States Agency.d: Funds USAID’s administrative and operating costs for managing U.S. international development and humanitarian programs in over 100 countries. Covers staff salaries, overseas mission operations, and program management.
Operating Expenses, United States Agency.b: Developing countries, U.S. foreign assistance programs

Export-Import Bank of the United States.d: Provides export credit financing including direct loans, guarantees, and insurance to support U.S. exports when private financing is unavailable. Operates on a self-sustaining basis using fees and interest income.
Export-Import Bank of the United States.b: U.S. exporters, particularly small businesses, and foreign buyers of U.S. goods

Rural Housing Insurance Fund, USDA.d: Provides direct and guaranteed loans for single-family and multi-family housing in rural areas with populations under 35,000. Supports homeownership and rental housing through the Section 502 and Section 515 programs.
Rural Housing Insurance Fund, USDA.b: Low- and moderate-income rural homebuyers and renters

Rural Utilities Service, USDA.d: Provides loans, grants, and loan guarantees for rural electric, telecommunications, water, and waste disposal infrastructure. Originally created as the Rural Electrification Administration to bring electricity to rural America.
Rural Utilities Service, USDA.b: Rural electric cooperatives, telephone companies, water systems

Federal-Aid Highways, Federal Highway Ad.d: The primary federal program funding construction, reconstruction, and maintenance of the National Highway System and other federal-aid roads through formula grants to states. Funded largely through the Highway Trust Fund from fuel taxes.
Federal-Aid Highways, Federal Highway Ad.b: State transportation departments, motorists, commercial freight industry

Grants-in-Aid for Airports (Airport Impr.d: Provides formula and discretionary grants to public-use airports for planning, construction, and rehabilitation of airport infrastructure including runways, taxiways, and safety improvements. Funded by the Airport and Airway Trust Fund.
Grants-in-Aid for Airports (Airport Impr.b: Public-use airports, air travelers, general aviation community

National Railroad Passenger Corporation.d: Provides federal grants to Amtrak for operating and capital expenses of the national intercity passenger rail system. Supports approximately 500 stations across 46 states with over 30 million annual riders.
National Railroad Passenger Corporation.b: Amtrak riders, communities served by intercity passenger rail

Operations and Administration, Maritime.d: Funds MARAD’s programs supporting the U.S. merchant marine fleet, including the Maritime Security Program, Ready Reserve Force, mariner training at the U.S. Merchant Marine Academy, and ship disposal. Ensures sealift capacity for national defense.
Operations and Administration, Maritime.b: U.S. merchant marine industry, national defense sealift

Scientific and Technical Research and Se.d: Funds NIST’s laboratory research programs in advanced manufacturing, cybersecurity, physical sciences, and engineering to develop measurement standards, technology, and practices. Supports U.S. innovation and industrial competitiveness.
Scientific and Technical Research and Se.b: U.S. industry, standards community, technology sector

Science, NASA.d: Funds NASA’s space and Earth science programs including planetary exploration, astrophysics, heliophysics, and Earth observation missions. Supports missions such as the James Webb Space Telescope, Mars rovers, and climate monitoring satellites.
Science, NASA.b: Scientific research community, general public, climate and weather monitoring

Exploration, NASA.d: Funds NASA’s human spaceflight exploration programs including the Artemis lunar program, Space Launch System, Orion spacecraft, and human landing systems. Aims to return humans to the Moon and eventually send crews to Mars.
Exploration, NASA.b: Aerospace industry, scientific community, national space exploration goals

Space Operations, NASA.d: Funds NASA’s ongoing human spaceflight operations including the International Space Station, commercial crew transportation, space communications networks, and launch services. Manages ISS partnerships with international space agencies.
Space Operations, NASA.b: Astronauts, ISS international partners, commercial space industry

Salaries and Expenses, Smithsonian Insti.d: Funds the operation of the world’s largest museum, education, and research complex, including 21 museums, the National Zoo, and nine research centers. Most Smithsonian museums are free and located on the National Mall.
Salaries and Expenses, Smithsonian Insti.b: Museum visitors, researchers, educators, general public

Salaries and Expenses, Library of Congre.d: Funds operations of the world’s largest library, which holds over 170 million items and serves as the research library for Congress. Administers the U.S. Copyright Office and provides services to the blind and print disabled.
Salaries and Expenses, Library of Congre.b: Members of Congress, researchers, copyright holders, general public

Government Publishing Office (Revolving.d: Funds the production and distribution of official federal government publications including the Congressional Record, Federal Register, and U.S. Code. Operates as a revolving fund reimbursed by customer agencies.
Government Publishing Office (Revolving.b: Congress, federal agencies, public accessing government documents

Salaries and Expenses, Congressional Bud.d: Funds CBO’s nonpartisan analysis of budgetary and economic issues for Congress, including cost estimates of legislation, budget projections, and economic forecasts. Created by the Congressional Budget Act of 1974.
Salaries and Expenses, Congressional Bud.b: Members of Congress, congressional committees, public policy process

Family Housing Construction, Army.d: Funds construction and improvement of on-post family housing for Army service members and their families. Largely privatized since 1996 under the Military Housing Privatization Initiative.
Family Housing Construction, Army.b: Army service members and families in on-post housing

Family Housing Operation and Maintenance.d: Funds operation, maintenance, and utilities for Army family housing units not yet privatized. Covers housing management, furnishings, and leasing of family housing in areas without adequate private sector options.
Family Housing Operation and Maintenance.b: Army service members and families in government-managed housing

Family Housing Construction, Navy and Ma.d: Funds construction, improvement, and planning of family housing for Navy and Marine Corps personnel at installations in the U.S. and overseas. Largely transitioned to private-public ventures under housing privatization.
Family Housing Construction, Navy and Ma.b: Navy and Marine Corps service members and families

Family Housing Construction, Air Force.d: Funds construction and improvement of Air Force family housing at domestic and overseas installations. Most stateside housing has been privatized; appropriation primarily supports overseas locations.
Family Housing Construction, Air Force.b: Air Force service members and families

Defense Commissary Agency.d: Operates a worldwide chain of approximately 236 commissary stores on military installations providing groceries at cost plus a 5% surcharge to military families. Considered a key quality-of-life benefit for the military community.
Defense Commissary Agency.b: Active-duty military, retirees, reservists, and their families

Defense Logistics Agency Operations.d: Funds DLA’s combat logistics support operations including supply chain management for fuel, food, clothing, construction materials, medical supplies, and spare parts for all military services. Manages over 5 million items.
Defense Logistics Agency Operations.b: All branches of the U.S. military, federal agencies

General Fund, Defense Intelligence Agenc.d: Funds DIA’s military intelligence operations providing all-source intelligence analysis, human intelligence collection, and defense intelligence support to combatant commanders and policymakers. The primary producer of foreign military intelligence.
General Fund, Defense Intelligence Agenc.b: Combatant commanders, Defense Department, national security policymakers

National Security Agency/Central Securit.d: Funds NSA’s signals intelligence collection and information assurance mission, providing foreign intelligence and cybersecurity capabilities. The largest U.S. intelligence agency by budget and personnel.
National Security Agency/Central Securit.b: Intelligence community, military operations, cybersecurity defense

Defense Contract Audit Agency.d: Funds DCAA’s contract auditing services for the Department of Defense and other federal agencies, reviewing defense contractor pricing, costs, and financial systems. Performs approximately 5,000 audit reports annually.

Construction, Army Corps of Engineers, C.d: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers’ civil works construction program for flood and storm damage reduction, navigation, ecosystem restoration, and water supply projects. Includes major levee, dam, and waterway infrastructure.
Construction, Army Corps of Engineers, C.b: Communities in flood-prone areas, commercial navigation, water supply users

Buildings and Facilities, Federal Prison.d: Funds the construction, modernization, and repair of federal correctional institutions operated by the Bureau of Prisons. Supports capacity for the federal inmate population across all security levels.
Buildings and Facilities, Federal Prison.b: Federal prison system, correctional staff, public safety

Salaries and Expenses, Courts of Appeals.d: Funds the operating costs of the federal court system including judges’ staff, clerks of court, probation and pretrial services, court interpreters, and juror fees. Supports approximately 870 authorized federal judgeships.
Salaries and Expenses, Courts of Appeals.b: Federal judiciary, litigants, jurors, justice system

United States Tax Court.d: Funds operations of the independent judicial body where taxpayers can dispute IRS determinations of tax deficiencies before paying the contested amount. Handles approximately 30,000 cases annually.

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Trade Com.d: Funds the FTC’s consumer protection and antitrust enforcement activities, including investigating unfair business practices, merger review, and consumer education. Funded primarily through Hart-Scott-Rodino merger filing fees.

Salaries and Expenses, Securities and Ex.d: Funds SEC’s regulation of securities markets, including corporate disclosure oversight, enforcement against fraud, registration of broker-dealers, and investor protection. Funded through transaction fees and filing fees.

Commodity Futures Trading Commission.d: Funds regulation of U.S. commodity futures and options markets and the swaps market, including enforcement against market manipulation and fraud. Oversees designated contract markets and swap execution facilities.
Commodity Futures Trading Commission.b: Market participants, agricultural producers, financial market integrity

Nuclear Regulatory Commission.d: Funds NRC’s regulation of civilian nuclear power plants, research reactors, nuclear materials, and radioactive waste to protect public health and safety. Licenses and inspects approximately 93 operating commercial nuclear reactors.
Nuclear Regulatory Commission.b: Communities near nuclear facilities, nuclear industry, public safety

Board of Governors, Federal Reserve Syst.d: Funds the Federal Reserve Board’s operations including monetary policy, banking supervision, financial stability oversight, and payment system regulation. Self-funded through assessments on Federal Reserve Banks, not congressional appropriations.
Board of Governors, Federal Reserve Syst.b: U.S. economy, banking system, monetary policy

Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation.d: Insures deposits up to $250,000 per depositor at member banks and thrift institutions, and resolves failed insured depository institutions. Funded by insurance premiums from member institutions, not taxpayer funds.
Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation.b: Bank depositors, stability of the banking system

National Credit Union Administration.d: Charters, regulates, and insures federal credit unions and insures most state-chartered credit unions through the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund. Insures deposits up to $250,000 per member.
National Credit Union Administration.b: Credit union members, credit union system

Farm Credit System Insurance Corporation.d: Insures the timely payment of principal and interest on obligations issued by Farm Credit System banks, maintaining confidence in the agricultural credit system. Funded through insurance premiums from FCS banks.
Farm Credit System Insurance Corporation.b: Investors in Farm Credit System securities, agricultural borrowers

Government National Mortgage Association.d: Guarantees the timely payment of principal and interest on mortgage-backed securities backed by federally insured or guaranteed loans (FHA, VA, USDA). Provides liquidity to the government-insured mortgage market without taxpayer appropriations.
Government National Mortgage Association.b: Mortgage borrowers with government-insured loans, MBS investors

Federal Housing Finance Agency (Fannie M.d: Regulates and supervises Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and the Federal Home Loan Banks to ensure they operate safely and support the housing finance market. Placed Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac into conservatorship in September 2008.
Federal Housing Finance Agency (Fannie M.b: Mortgage market, homeowners, housing finance stability

Federal Home Loan Banks.d: A system of 11 government-sponsored enterprise banks that provide low-cost funding (advances) to member financial institutions to support housing finance, community investment, and liquidity. Privately capitalized by member institutions.
Federal Home Loan Banks.b: Member banks, thrifts, credit unions, and insurance companies; homebuyers

Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (Bu.d: Funds the CFPB’s regulation and enforcement of federal consumer financial laws covering mortgages, credit cards, student loans, and other consumer financial products. Funded through Federal Reserve System transfers, not congressional appropriations.
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (Bu.b: Consumers of financial products and services

Office of the Comptroller of the Currenc.d: Charters, regulates, and supervises national banks and federal savings associations to ensure they operate safely and provide fair access to financial services. Self-funded through assessments on supervised institutions.
Office of the Comptroller of the Currenc.b: Customers of national banks, banking system stability

Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (Fi.d: Safeguards the financial system from illicit use by collecting and analyzing financial intelligence data, primarily Bank Secrecy Act reports, and administering anti-money laundering regulations. Designated as the U.S. financial intelligence unit.
Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (Fi.b: Law enforcement, financial institutions, financial system integrity

Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC),.d: Administers and enforces U.S. economic sanctions programs against targeted foreign countries, terrorists, narcotics traffickers, and proliferators of weapons of mass destruction. Maintains the Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list.
Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC),.b: U.S. national security, international sanctions regime

Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau.d: Collects federal excise taxes on alcohol, tobacco, firearms, and ammunition, and regulates the alcohol and tobacco industries to protect consumers. Split from ATF by the Homeland Security Act of 2002.
Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau.b: Federal revenue, consumers of alcohol and tobacco products

United States Mint, Public Enterprise Fu.d: Manufactures and distributes circulating coinage, bullion, and numismatic products for the nation. Operates as a public enterprise fund with revenues from seigniorage and sales offsetting production costs.

Bureau of Engraving and Printing, Operat.d: Designs, manufactures, and supplies Federal Reserve notes (paper currency) and other security documents for the federal government. Operates as a revolving fund reimbursed by the Federal Reserve for currency production.
Bureau of Engraving and Printing, Operat.b: Federal Reserve System, general public, commerce

Operations Support, Internal Revenue Ser.d: Funds IRS core operational functions including rent, communications, IT infrastructure, security, printing, and postage needed to administer the tax system. Supports the foundational infrastructure for all IRS activities.

Enforcement, Internal Revenue Service.d: Funds IRS examination of tax returns, collection of delinquent taxes, criminal investigation of tax fraud, and legal counsel activities. Historically returns significantly more revenue than its cost through compliance enforcement.
Enforcement, Internal Revenue Service.b: Federal revenue, tax compliance, honest taxpayers

Business Systems Modernization, Internal.d: Funds IRS technology modernization efforts to replace legacy systems, improve e-filing capabilities, and enhance taxpayer services through digital transformation. Received a major boost from the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022.

Customs and Border Protection, Duties, T.d: Accounts for the collection of customs duties, excise taxes, and user fees on imported goods entering the United States. One of the oldest sources of federal revenue, collected at ports of entry.
Customs and Border Protection, Duties, T.b: Federal revenue, domestic industries under trade law protection

Immigration Examinations Fee Account, US.d: Funds U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services’ adjudication of immigration benefit applications including naturalization, green cards, work permits, and asylum cases. Operates almost entirely on application fees rather than appropriations.
Immigration Examinations Fee Account, US.b: Immigration applicants, employers of foreign workers

Indian Health Service, Services Account.d: Funds direct health care services to approximately 2.6 million American Indians and Alaska Natives through a network of hospitals, clinics, and health programs. Fulfills the federal trust responsibility for tribal health care.
Indian Health Service, Services Account.b: American Indians and Alaska Natives in federally recognized tribes

Immunization and Respiratory Diseases, C.d: Funds CDC’s immunization programs including the Vaccines for Children program, pandemic influenza preparedness, and surveillance of vaccine-preventable diseases. Supports vaccine distribution infrastructure and outbreak response.
Immunization and Respiratory Diseases, C.b: Children, adults receiving vaccinations, public health

HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, STI, and TB P.d: Funds CDC’s domestic prevention programs for HIV/AIDS, sexually transmitted infections, viral hepatitis, and tuberculosis including surveillance, testing, and prevention interventions. Supports Ryan White-related surveillance and partner notification.
HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, STI, and TB P.b: Populations at risk for HIV, STIs, hepatitis, and TB

Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Pr.d: Funds CDC programs addressing heart disease, cancer, diabetes, tobacco use, obesity, and other chronic conditions through surveillance, research, and community prevention grants. Chronic diseases account for the majority of U.S. deaths and health care costs.
Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Pr.b: General public, populations at risk for chronic diseases

Salaries and Expenses, Food and Drug Adm.d: Funds FDA’s regulation of food safety, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, biologics, cosmetics, tobacco, and veterinary products to protect public health. Funded through a combination of appropriations and industry user fees.
Salaries and Expenses, Food and Drug Adm.b: Consumers, patients, food and pharmaceutical industries

Substance Abuse and Mental Health Servic.d: Funds programs to reduce the impact of substance abuse and mental illness through grants, data collection, and policy coordination. Administers the Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Block Grant and Community Mental Health Services Block Grant.
Substance Abuse and Mental Health Servic.b: Individuals with substance use disorders and mental illness, state behavioral health agencies

Health Resources and Services Administra.d: Funds programs improving health care access for underserved populations, including community health centers, the National Health Service Corps, Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program, and maternal and child health programs. Supports approximately 1,400 community health centers.
Health Resources and Services Administra.b: Medically underserved populations, uninsured and underinsured individuals

Refugee and Entrant Assistance, Administ.d: Funds resettlement assistance for refugees, asylees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, and unaccompanied alien children, including cash and medical assistance, social services, and the Unaccompanied Children Program. Authorized by the Refugee Act of 1980.

Aging and Disability Services Programs,.d: Funds Older Americans Act programs including home-delivered and congregate meals (Meals on Wheels), caregiver support, elder abuse prevention, and independent living services for older adults and people with disabilities. Serves approximately 11 million older adults.
Aging and Disability Services Programs,.b: Older adults age 60+, people with disabilities, family caregivers

Program Management, Centers for Medicare.d: Funds CMS’s administrative costs for overseeing Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP, and the Health Insurance Marketplaces, including claims processing, program integrity, and quality improvement. Manages programs covering over 150 million Americans.
Program Management, Centers for Medicare.b: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries, health care providers

Agency for Healthcare Research and Quali.d: Funds research to improve the quality, safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of health care for all Americans. Produces evidence-based clinical practice guidelines, patient safety research, and health services research.
Agency for Healthcare Research and Quali.b: Health care providers, patients, health policy researchers

Biomedical Advanced Research and Develop.d: Funds development and procurement of medical countermeasures against chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and emerging infectious disease threats. Played a central role in Operation Warp Speed for COVID-19 vaccine development.
Biomedical Advanced Research and Develop.b: National public health preparedness, general population during health emergencies

Military Construction, Defense-Wide.d: Funds construction projects for defense agencies and activities not assigned to a specific military department, including Special Operations Command, defense health, and intelligence facilities.
Military Construction, Defense-Wide.b: Defense agencies, special operations forces, defense-wide installations

Military Construction, Space Force.d: Funds planning, design, and construction of Space Force facilities including satellite control centers, space launch infrastructure, and mission support buildings at space-focused installations.
Military Construction, Space Force.b: U.S. Space Force installations and personnel

Military Construction, Army National Gua.d: Funds construction of readiness centers (armories), training facilities, and maintenance buildings for Army National Guard units across 54 states and territories.
Military Construction, Army National Gua.b: Army National Guard units and personnel

Military Construction, Army Reserve.d: Funds construction of Army Reserve centers, training facilities, and maintenance buildings to support the readiness of Reserve component forces.
Military Construction, Army Reserve.b: U.S. Army Reserve units and personnel

Disaster Relief Fund, Federal Emergency.d: The primary funding source for federal disaster response and recovery under the Stafford Act, covering individual assistance, public assistance, hazard mitigation, and mission assignments to other federal agencies. Activated upon presidential disaster declarations.
Disaster Relief Fund, Federal Emergency.b: Disaster-affected individuals, communities, state and local governments

National Flood Insurance Fund, FEMA.d: Administers the National Flood Insurance Program providing federally backed flood insurance to homeowners, renters, and businesses in participating communities. Covers approximately 5 million policies with significant accumulated debt to Treasury.
National Flood Insurance Fund, FEMA.b: Property owners in flood-prone areas, floodplain communities

Environmental Programs and Management, E.d: Funds EPA’s core regulatory and compliance programs including air quality, water quality, pesticides, toxic substances, and environmental justice activities. The agency’s primary operating account for environmental protection and standard-setting.
Environmental Programs and Management, E.b: General public, environmental and public health

Leaking Underground Storage Tank Trust F.d: Funds cleanup of petroleum releases from underground storage tanks at gas stations and other facilities, and supports state tank programs. Funded by a 0.1-cent-per-gallon tax on motor fuels.
Leaking Underground Storage Tank Trust F.b: Communities affected by underground storage tank contamination

National Institute of Mental Health, NIH.d: Funds research on mental disorders including depression, schizophrenia, PTSD, and autism spectrum disorder to develop new interventions and improve understanding of brain function. The lead federal agency for research on mental illness.
National Institute of Mental Health, NIH.b: Individuals with mental illness, mental health research community

National Institute on Drug Abuse, NIH.d: Funds research on drug use and addiction, including neuroscience of addiction, development of prevention and treatment strategies, and epidemiology of substance use disorders. Conducts critical research on the opioid crisis.
National Institute on Drug Abuse, NIH.b: Individuals with substance use disorders, addiction research community

National Institute of Diabetes and Diges.d: Funds research on diabetes, endocrine and metabolic diseases, digestive diseases, nutrition, obesity, and kidney/urologic diseases. Supports major clinical trials and the national kidney and pancreas transplant registries.
National Institute of Diabetes and Diges.b: Patients with diabetes, kidney disease, and digestive disorders

National Institute on Aging, NIH.d: Funds research on the aging process and age-related diseases, particularly Alzheimer’s disease and related dementias. Received significant funding increases through the National Alzheimer’s Project Act.
National Institute on Aging, NIH.b: Older adults, Alzheimer’s patients and caregivers, aging research community

National Institute of Child Health and H.d: Funds research on fertility, pregnancy, childhood development, intellectual and developmental disabilities, and population dynamics. Conducts research on maternal health, SIDS, and learning disabilities.
National Institute of Child Health and H.b: Mothers, children, individuals with developmental disabilities

National Institute of Neurological Disor.d: Funds research on the causes, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of neurological disorders and stroke including Parkinson’s disease, epilepsy, traumatic brain injury, and ALS. Supports the BRAIN Initiative.
National Institute of Neurological Disor.b: Patients with neurological disorders, neuroscience research community

National Institute of Environmental Heal.d: Funds research on how environmental exposures affect human health, including toxicology, epidemiology of environmental diseases, and the National Toxicology Program. Also manages the Superfund Research Program.
National Institute of Environmental Heal.b: Communities exposed to environmental hazards, environmental health researchers

Construction, Bureau of Reclamation.d: Funds construction and rehabilitation of water resource projects in the 17 western states including dams, canals, and hydropower facilities. Supports major projects for irrigation, municipal water supply, and water recycling.

Procurement, Defense-Wide.d: Funds procurement of equipment and supplies for defense agencies not assigned to a specific military service, including Special Operations Command, missile defense, and defense-wide information systems.

RDT&E, Defense-Wide.d: Funds research, development, test, and evaluation for defense-wide programs including DARPA advanced research, missile defense development, chemical/biological defense, and information systems technology.
RDT&E, Defense-Wide.b: Defense innovation ecosystem, military technology advancement

Defense Health Program.d: Funds the Military Health System providing medical and dental care to active-duty service members, retirees, and dependents through military treatment facilities and the TRICARE purchased care network. Serves approximately 9.6 million beneficiaries.
