Defense Health Program.b: Active-duty military, retirees, and dependents using the Military Health System

Operation and Maintenance, Air Force Res.d: Funds day-to-day operating costs of the Air Force Reserve including training, base operations, equipment maintenance, and civilian technician pay. Supports the combat readiness of approximately 70,000 Reserve Airmen.
Operation and Maintenance, Air Force Res.b: Air Force Reserve units and personnel

Operation and Maintenance, Army Reserve.d: Funds day-to-day operating costs of the Army Reserve including training, equipment maintenance, base operations, and civilian support staff. Supports readiness of approximately 189,000 Army Reserve soldiers.
Operation and Maintenance, Army Reserve.b: U.S. Army Reserve units and personnel

National Guard Personnel, Air Force.d: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Air National Guard members during drill, annual training, and active-duty periods. Covers approximately 107,000 Air Guard members in 54 states and territories.
National Guard Personnel, Air Force.b: Air National Guard members and their dependents

Operation and Maintenance, Air National.d: Funds day-to-day operating costs of the Air National Guard including flying operations, equipment maintenance, base operations, and civilian technician pay. Supports fighter, airlift, tanker, and ISR missions.
Operation and Maintenance, Air National.b: Air National Guard units and flying operations

Reserve Personnel, Army.d: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Army Reserve members during drill weekends, annual training, and periods of active duty or mobilization.
Reserve Personnel, Army.b: U.S. Army Reserve members and their dependents

Reserve Personnel, Navy.d: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Navy Reserve members during drill, annual training, and active-duty periods. Covers approximately 59,000 Selected Reserve sailors.
Reserve Personnel, Navy.b: Navy Reserve members and their dependents

Reserve Personnel, Marine Corps.d: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Marine Corps Reserve members during drill, annual training, and active-duty periods. Covers approximately 36,000 Selected Marine Corps Reservists.
Reserve Personnel, Marine Corps.b: Marine Corps Reserve members and their dependents

Reserve Personnel, Air Force.d: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Air Force Reserve members during drill weekends, annual training, and mobilization periods. Supports approximately 70,000 Air Force Reserve members.
Reserve Personnel, Air Force.b: Air Force Reserve members and their dependents

Weapons Procurement, Navy.d: Funds procurement of Navy weapons systems including missiles (Tomahawk, SM-6, LRASM), torpedoes, guns, ammunition, and associated equipment for surface ships, submarines, and aircraft.
Weapons Procurement, Navy.b: U.S. Navy combat forces, defense industrial base

Missile Procurement, Air Force.d: Funds procurement of Air Force missile systems including air-to-air missiles (AIM-9X, AIM-120), air-to-ground munitions (JASSM), and space launch vehicles.
Missile Procurement, Air Force.b: U.S. Air Force combat and space launch operations

Procurement of Ammunition, Army.d: Funds procurement of all classes of Army ammunition including small arms, medium caliber, tank ammunition, mortar rounds, artillery shells, and demolitions. Maintains ammunition production base capacity.
Procurement of Ammunition, Army.b: U.S. Army combat units, ammunition industrial base

Procurement of Ammunition, Navy and Mari.d: Funds procurement of ammunition and ordnance for the Navy and Marine Corps including naval gun ammunition, bombs, rockets, and small arms ammunition.
Procurement of Ammunition, Navy and Mari.b: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps combat forces

Procurement of Ammunition, Air Force.d: Funds procurement of Air Force munitions including bombs (JDAM), missiles, cartridges, and other expendable ordnance items for combat and training.
Procurement of Ammunition, Air Force.b: U.S. Air Force combat and training operations

Defense Working Capital Funds.d: Revolving funds that finance business-like activities across DoD including depot maintenance, supply management, transportation, and information services on a customer-reimbursement basis. Operates with approximately $90 billion in annual revenue.
Defense Working Capital Funds.b: All DoD components purchasing logistics and support services

Drug Interdiction and Counter-Drug Activ.d: Funds DoD’s support to federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies for counter-drug operations, including detection and monitoring, intelligence sharing, and training. Provides military assets and expertise for drug interdiction.
Drug Interdiction and Counter-Drug Activ.b: Federal and state drug enforcement agencies, border security

Office of the Inspector General, Departm.d: Funds independent oversight of DoD programs and operations through audits, investigations, and evaluations to promote efficiency and prevent fraud, waste, and abuse. Largest inspector general office in the federal government.

Chemical Agents and Munitions Destructio.d: Funds the safe destruction of the U.S. chemical weapons stockpile in compliance with the Chemical Weapons Convention. Program nearing completion with destruction of remaining mustard and nerve agent stocks.
Chemical Agents and Munitions Destructio.b: Communities near chemical weapons storage sites, international treaty compliance

National Science Foundation, Education a.d: Funds STEM education programs from K-12 through postdoctoral levels, including research experiences for undergraduates, graduate research fellowships, and programs to broaden participation in science and engineering.
National Science Foundation, Education a.b: STEM students and educators at all levels, underrepresented groups in STEM

National Science Foundation, Major Resea.d: Funds the acquisition, construction, and commissioning of major research facilities and equipment including telescopes, research vessels, and large-scale computing infrastructure costing over $100 million.
National Science Foundation, Major Resea.b: Scientific research community, major research facility users

Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Indian.d: Funds the operation of approximately 183 elementary and secondary schools and dormitories serving American Indian and Alaska Native students on or near reservations. Also supports tribal colleges and universities.
Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Indian.b: American Indian and Alaska Native students in BIE-funded schools

Indian Housing Block Grant, HUD.d: Provides formula-based housing assistance grants to Indian tribes and tribally designated housing entities under the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act (NAHASDA). Replaced multiple earlier HUD Indian housing programs.
Indian Housing Block Grant, HUD.b: American Indian and Alaska Native families needing affordable housing

HOME Investment Partnerships Program, HU.d: Provides formula grants to states and localities for a wide range of affordable housing activities including construction, rehabilitation, rental assistance, and homebuyer assistance for low-income households. The largest federal block grant for affordable housing.

Housing Opportunities for Persons with A.d: Provides housing assistance and supportive services to low-income persons living with HIV/AIDS and their families. Funds tenant-based rental assistance, short-term supported housing, and housing information services.
Housing Opportunities for Persons with A.b: Low-income individuals and families living with HIV/AIDS

Homeless Assistance Grants, HUD.d: Funds programs to assist individuals and families experiencing homelessness through emergency shelter, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and rapid re-housing under the Continuum of Care program. Authorized by the McKinney-Vento Act.
Homeless Assistance Grants, HUD.b: Individuals and families experiencing or at risk of homelessness

Federal Pell Grants, Department of Educa.d: Provides need-based grants to low-income undergraduate students to promote post-secondary education access, with maximum awards adjusted annually. The largest federal grant program for higher education, serving approximately 7 million students.

Impact Aid, Department of Education.d: Provides payments to school districts that are financially burdened by federal activities, including districts with large amounts of tax-exempt federal land or that serve children of military families and those living on Indian lands.
Impact Aid, Department of Education.b: School districts affected by federal presence, military-connected students

English Language Acquisition Programs, D.d: Provides formula grants to states for programs serving English learners and immigrant students in K-12 schools. Authorized under Title III of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act.
English Language Acquisition Programs, D.b: English learner and immigrant students in public schools

Student Aid Administration, Department o.d: Funds the administrative costs of Federal Student Aid, the largest provider of student financial aid in the nation, managing a loan portfolio exceeding $1.6 trillion and processing approximately 18 million FAFSA applications annually.
Student Aid Administration, Department o.b: Student loan borrowers, financial aid applicants

School Improvement Programs, Department.d: Funds a collection of formula and competitive grant programs supporting state and local education reform efforts, including 21st Century Community Learning Centers, charter schools, and magnet schools programs.
School Improvement Programs, Department.b: K-12 students, underperforming schools, after-school program participants

Job Corps, Department of Labor.d: Operates the nation’s largest residential education and job training program for at-risk youth ages 16-24, providing academic and vocational training, health care, and job placement at approximately 121 centers. Authorized under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act.

Employment and Training Administration,.d: Funds workforce development programs including Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act formula grants to states, adult and dislocated worker training, apprenticeship, and trade adjustment assistance.
Employment and Training Administration,.b: Job seekers, dislocated workers, employers, state workforce agencies

Unemployment Trust Fund, Department of L.d: Holds state and federal unemployment tax revenues and funds unemployment insurance benefit payments through state programs, as well as federal administrative costs. Extended benefits are triggered during periods of high unemployment.
Unemployment Trust Fund, Department of L.b: Unemployed workers receiving state unemployment insurance benefits

Mine Safety and Health Administration, D.d: Funds MSHA’s enforcement of mine safety and health standards through inspections, investigations, and training at the nation’s coal and metal/non-metal mines. Created by the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977.

Occupational Safety and Health Administr.d: Funds OSHA’s enforcement of workplace safety and health standards through inspections, citations, training, and compliance assistance. Covers approximately 130 million workers at 8 million worksites.
Occupational Safety and Health Administr.b: Workers in covered industries, workplace safety

Low Income Home Energy Assistance Progra.d: Provides block grants to states for heating and cooling assistance, crisis intervention, and weatherization for low-income households. Serves approximately 5-6 million households annually during peak energy cost periods.
Low Income Home Energy Assistance Progra.b: Low-income households, especially elderly and disabled, facing energy costs

Child Support Enforcement, ACF, HHS.d: Provides federal matching funds to states for establishing paternity, locating absent parents, establishing and enforcing child support orders, and collecting payments. Collects approximately $34 billion annually in child support.
Child Support Enforcement, ACF, HHS.b: Children and custodial parents owed child support

Social Services Block Grant (Title XX),.d: Provides flexible block grants to states for a broad range of social services including child care, child protective services, adult protective services, and services for people with disabilities. States have wide discretion in fund usage.
Social Services Block Grant (Title XX),.b: Low-income individuals and families, vulnerable populations needing social services

Head Start, ACF, HHS.d: Provides comprehensive early childhood education, health, nutrition, and parent involvement services to low-income children ages birth to five and pregnant women. Serves approximately 800,000 children through locally operated programs.
Head Start, ACF, HHS.b: Low-income preschool children and their families

Payments to States for Child Care and De.d: Provides federal funds to states for child care subsidies enabling low-income parents to work or attend education and training. Also funds quality improvement activities and health and safety improvements for child care settings.
Payments to States for Child Care and De.b: Low-income working families needing child care assistance

Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program, HRSA.d: Provides comprehensive HIV/AIDS care and treatment services to low-income, uninsured, and underinsured people living with HIV. Funds the AIDS Drug Assistance Program (ADAP) providing antiretroviral medications to over 260,000 individuals.

Community Health Centers (Section 330),.d: Funds approximately 1,400 community health centers providing primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services to medically underserved communities regardless of ability to pay. Serves approximately 30 million patients annually.

National Health Service Corps, HRSA.d: Provides scholarships and loan repayment to health professionals who serve in health professional shortage areas (HPSAs) in exchange for a commitment to practice in underserved communities. Supports approximately 20,000 providers.
National Health Service Corps, HRSA.b: Health professionals with student debt, underserved communities lacking providers

Title X Family Planning Program, HHS.d: Funds a network of approximately 4,000 family planning service sites providing contraception, STI testing, cancer screening, and other preventive health services to low-income and uninsured individuals. The sole federal program dedicated to family planning.
Title X Family Planning Program, HHS.b: Low-income women and men seeking family planning and reproductive health services

Maternal and Child Health Block Grant (T.d: Provides formula grants to states for maternal and child health services, children with special health care needs, and related programs. The oldest federal-state partnership in public health, supporting newborn screening, prenatal care, and child health programs.
Maternal and Child Health Block Grant (T.b: Pregnant women, mothers, infants, children, and children with special health care needs

National Institutes of Health, Buildings.d: Funds the construction, renovation, and maintenance of NIH research facilities on the main Bethesda campus and other NIH-owned locations. Supports the physical infrastructure needed for the intramural research program.
National Institutes of Health, Buildings.b: NIH intramural research programs, campus infrastructure

Commodity Assistance Program, USDA.d: Funds the purchase and distribution of USDA commodities for domestic food assistance programs including The Emergency Food Assistance Program (TEFAP), the Commodity Supplemental Food Program, and commodity purchases for schools.
Commodity Assistance Program, USDA.b: Food banks, low-income individuals, school meal programs

Food Safety and Inspection Service, USDA.d: Funds USDA’s inspection of domestic and imported meat, poultry, and processed egg products to ensure safety, wholesomeness, and proper labeling. Employs approximately 7,800 inspectors at over 6,500 establishments.

Animal and Plant Health Inspection Servi.d: Funds programs to protect U.S. agriculture from plant and animal pests and diseases, regulate genetically engineered organisms, and manage wildlife damage. Responds to outbreaks such as avian influenza and citrus greening.
Animal and Plant Health Inspection Servi.b: Agricultural producers, livestock industry, food supply security

Natural Resources Conservation Service,.d: Provides technical and financial assistance to farmers and landowners for conservation practices through programs including EQIP, CSP, and conservation planning. Originally established as the Soil Conservation Service.
Natural Resources Conservation Service,.b: Farmers, ranchers, landowners, soil and water conservation

Agricultural Research Service, USDA.d: USDA’s principal in-house research agency conducting basic and applied research in animal and crop production, food safety, nutrition, and natural resources at over 90 locations. The largest agricultural research organization in the world.
Agricultural Research Service, USDA.b: Agricultural producers, food industry, scientific community

Rural Development Programs, USDA.d: Provides loans, grants, and technical assistance for rural community facilities, business development, and essential services. Includes the Community Facilities Program, Rural Business-Cooperative Service, and rural broadband programs.
Rural Development Programs, USDA.b: Rural communities, businesses, and essential service providers

Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewabl.d: Funds research, development, and deployment of clean energy technologies including solar, wind, geothermal, bioenergy, advanced vehicles, building efficiency, and advanced manufacturing. Received major boosts from the IIJA and IRA.
Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewabl.b: Clean energy industry, consumers, climate and energy security

Environmental and Other Defense Activiti.d: Funds cleanup of contaminated sites from decades of nuclear weapons production and government-sponsored nuclear energy research, including the Hanford and Savannah River sites. One of the world’s largest environmental remediation programs.
Environmental and Other Defense Activiti.b: Communities near former nuclear production sites, environmental remediation

Nuclear Energy Programs, DOE.d: Funds research and development of advanced nuclear energy technologies including small modular reactors, advanced reactor designs, nuclear fuel cycle research, and management of spent nuclear fuel and high-level radioactive waste.
Nuclear Energy Programs, DOE.b: Nuclear energy industry, energy security, climate goals

Power Marketing Administrations (Southea.d: Three DOE power marketing administrations that market and transmit wholesale electricity generated at federal hydroelectric dams to preference customers including rural cooperatives and municipal utilities across their respective regions.
Power Marketing Administrations (Southea.b: Rural electric cooperatives, municipal utilities, wholesale power customers

Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.d: Regulates interstate transmission of electricity, natural gas, and oil, licenses hydroelectric projects, and oversees wholesale electricity markets. Self-funded through annual charges and filing fees assessed on regulated entities.
Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.b: Energy consumers, regulated utilities, wholesale energy markets

Small Business Administration, Salaries.d: Funds SBA’s operating costs for administering small business programs including guaranteed lending (7(a) and 504 loans), contracting assistance, business counseling, and small business advocacy. Supports approximately 33 million small businesses.

Legal Services Corporation.d: Provides grants to independent nonprofit legal aid programs delivering civil legal assistance to low-income Americans in matters including housing, family law, consumer issues, and public benefits. Serves approximately 1.8 million people annually.
Legal Services Corporation.b: Low-income Americans needing civil legal assistance

Corporation for National and Community S.d: Funds national service programs including AmeriCorps State and National, AmeriCorps VISTA, AmeriCorps NCCC, and Senior Corps, engaging Americans in community service in exchange for education awards. Supports approximately 200,000 members and volunteers annually.
Corporation for National and Community S.b: AmeriCorps members, communities served by national service programs

Corporation for Public Broadcasting.d: Provides federal funding to public television and radio stations, supporting programming, station operations, and interconnection services for the public broadcasting system. Funds approximately 1,500 locally managed public media stations.
Corporation for Public Broadcasting.b: Public media audiences, local public television and radio stations

National Endowment for the Arts.d: Provides grants to nonprofit arts organizations, state arts agencies, and individual artists to support artistic excellence, creativity, and public access to the arts. The largest annual funder of the arts in the United States.
National Endowment for the Arts.b: Arts organizations, artists, communities served by funded arts programs

National Endowment for the Humanities.d: Provides grants for humanities research, education, preservation, and public programs in areas including history, literature, philosophy, languages, and archaeology. Supports state humanities councils in every state and territory.
National Endowment for the Humanities.b: Scholars, educators, cultural institutions, general public

Institute of Museum and Library Services.d: Provides federal grants and policy development for the nation’s approximately 35,000 museums and 120,000 libraries. Administers the Library Services and Technology Act and Museum Services Act funding.
Institute of Museum and Library Services.b: Public libraries, museums, library and museum patrons

Election Assistance Commission.d: Provides grants and guidance to state and local election administrators for election administration improvements, voting system certification, and election security. Created by the Help America Vote Act following the 2000 election.
Election Assistance Commission.b: State and local election officials, voters

Government Accountability Office.d: Funds GAO’s audits, evaluations, and investigations of federal programs and expenditures on behalf of Congress. Known as the ’congressional watchdog,’ it produces approximately 700 reports annually identifying billions in potential savings.

Office of Personnel Management, Salaries.d: Funds OPM’s administration of the federal civil service including hiring policy, classification, employee benefits, retirement, background investigations, and workforce management for approximately 2.1 million federal civilian employees.
Office of Personnel Management, Salaries.b: Federal civilian employees, job applicants, retirees

General Services Administration, Acquisi.d: A revolving fund financing GSA’s procurement services including Federal Supply Schedules, assisted acquisition, and technology solutions for federal agencies. Provides government-wide contracts generating significant volume discounts.
General Services Administration, Acquisi.b: Federal agencies purchasing goods and services

Payments to Health Care Trust Funds, CMS.d: Treasury transfers general revenue funds to the Medicare Hospital Insurance (Part A) and Supplementary Medical Insurance (Part B/D) trust funds. Covers the gap between dedicated revenues and program costs.

Payments to Military Retirement Fund, De.d: Treasury payment to the Military Retirement Fund covering the unfunded liability for military retirement benefits accrued before the fund’s creation. Ensures actuarial soundness of the retirement system.

Refundable Premium Assistance Tax Credit.d: Funds the premium tax credits under the Affordable Care Act for individuals purchasing health insurance on marketplace exchanges. Refundable portion paid to insurers on behalf of eligible enrollees.
Refundable Premium Assistance Tax Credit.b: Low- and moderate-income individuals/families purchasing ACA marketplace plans

Medicare Prescription Drug Account, CMS,.d: Finances the Medicare Part D prescription drug benefit enacted by the Medicare Modernization Act. Funded primarily through beneficiary premiums and general revenue transfers.
Medicare Prescription Drug Account, CMS,.b: Medicare beneficiaries enrolled in Part D drug plans

Federal Direct Student Loan Program, Edu.d: Provides federal student loans directly from the Treasury to eligible students and parents for postsecondary education. Includes Stafford (subsidized/unsubsidized), PLUS, and consolidation loans.
Federal Direct Student Loan Program, Edu.b: College and graduate students, parents of undergraduates

Medical Services, Veterans Health Admini.d: Funds inpatient, outpatient, and mental health care services at VA medical centers and clinics nationwide. The largest single VA appropriation supporting direct health care delivery.
Medical Services, Veterans Health Admini.b: Eligible veterans enrolled in VA health care

Payment Where Earned Income Credit Excee.d: Funds the refundable portion of the Earned Income Tax Credit when the credit exceeds a filer’s income tax liability. One of the largest anti-poverty programs in the federal budget.
Payment Where Earned Income Credit Excee.b: Low- to moderate-income working individuals and families

Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Con.d: Provides cash to liquidate obligations incurred under contract authority for the federal-aid highway program. Funded primarily from the Highway Trust Fund’s highway account.
Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Con.b: State and local highway agencies, motorists, commercial transportation

Payments to Social Security Trust Funds,.d: General fund transfers to the OASI and DI trust funds covering items such as military service credits, taxation of benefits revenue, and special COVID-era credits. Ensures trust fund solvency for legislatively mandated obligations.

Payment to Civil Service Retirement and.d: Treasury payment covering the government’s share of retirement costs for federal civilian employees under CSRS and FERS, including the unfunded liability amortization. The largest single intragovernmental transfer for civilian retirement.
Payment to Civil Service Retirement and.b: Federal civilian retirees and their survivors

Military Personnel, Army.d: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for active-duty Army enlisted and officer personnel. Covers basic pay, BAH, BAS, special/incentive pays, and permanent change of station travel.

Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.d: Funds Air Force research, development, testing, and evaluation of weapons systems, technology, and equipment. Covers basic research through operational system development and live-fire testing.
Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.b: Air Force, defense contractors, national security

Defense Health Program, Defense.d: Consolidated account funding the TRICARE military health system, including direct care at military treatment facilities and purchased care from civilian providers. Covers medical and dental services for active duty, dependents, and retirees.
Defense Health Program, Defense.b: Active-duty members, military retirees, and dependents

State and Tribal Assistance Grants, EPA.d: EPA’s largest grant program funding state, tribal, and local governments for water infrastructure (drinking water and clean water state revolving funds) and environmental program implementation. Includes categorical grants for pollution control programs.
State and Tribal Assistance Grants, EPA.b: State/tribal/local governments, public water systems, communities

Unemployment Trust Fund - Treasury Manag.d: Holds federal and state unemployment tax receipts and invests them in special Treasury obligations. States draw from their individual accounts to pay unemployment insurance benefits.
Unemployment Trust Fund - Treasury Manag.b: Unemployed workers eligible for state/federal unemployment benefits

Taxation on OASDI Benefits, HI, CMS, HHS.d: Transfers revenue from federal income taxation of Social Security benefits to the Medicare Hospital Insurance trust fund. Enacted as part of the 1993 Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act.

Disaster Relief Fund, FEMA, Homeland Sec.d: Primary federal account for disaster response and recovery, funding FEMA’s public and individual assistance programs after presidential disaster declarations. Receives both regular appropriations and emergency supplemental funding.

Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.d: Funds RDT&E activities managed by defense agencies and the Office of the Secretary of Defense, including DARPA, MDA, and DTRA programs. Covers cross-cutting research not assigned to a specific military department.

Military Personnel, Navy.d: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for active-duty Navy enlisted and officer personnel. Covers basic pay, housing and subsistence allowances, and special/incentive pays.

Military Personnel, Air Force.d: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for active-duty Air Force and Space Force personnel. Covers basic pay, BAH, BAS, special pays, and PCS travel.
Military Personnel, Air Force.b: Active-duty Air Force and Space Force service members

Child Nutrition Programs, Food and Nutri.d: Funds the National School Lunch Program, School Breakfast Program, Child and Adult Care Food Program, and other child feeding initiatives. Provides cash reimbursements and commodity support to participating institutions.
Child Nutrition Programs, Food and Nutri.b: Children in schools, childcare, and summer feeding programs

Other Procurement, Air Force.d: Funds Air Force procurement of vehicles, communications/electronics equipment, and other support items not covered by aircraft or missile procurement. Includes classified programs and base support equipment.
Other Procurement, Air Force.b: Air Force units and national defense

Medical Community Care, Veterans Health.d: Funds veteran health care purchased from non-VA community providers under the MISSION Act. Covers care when VA facilities lack capacity, capability, or geographic accessibility.
Medical Community Care, Veterans Health.b: Veterans requiring care from community (non-VA) health providers

Acquisition Services Fund, GSA.d: GSA revolving fund providing federal agencies with IT solutions, professional services, and assisted acquisition services on a fee-for-service basis. Successor to the former IT Fund, operating without annual appropriations.
Acquisition Services Fund, GSA.b: Federal agencies purchasing IT and professional services

Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.d: Funds Navy and Marine Corps RDT&E for weapons systems, shipboard systems, marine technology, and advanced concepts. Covers basic research through operational systems development.
Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.b: Navy/Marine Corps, defense industry, national security

Payment Where Child Tax Credit Exceeds L.d: Funds the refundable (additional) portion of the Child Tax Credit when it exceeds a filer’s tax liability. Significantly expanded temporarily by the 2021 American Rescue Plan.
Payment Where Child Tax Credit Exceeds L.b: Families with qualifying children, especially lower-income households

Universal Service Fund, FCC.d: Off-budget fund financed by telecom carrier assessments to support affordable telecommunications in underserved areas. Includes E-Rate (schools/libraries), Lifeline, Rural Health Care, and High-Cost programs.
Universal Service Fund, FCC.b: Rural/low-income consumers, schools, libraries, rural health providers

Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.d: Funds Army RDT&E for ground combat vehicles, weapons, aviation, soldier systems, and network technologies. Covers basic and applied research through full-scale system development and testing.
Research, Development, Test and Evaluati.b: Army units, defense contractors, national security

Broadband Equity Access and Deployment P.d: $42.45 billion program created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act to fund broadband infrastructure deployment in unserved and underserved areas. Distributes formula grants to states and territories.
Broadband Equity Access and Deployment P.b: Unserved/underserved communities, states, territories, broadband providers

Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund,.d: Funds the federal crop insurance program, paying premium subsidies and administrative/operating expense reimbursements to private insurance companies. Covers underwriting losses when indemnities exceed premiums.
Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund,.b: Farmers, ranchers, and approved private crop insurance companies

Operations and Support, CBP, Homeland Se.d: Primary appropriation for Customs and Border Protection’s operations including border security, trade enforcement, and immigration inspection. Covers personnel compensation, technology, and air/marine operations.
Operations and Support, CBP, Homeland Se.b: CBP workforce, border communities, lawful trade and travel

Deposit Insurance Fund, FDIC.d: Insures bank deposits up to $250,000 per depositor per institution, funded by risk-based assessments on insured depository institutions. Covers losses from bank failures and resolution costs.
Deposit Insurance Fund, FDIC.b: Depositors at FDIC-insured banks and savings institutions

Department of Defense Military Retiremen.d: Trust fund paying retired pay and survivor benefits for military retirees, financed by DoD contributions, Treasury payments for the unfunded liability, and investment earnings. Operates on an accrual accounting basis.
Department of Defense Military Retiremen.b: Military retirees, survivors, and their families

Employees Health Benefits Fund, OPM.d: Revolving fund financing the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program, the largest employer-sponsored health plan in the U.S. Funded by employee and government premium contributions.
Employees Health Benefits Fund, OPM.b: Federal employees, retirees, and their dependents

Operation and Maintenance, Navy.d: Funds Navy day-to-day operating and training costs including ship and aircraft maintenance, base operations, and civilian personnel. The Navy’s largest annual appropriation by obligation.
Operation and Maintenance, Navy.b: Navy fleet readiness and operational forces

Operation and Maintenance, Army.d: Funds Army training, equipment maintenance, base operations, logistics, and civilian workforce. Sustains readiness of active-duty Army units and installations worldwide.

Operation and Maintenance, Air Force.d: Funds Air Force flying operations, depot maintenance, base support, civilian personnel, and logistics. Sustains combat readiness of air and space forces.
Operation and Maintenance, Air Force.b: Air Force operational units and installations

Operation and Maintenance, Defense-Wide.d: Funds operating costs for defense agencies and OSD activities including SOCOM, DISA, DCMA, and defense-wide initiatives. Covers personnel, logistics, and program operations across multiple agencies.
Operation and Maintenance, Defense-Wide.b: Defense agencies, combatant commands, special operations forces

Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund, VA.d: Created by the PACT Act to fund health care and benefits for veterans exposed to burn pits, Agent Orange, and other toxic substances. Designated as mandatory spending outside discretionary caps.

Education for the Disadvantaged, Educati.d: Funds Title I grants to local educational agencies serving high concentrations of children from low-income families. The largest federal K-12 education program supporting academic achievement in high-poverty schools.
Education for the Disadvantaged, Educati.b: Low-income students and high-poverty school districts

Operations and Support, ICE, Homeland Se.d: Primary appropriation for Immigration and Customs Enforcement covering homeland security investigations, enforcement and removal operations, and detention. Funds personnel, detention beds, and investigative operations.

Science, Energy Programs, DOE.d: Funds DOE’s Office of Science, the nation’s largest sponsor of basic research in the physical sciences. Supports national laboratories, large-scale user facilities, and research in energy, computing, and nuclear physics.
Science, Energy Programs, DOE.b: Researchers, national laboratories, universities, scientific community

Medical Support and Compliance, VHA, VA.d: Funds VA health care administration, oversight, quality management, and compliance activities. Covers health informatics, medical research support, and management infrastructure.
Medical Support and Compliance, VHA, VA.b: VA health system administration and indirectly all VA patients

Operating Expenses, Coast Guard, Homelan.d: Funds Coast Guard day-to-day operations including search and rescue, maritime law enforcement, port security, and aids to navigation. Covers military and civilian personnel, maintenance, and logistics.
Operating Expenses, Coast Guard, Homelan.b: Coast Guard personnel, mariners, port communities

Operations and Support, TSA, Homeland Se.d: Funds Transportation Security Administration passenger and baggage screening, Federal Air Marshal Service, and surface transportation security. Created after September 11 to secure the nation’s transportation systems.
Operations and Support, TSA, Homeland Se.b: Air travelers, transportation systems, TSA workforce

Community Development Fund (CDBG), HUD.d: Formula and competitive grants to states and local governments for community development activities including housing rehabilitation, infrastructure, and economic development. Primarily targets low- and moderate-income communities.

General Operating Expenses, VBA, VA.d: Funds the Veterans Benefits Administration’s claims processing, compensation and pension administration, education benefits processing, and loan guaranty oversight. Covers staffing and IT for all VBA programs.
General Operating Expenses, VBA, VA.b: Veterans and dependents applying for or receiving VA benefits

Foster Care and Permanency Payments, ACF.d: Open-ended entitlement funding maintenance payments for children in foster care, adoption assistance, and kinship guardianship assistance under Title IV-E of the Social Security Act. Federal share is based on state Medicaid matching rates.
Foster Care and Permanency Payments, ACF.b: Children in foster care, adoptive families, kinship guardians

Exchange Stabilization Fund, Treasury.d: Treasury fund for foreign exchange market intervention and international monetary stabilization, capitalized from gold revaluation profits. Also used for emergency lending (e.g., backed TARP and money market guarantees in 2008).
Exchange Stabilization Fund, Treasury.b: U.S. financial system stability, international monetary order

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison Sy.d: Funds operation of the federal prison system including custody, care, and correctional programs for federal inmates across 122+ institutions. Covers staff compensation, inmate medical care, food, and facility operations.
Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison Sy.b: Federal inmates, correctional staff, public safety

Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to M.d: Treasury payments to the Military Retirement Fund to cover concurrent receipt of military retirement pay and VA disability compensation. Authorized by the FY2004 NDAA eliminating the offset for qualifying retirees.
Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to M.b: Military retirees with combat-related or qualifying VA disability ratings

Readjustment Benefits, VBA, VA (GI Bill).d: Funds the Post-9/11 GI Bill, Montgomery GI Bill, and other readjustment programs including vocational rehabilitation and employment services. One of the largest mandatory spending accounts at VA.
Readjustment Benefits, VBA, VA (GI Bill).b: Veterans, service members, and eligible dependents pursuing education/training

Federal Family Education Loan Program Ac.d: Manages the legacy portfolio of federally guaranteed student loans originated by private lenders before the program ended in 2010. Now in wind-down, covering default claims, consolidation, and interest subsidies on outstanding loans.
Federal Family Education Loan Program Ac.b: Borrowers with outstanding FFEL loans, guaranty agencies

Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers.d: Finances Army Corps of Engineers work performed for non-federal entities and other federal agencies on a reimbursable basis. Covers environmental remediation, water testing, and engineering services.
Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers.b: Federal agencies, state/local governments, and other customers of Corps services

DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Car.d: Accrual fund financing TRICARE-for-Life benefits for Medicare-eligible military retirees, dependents, and survivors. Established by the FY2001 NDAA with contributions from DoD and Treasury.
DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Car.b: Medicare-eligible military retirees, dependents, and survivors age 65+

Salaries and Expenses, FBI, Justice.d: Primary appropriation for the Federal Bureau of Investigation covering counterterrorism, counterintelligence, cybercrime, and criminal investigative programs. Funds special agents, intelligence analysts, and forensic capabilities.
Salaries and Expenses, FBI, Justice.b: Federal law enforcement, public safety, national security

Reserve Personnel, Army/Navy/Marine Corp.d: Funds pay and allowances for Selected Reserve and Individual Ready Reserve members during drill, annual training, and active-duty tours. Separate appropriation for each service’s reserve component.
Reserve Personnel, Army/Navy/Marine Corp.b: Reserve component service members across all branches

Procurement of Ammunition, Army/Navy/Air.d: Funds procurement of conventional ammunition, munitions, and related items for each service. Covers small arms, artillery, bombs, and other expendable ordnance.
Procurement of Ammunition, Army/Navy/Air.b: Military service members and combat forces

Office of the Inspector General, DoD.d: Funds the DoD IG’s independent oversight through audits, investigations, evaluations, and hotline operations. Provides accountability for the largest departmental budget in the federal government.

Military Construction, Army/Navy/Air For.d: Funds planning, design, and construction of military facilities including barracks, training ranges, maintenance facilities, and operational buildings. Separate appropriations for each service and component.
Military Construction, Army/Navy/Air For.b: Military service members, installations, and local communities

Family Housing Construction and O&M, Arm.d: Funds construction of new military family housing and operation/maintenance of existing units including utilities, furnishings, and privatization support. Separate accounts for each service.
Family Housing Construction and O&M, Arm.b: Military families residing in on-base housing

Defense Intelligence Agency.d: Funds the principal producer of foreign military intelligence for DoD, providing analysis, HUMINT collection, and technical intelligence to warfighters and policymakers. Budget details are largely classified.

National Security Agency.d: Funds signals intelligence collection, cryptologic activities, and cybersecurity operations for the nation. Budget is classified within the National Intelligence Program and Military Intelligence Program.
National Security Agency.b: National security community, military operations, cybersecurity defense

Federal Housing Finance Agency.d: Independent regulator overseeing Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and the Federal Home Loan Banks. Created by HERA 2008, replacing OFHEO and the FHFB.

Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC).d: Treasury office administering and enforcing U.S. economic and trade sanctions programs against targeted countries, entities, and individuals. Maintains the Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list.
Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC).b: U.S. national security and foreign policy objectives

United States Mint.d: Treasury bureau manufacturing and distributing circulating coinage, bullion, and numismatic products. Self-funded through seigniorage (difference between face value and production cost) and product sales.
United States Mint.b: U.S. economy, coin collectors, bullion investors

Bureau of Engraving and Printing.d: Treasury bureau designing, manufacturing, and supplying Federal Reserve notes (paper currency) and other security documents. Self-funded revolving fund financed by payments from the Federal Reserve.
Bureau of Engraving and Printing.b: Federal Reserve System, U.S. economy, currency users

Operations Support, IRS.d: Funds IRS infrastructure including IT, facilities, printing, postage, and shared services that support all IRS operations. Reorganized from prior IRS accounts under the IRS Restructuring and Reform Act implementation.
Operations Support, IRS.b: IRS workforce and indirectly all U.S. taxpayers through functional tax administration

Enforcement, IRS.d: Funds IRS examination, collection, criminal investigation, and regulatory enforcement activities to ensure tax compliance. Covers audits, investigations of tax fraud, and legal counsel for enforcement actions.
Enforcement, IRS.b: U.S. Treasury (revenue collection) and compliant taxpayers through deterrence of tax evasion

Business Systems Modernization, IRS.d: Dedicated capital account for modernizing IRS legacy information technology systems, including taxpayer-facing digital services. Created under the IRS Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998.
Business Systems Modernization, IRS.b: Taxpayers through improved digital services; IRS through modernized IT infrastructure

Customs Duties Collections, CBP.d: One of the oldest federal revenue accounts, collecting tariffs and duties on imported goods at U.S. ports of entry. Administered by Customs and Border Protection within DHS since 2003, successor to the original U.S. Customs Service.
Customs Duties Collections, CBP.b: U.S. Treasury (revenue); domestic industries protected by tariffs

Indian Health Service.d: Provides federally funded health care to members of federally recognized American Indian and Alaska Native tribes through a network of hospitals, clinics, and contracted tribal health programs. Established by the Transfer Act of 1954, effective 1955.
Indian Health Service.b: Approximately 2.6 million American Indians and Alaska Natives in federally recognized tribes

CDC Immunization Programs.d: Funds vaccine purchase, distribution, surveillance, and immunization infrastructure through programs like Vaccines for Children (VFC) and Section 317 grants. Supports state and local immunization efforts and outbreak response.
CDC Immunization Programs.b: Children, adults, and communities nationwide; uninsured and underinsured populations for vaccine access

CDC HIV/AIDS Prevention.d: Funds surveillance, testing, prevention interventions, and public education to reduce HIV transmission in the United States. Includes funding to state and local health departments and community-based organizations.
CDC HIV/AIDS Prevention.b: Populations at elevated HIV risk; people living with HIV; state and local public health agencies

CDC Chronic Disease Prevention.d: Funds programs addressing heart disease, cancer, diabetes, tobacco use, and other chronic conditions through surveillance, prevention grants, and health promotion. Operates through the National Center for Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Promotion.
CDC Chronic Disease Prevention.b: General U.S. population; communities with high chronic disease burden; state health departments

FDA Salaries and Expenses.d: Primary operating account for the Food and Drug Administration, funding regulation of food safety, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, biologics, tobacco, and cosmetics. Supplemented significantly by industry user fees since 1992.
FDA Salaries and Expenses.b: U.S. consumers of food, drugs, medical devices, and regulated products

SAMHSA Programs.d: Funds the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration’s grant programs including the Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Block Grant and Community Mental Health Services Block Grant. Created by the ADAMHA Reorganization Act of 1992.
SAMHSA Programs.b: Individuals with substance use disorders and mental illness; state behavioral health agencies; community treatment providers

HRSA Health Programs.d: Umbrella operating account for the Health Resources and Services Administration, funding workforce development, primary care access, maternal/child health, and health systems in underserved areas. HRSA was established as a PHS agency in 1982.
HRSA Health Programs.b: Medically underserved populations, rural communities, health professionals in shortage areas

Refugee and Entrant Assistance, ACF, HHS.d: Funds resettlement services for refugees, asylees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, and unaccompanied migrant children, including cash assistance, medical screening, and social integration. Authorized by the Refugee Act of 1980.
Refugee and Entrant Assistance, ACF, HHS.b: Refugees, asylees, unaccompanied alien children, Cuban/Haitian entrants, victims of trafficking

Aging and Disability Services, ACL.d: Funds programs under the Older Americans Act including home-delivered and congregate meals, caregiver support, senior community service employment, and disability services. ACL was established in 2012 consolidating the Administration on Aging.
Aging and Disability Services, ACL.b: Older adults (60+), family caregivers, people with disabilities

CMS Program Management.d: Administrative operating account for the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, funding program oversight, claims processing, fraud prevention, survey and certification of providers, and ACA marketplace operations. Separate from benefit payments.
CMS Program Management.b: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries indirectly through program integrity and administration

BARDA/ASPR.d: The Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority, within the Assistant Secretary for Preparedness and Response, develops medical countermeasures against chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and pandemic threats. Created by the Pandemic and All-Hazards Preparedness Act of 2006.
BARDA/ASPR.b: U.S. population (national health security); pharmaceutical/biotech firms developing countermeasures

Low Income Home Energy Assistance Progra.d: Block grant helping low-income households pay heating and cooling bills and weatherize homes, distributed to states based on climate and poverty formulas. Authorized by the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Act of 1981.
Low Income Home Energy Assistance Progra.b: Low-income households, especially elderly, disabled, and families with young children facing high energy costs

Child Support Enforcement, ACF.d: Federal matching funds for state child support enforcement programs including paternity establishment, support order establishment, and collections. Created as Title IV-D of the Social Security Act in 1975.
Child Support Enforcement, ACF.b: Custodial parents and children owed child support; state IV-D agencies

Social Services Block Grant (Title XX).d: Flexible block grant allowing states to fund a wide range of social services including child care, protective services, foster care, and services for disabled adults. Authorized under Title XX of the Social Security Act.
Social Services Block Grant (Title XX).b: Low-income individuals and families; children at risk of abuse/neglect; elderly and disabled adults

Head Start, ACF.d: Provides comprehensive early childhood education, health, nutrition, and family support services to low-income children ages 0-5 and their families. Launched as part of President Johnson’s War on Poverty.
Head Start, ACF.b: Low-income preschool children and their families; pregnant women (Early Head Start)

Child Care and Development Block Grant.d: Primary federal program subsidizing child care for low-income working families, distributed as block grants to states with substantial state flexibility. Reauthorized and expanded by the CCDBG Act of 2014.
Child Care and Development Block Grant.b: Low-income working families with children under age 13; child care providers

Title X Family Planning Program.d: The sole federal program dedicated to providing comprehensive family planning and preventive health services, including contraception, STI screening, and cancer screening. Authorized under Title X of the Public Health Service Act.
Title X Family Planning Program.b: Low-income and uninsured individuals seeking reproductive health services; approximately 4 million patients annually

Maternal and Child Health Block Grant (T.d: One of the oldest federal-state partnership programs, funding maternal and child health services, children with special health care needs, and public health infrastructure. Originally part of the Social Security Act of 1935.
Maternal and Child Health Block Grant (T.b: Pregnant women, mothers, infants, children, and children with special health care needs

NIH Buildings and Facilities.d: Capital account funding construction, renovation, and maintenance of NIH research facilities, primarily on the Bethesda, Maryland campus. Supports the physical infrastructure for intramural biomedical research.
NIH Buildings and Facilities.b: NIH intramural researchers; biomedical research enterprise

Environmental Cleanup Programs, DOE.d: Funds cleanup of radioactive and hazardous waste at former nuclear weapons production and energy research sites across the DOE complex, managed by the Office of Environmental Management. One of the largest cleanup programs in the world.
Environmental Cleanup Programs, DOE.b: Communities near contaminated DOE sites; environment; cleanup workforce

Small Business Administration Salaries a.d: Operating account for the SBA funding administration of small business loan guaranty programs, counseling, contracting assistance, and advocacy. SBA was created by the Small Business Act of 1953.
Small Business Administration Salaries a.b: Small businesses, entrepreneurs, and SBA staff

AmeriCorps/Corporation for National and.d: Funds national service programs including AmeriCorps State and National, AmeriCorps VISTA, AmeriCorps NCCC, and Senior Corps, engaging Americans in community service in exchange for education awards. Created by the National and Community Service Trust Act of 1993.
AmeriCorps/Corporation for National and.b: Service members (education awards), communities receiving volunteer service, nonprofits hosting members

OPM Salaries and Expenses.d: Operating account for the Office of Personnel Management, which manages the federal civilian workforce including hiring policy, background investigations, employee benefits administration, and retirement services. OPM was established when the Civil Service Commission was reorganized in 1979.
OPM Salaries and Expenses.b: Federal civilian employees, retirees, and federal agencies

GSA Acquisition Services Fund.d: Revolving fund through which GSA procures goods and services (IT, vehicles, supplies, telecommunications) in bulk for federal agencies, leveraging economies of scale. Operates on a fee-for-service cost-recovery basis.
GSA Acquisition Services Fund.b: Federal agencies purchasing goods and services; taxpayers through procurement savings

NIH National Institute of Mental Health.d: Funds research on mental disorders including depression, schizophrenia, anxiety, PTSD, and autism spectrum disorder, spanning basic neuroscience through clinical trials and services research. The lead federal agency for research on mental illnesses.
NIH National Institute of Mental Health.b: Individuals with mental health conditions; researchers; clinicians through improved treatment evidence

NIH National Institute on Drug Abuse.d: Funds research on drug use and addiction including neuroscience of addiction, prevention, treatment development, and public health consequences. Leads federal research on the opioid crisis and emerging drug threats.
NIH National Institute on Drug Abuse.b: Individuals with substance use disorders; researchers; public health agencies; treatment providers

NIH NIDDK (National Institute of Diabete.d: Funds research on diabetes, endocrine and metabolic diseases, digestive diseases, nutrition, obesity, and kidney/urologic/hematologic diseases. Supports major clinical trials and research networks for these conditions.
NIH NIDDK (National Institute of Diabete.b: Patients with diabetes, kidney disease, digestive disorders, and related conditions; researchers

NIH National Institute on Aging.d: Leads federal research on aging and age-related diseases, particularly Alzheimer’s disease and related dementias, including biology of aging, behavioral research, and geriatrics. Received major funding increases for Alzheimer’s research since 2015.
NIH National Institute on Aging.b: Older adults, Alzheimer’s patients and caregivers, aging researchers

NIH NICHD (Eunice Kennedy Shriver Nation.d: Funds research on fertility, pregnancy, childhood development, intellectual and developmental disabilities, and rehabilitation science. Established at President Kennedy’s direction to study the processes of human development.
NIH NICHD (Eunice Kennedy Shriver Nation.b: Infants, children, pregnant women, individuals with developmental disabilities, researchers

NIH NINDS (National Institute of Neurolo.d: Funds research on the brain, spinal cord, and nervous system disorders including stroke, epilepsy, Parkinson’s, ALS, traumatic brain injury, and multiple sclerosis. Originally the National Institute of Neurological Diseases and Blindness.
NIH NINDS (National Institute of Neurolo.b: Patients with neurological disorders; neuroscience researchers; clinicians

NIH NIEHS (National Institute of Environ.d: Funds research on how environmental exposures (chemicals, pollutants, radiation, climate) affect human health and disease, including the Superfund Research Program. Located in Research Triangle Park, North Carolina.
NIH NIEHS (National Institute of Environ.b: Communities exposed to environmental hazards; environmental health researchers; regulatory agencies using NIEHS evidence

Bureau of Reclamation Construction.d: Funds construction and rehabilitation of water infrastructure projects (dams, reservoirs, canals, water treatment facilities) in the 17 western states. Established by the Reclamation Act of 1902 to develop arid western lands through irrigation.
Bureau of Reclamation Construction.b: Western U.S. water users including irrigators, municipalities, tribes, and hydropower consumers

NSF Education and Human Resources.d: Funds STEM education programs from K-12 through graduate level, including teacher preparation, undergraduate research experiences, graduate fellowships, and broadening participation of underrepresented groups. NSF’s education directorate.
NSF Education and Human Resources.b: STEM students at all levels, educators, researchers in STEM education, underrepresented groups in science

NSF Major Research Equipment and Facilit.d: Funds construction of large-scale, cutting-edge research infrastructure such as major telescopes, particle detectors, research vessels, and cyberinfrastructure. Supports facilities too large for individual research grants.
NSF Major Research Equipment and Facilit.b: Research community using large-scale facilities; universities; national and international scientific collaborations

Bureau of Indian Education.d: Operates and funds approximately 180 elementary and secondary schools and dormitories serving Native American students on or near reservations, plus supports tribal colleges. One of only two federal school systems in the U.S.
Bureau of Indian Education.b: American Indian and Alaska Native students in BIE-funded schools; tribal colleges and universities

HOME Investment Partnerships, HUD.d: Formula block grant to states and localities for building, buying, and rehabilitating affordable housing for low-income households, including rental and homeowner assistance. Created by the Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act of 1990.
HOME Investment Partnerships, HUD.b: Low-income renters and homebuyers; state and local governments; nonprofit housing developers

HOPWA (Housing Opportunities for Persons.d: The only federal program dedicated to housing assistance for people living with HIV/AIDS and their families, providing rental assistance, short-term housing, and supportive services. Authorized by the AIDS Housing Opportunity Act.
HOPWA (Housing Opportunities for Persons.b: Low-income persons living with HIV/AIDS and their families

Impact Aid, Education.d: Compensates school districts for lost property tax revenue due to federal presence (military bases, Indian lands, federal facilities) and educates federally connected children. One of the oldest federal education programs.
Impact Aid, Education.b: School districts with significant federal land or military installations; children of military families and those on Indian lands

English Language Acquisition, Education.d: Formula grants to states for programs serving English learners (ELs) in K-12 schools, supporting language instruction and academic achievement. Originally the Bilingual Education Act (Title VII), now Title III of ESEA/ESSA.
English Language Acquisition, Education.b: English learner students in public schools; immigrant children and youth; schools with EL populations

Student Aid Administration, Education.d: Funds the administrative costs of operating the federal student financial aid programs (Pell Grants, Direct Loans, work-study), including application processing, loan servicing, and compliance. Operates Federal Student Aid as a PBO.
Student Aid Administration, Education.b: College students and borrowers; postsecondary institutions; loan servicers

School Improvement Programs, Education.d: Funds various K-12 education programs supporting school improvement, including 21st Century Community Learning Centers, education for homeless children, rural education, and other formula and competitive grants under ESEA/ESSA.
School Improvement Programs, Education.b: K-12 students in underperforming or high-need schools; school districts; states

Job Corps, Labor.d: Residential education and vocational training program for at-risk youth ages 16-24, providing academic instruction, career technical training, and social skills development at approximately 120 centers nationwide. Created by the Economic Opportunity Act of 1964.
Job Corps, Labor.b: Disadvantaged and at-risk youth ages 16-24 lacking education or job skills

Employment and Training Administration,.d: Funds federal workforce development programs including WIOA formula grants to states, Trade Adjustment Assistance, apprenticeship, unemployment insurance administration, and employment services. Current framework authorized under WIOA (2014).
Employment and Training Administration,.b: Job seekers, dislocated workers, employers, state workforce agencies, One-Stop Career Centers

Mine Safety and Health Administration, L.d: Funds enforcement of mine safety and health standards in all U.S. mines (coal and metal/nonmetal) through inspections, investigations, training, and standard-setting. Established by the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977.
Mine Safety and Health Administration, L.b: Mine workers (approximately 250,000); mining companies through safety compliance guidance

OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Adm.d: Funds workplace safety and health inspections, standard-setting, whistleblower protections, compliance assistance, and training grants. Created by the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970.
OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Adm.b: Workers in private industry and some public sector; employers through compliance assistance

Child Nutrition Programs, USDA.d: Umbrella account funding the National School Lunch Program, School Breakfast Program, Child and Adult Care Food Program, Summer Food Service, and Fresh Fruit and Vegetable Program through federal reimbursements to schools and institutions. Originally authorized by the National School Lunch Act of 1946.
Child Nutrition Programs, USDA.b: Children in schools, child care, and summer programs; low-income families; schools and care institutions

WIC Program (Special Supplemental Nutrit.d: Provides supplemental foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals to low-income pregnant/postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5. Piloted in 1972, made permanent in 1975.
WIC Program (Special Supplemental Nutrit.b: Low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children under age 5 (approximately 6 million participants)

National School Lunch Program.d: Provides free and reduced-price lunches to eligible children in participating schools and residential child care institutions through per-meal federal reimbursements and commodity donations. The largest child nutrition program, serving about 30 million children daily.
National School Lunch Program.b: School-age children, particularly low-income students qualifying for free or reduced-price meals

School Breakfast Program.d: Provides federal reimbursement for breakfast served in schools, with higher rates for free and reduced-price meals for low-income students. Piloted in 1966, made permanent in 1975, serves approximately 15 million children daily.
School Breakfast Program.b: School-age children, especially low-income students; schools in low-income areas with severe need

Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service.d: Funds wildfire suppression, fuels management, prescribed burning, preparedness, and post-fire rehabilitation on National Forest System lands and adjacent areas. One of the largest and most volatile USDA accounts due to fire season costs.
Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service.b: Communities in the wildland-urban interface; national forests; firefighters; affected ecosystems

Management of Lands and Resources, BLM.d: Primary operating account for the Bureau of Land Management funding administration of 245 million acres of public lands including grazing, energy/mineral leasing, recreation, wild horse management, and resource protection.
Management of Lands and Resources, BLM.b: Ranchers, energy companies, recreationists, and communities near BLM-managed public lands

Resource Management, Fish and Wildlife S.d: Primary operating account for the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service funding management of the National Wildlife Refuge System, endangered species conservation, migratory bird management, fisheries, and law enforcement.
Resource Management, Fish and Wildlife S.b: Wildlife species, hunters, anglers, wildlife watchers, and the general public

Operation of the National Park System, N.d: Primary operating account for the National Park Service funding daily operations, maintenance, resource protection, visitor services, and law enforcement across 400+ national park units. NPS was established by the Organic Act of 1916.
Operation of the National Park System, N.b: Park visitors (300+ million annually), local gateway communities, cultural and natural heritage

NOAA Operations, Research, and Facilitie.d: Primary operating account for NOAA funding the National Weather Service, fisheries management, ocean and coastal research, satellite operations, and marine conservation. Supports weather forecasting, climate science, and marine stewardship.
NOAA Operations, Research, and Facilitie.b: General public (weather/climate services), commercial fishermen, mariners, coastal communities, aviation

Census Bureau Operations.d: Funds the Census Bureau’s periodic and current surveys, the decennial census, the American Community Survey, and economic censuses that provide essential data for congressional apportionment and federal fund allocation.
Census Bureau Operations.b: Congress, federal/state/local governments, businesses, researchers, and the public through demographic and economic data

Patent and Trademark Office.d: Almost entirely fee-funded agency that examines and grants patents and registers trademarks, protecting intellectual property rights and promoting innovation. Operates as a performance-based organization within the Department of Commerce.
Patent and Trademark Office.b: Inventors, businesses, trademark holders, and the innovation economy broadly

Bonneville Power Administration.d: Self-financing federal power marketing administration that markets wholesale electricity from 31 federal dams and one nuclear plant in the Pacific Northwest, and operates 15,000 miles of transmission lines. Created by the Bonneville Project Act of 1937.
Bonneville Power Administration.b: Pacific Northwest utilities, industrial customers, ratepayers; fish and wildlife through mitigation programs

Tennessee Valley Authority.d: Federally owned corporation providing electricity, flood control, navigation, and economic development in the Tennessee Valley region across seven states. Created as a New Deal agency; now self-financing through power sales, no longer receiving appropriations.
Tennessee Valley Authority.b: Approximately 10 million people in the Tennessee Valley region; local power companies; regional economy

Railroad Retirement Board Benefits.d: Pays retirement, survivor, disability, unemployment, and sickness benefits to railroad workers and their families under a system separate from Social Security. Funded by payroll taxes on railroad employers and employees.
Railroad Retirement Board Benefits.b: Retired and disabled railroad workers, their spouses, survivors, and unemployed/sick railroad workers

Foreign Military Financing, State.d: Provides grants and loans to foreign governments for purchase of U.S. defense equipment, services, and training to advance U.S. national security interests. The largest share historically goes to Israel and Egypt.
Foreign Military Financing, State.b: Allied and partner foreign governments; U.S. defense industry; U.S. national security interests

Economic Support Fund, State/USAID.d: Flexible foreign assistance account supporting U.S. economic and political interests abroad through budget support, development assistance, and stabilization programs in strategically important countries. Managed jointly by State and USAID.
Economic Support Fund, State/USAID.b: Foreign governments and populations in strategically important countries; U.S. foreign policy interests

USAID Operating Expenses.d: Administrative account funding USAID staff salaries, overseas mission operations, travel, and infrastructure needed to manage U.S. foreign development and humanitarian assistance programs worldwide.
USAID Operating Expenses.b: USAID workforce; indirectly, populations in developing countries receiving U.S. assistance

Export-Import Bank.d: Self-sustaining federal agency providing export credit insurance, loan guarantees, and direct loans to support U.S. exports when private financing is unavailable, countering foreign export credit competition. Operates at no net cost to taxpayers in most years.
Export-Import Bank.b: U.S. exporters (especially small businesses), their foreign buyers, and U.S. workers in export industries

SBA Disaster Loans.d: Provides low-interest, long-term federal disaster loans to homeowners, renters, businesses, and nonprofits to repair or replace disaster-damaged property. The primary federal disaster loan program and SBA’s largest program by dollar volume.
SBA Disaster Loans.b: Homeowners, renters, businesses, and nonprofits in presidentially declared disaster areas

Department of Health and Human Services.d: Cabinet-level department responsible for protecting the health of Americans and providing essential human services. Encompasses Medicare, Medicaid, NIH research, FDA, CDC, and social service programs.
Department of Health and Human Services.b: All Americans, particularly elderly, low-income, disabled, and children

Department of the Treasury.d: Manages federal finances including revenue collection (IRS), debt servicing, currency production, and economic policy. Handles interest payments on national debt, tax refunds, and financial system oversight.
Department of the Treasury.b: Federal government operations, taxpayers receiving refunds, holders of U.S. debt

Social Security Administration.d: Independent agency administering Social Security retirement, survivors, and disability insurance programs funded through payroll taxes. Provides monthly cash benefits to eligible retirees, disabled workers, and survivors of deceased workers.
Social Security Administration.b: Retired workers, disabled individuals, survivors and dependents of deceased or retired workers

Department of Defense.d: Cabinet-level department providing military forces to deter war and protect national security. Funds the Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, Space Force, and defense agencies including personnel, operations, procurement, and R&D.
Department of Defense.b: U.S. military servicemembers, national security infrastructure, defense contractors, allied nations

Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance.d: Trust fund that receives Social Security payroll tax revenues and pays retirement and survivors benefits. Holds dedicated receipts from FICA/SECA taxes and interest on Treasury securities to fund monthly benefit payments.
Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance.b: Retired workers aged 62+, spouses, children, and survivors of deceased insured workers

Interest on the Public Debt (Indefinite).d: Indefinite appropriation to pay interest owed on Treasury securities including bills, notes, bonds, and savings bonds held by the public and government accounts. This is the federal government’s cost of borrowing.
Interest on the Public Debt (Indefinite).b: Holders of U.S. Treasury securities including domestic and foreign investors, mutual funds, pension funds, and foreign governments

Grants to States for Medicaid, Centers f.d: Federal matching grants to states for the Medicaid program, providing health coverage to low-income individuals, families, pregnant women, elderly, and people with disabilities. The federal share (FMAP) varies by state.
Grants to States for Medicaid, Centers f.b: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled individuals enrolled in state Medicaid programs

Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance.d: Trust fund financing Medicare Part B (physician/outpatient services) and Part D (prescription drugs). Funded by beneficiary premiums (~25%) and general revenue transfers (~75%).
Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance.b: Medicare-eligible individuals aged 65+ and certain disabled persons receiving Part B and Part D benefits

Payments to Health Care Trust Funds, Cen.d: General fund transfers to the Medicare trust funds representing the government’s employer share and the general revenue subsidy for SMI (Parts B and D). These intragovernmental transfers supplement payroll tax and premium revenue.
Payments to Health Care Trust Funds, Cen.b: Medicare trust funds (HI and SMI) and indirectly all Medicare beneficiaries

Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund -.d: Trust fund financing Medicare Part A, which covers inpatient hospital care, skilled nursing facility care, hospice, and home health services. Primarily funded by the 2.9% HI payroll tax.
Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund -.b: Medicare Part A beneficiaries including elderly aged 65+ and eligible disabled individuals needing hospital and post-acute care

Department of Veterans Affairs.d: Cabinet-level department providing healthcare, disability compensation, education benefits, home loan guarantees, and burial services to military veterans. Operates the nation’s largest integrated healthcare system.
Department of Veterans Affairs.b: U.S. military veterans, their dependents, and survivors

Refunding Internal Revenue Collections (.d: Indefinite appropriation for IRS tax refunds to taxpayers who overpaid their federal income, employment, estate, or excise taxes. Covers individual and corporate refunds including refundable credit payments.
Refunding Internal Revenue Collections (.b: Individual and corporate taxpayers who overpaid federal taxes

Department of Agriculture.d: Cabinet-level department overseeing farming policy, food safety, nutrition assistance (SNAP, school meals), rural development, forestry, and agricultural research. Administers the nation’s largest nutrition safety-net programs.
Department of Agriculture.b: Farmers, ranchers, low-income households receiving food assistance, rural communities, consumers

Office of Personnel Management.d: Independent agency managing the federal civilian workforce, including the Civil Service Retirement System, Federal Employees Retirement System, and Federal Employees Health Benefits program. Most of its budget flows through retirement and health benefit trust funds.
Office of Personnel Management.b: Current and retired federal civilian employees and their families

Department of Education.d: Cabinet-level department administering federal student financial aid (Pell Grants, student loans), K-12 education grants (Title I), special education funding (IDEA), and education research. Does not operate schools directly.
Department of Education.b: K-12 students, college students, schools, teachers, state and local education agencies

Compensation and Pensions, Veterans Bene.d: Funds disability compensation for service-connected conditions, pensions for wartime veterans with limited income, and dependency and indemnity compensation (DIC) for survivors of veterans who died from service-related causes.
Compensation and Pensions, Veterans Bene.b: Disabled veterans, low-income wartime veterans, surviving spouses and dependents of deceased veterans

Federal Disability Insurance Trust Fund.d: Trust fund receiving dedicated payroll tax revenue to pay Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) benefits to workers who become severely disabled before retirement age, and to their eligible dependents.
Federal Disability Insurance Trust Fund.b: Disabled workers under full retirement age, their spouses, and dependent children

Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense.d: Revolving fund operating on a break-even basis to finance commercial-type activities within the Department of Defense such as depot maintenance, supply management, transportation, and industrial operations. Customers (military services) pay for goods and services received.
Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense.b: DoD components, military services, and defense agencies requiring logistics and support services

Department of Transportation.d: Cabinet-level department overseeing federal highway, aviation, rail, transit, maritime, and pipeline transportation programs. Funds infrastructure construction, safety regulation, and transportation research.
Department of Transportation.b: State and local transportation agencies, highway and transit users, airlines, railroads, the traveling public

Department of Homeland Security.d: Cabinet-level department consolidating border security (CBP), immigration enforcement (ICE), cybersecurity (CISA), disaster response (FEMA), transportation security (TSA), Coast Guard, and Secret Service. Created after the September 11, 2001 attacks.
Department of Homeland Security.b: General public, border communities, disaster-affected populations, critical infrastructure operators

Refundable Premium Assistance Tax Credit.d: Funds the Affordable Care Act’s premium tax credits (subsidies) for individuals and families purchasing health insurance through the Health Insurance Marketplace. The refundable portion is paid to insurers on behalf of enrollees.
