Refundable Premium Assistance Tax Credit.b: Individuals and families with incomes between 100-400% of the federal poverty level purchasing ACA marketplace health plans

Medicare Prescription Drug Account, Cent.d: Account within the SMI Trust Fund financing Medicare Part D prescription drug benefits, established by the Medicare Modernization Act of 2003. Pays subsidies to private prescription drug plans and provides the low-income subsidy.
Medicare Prescription Drug Account, Cent.b: Medicare beneficiaries enrolled in Part D prescription drug plans, particularly low-income enrollees receiving extra help

Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Progra.d: The nation’s largest domestic food assistance program (formerly Food Stamps), providing electronic benefit transfer (EBT) cards to low-income households to purchase food. Benefits are based on household size, income, and expenses.
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Progra.b: Low-income individuals and families meeting income and asset eligibility requirements

Civil Service Retirement and Disability.d: Trust fund paying retirement annuities, survivor benefits, and disability benefits to federal civilian employees and retirees under CSRS and FERS. Funded by employee and agency contributions plus general fund payments for the unfunded liability.
Civil Service Retirement and Disability.b: Retired federal civilian employees, disabled federal employees, and their survivors

Department of Housing and Urban Developm.d: Cabinet-level department administering federal housing programs including rental assistance (Section 8), public housing, FHA mortgage insurance, community development block grants, and homelessness assistance. Works to increase homeownership and affordable housing.
Department of Housing and Urban Developm.b: Low-income renters, homebuyers, public housing residents, homeless individuals, communities receiving development grants

Department of Energy.d: Cabinet-level department responsible for nuclear weapons stewardship (NNSA), energy research and development, national laboratories, nuclear waste cleanup, and energy policy. Also manages the Strategic Petroleum Reserve and Power Marketing Administrations.
Department of Energy.b: National security apparatus, energy researchers, national laboratory scientists, energy consumers, communities affected by nuclear legacy sites

Deposit Insurance Fund, Federal Deposit.d: Insurance fund protecting depositors at FDIC-insured banks up to $250,000 per depositor per institution. Funded by premiums assessed on insured banks, not taxpayer dollars, and used to resolve failed banks and pay insured depositors.
Deposit Insurance Fund, Federal Deposit.b: Depositors at FDIC-insured banks and savings institutions

Medical Services, Veterans Health Admini.d: Funds inpatient and outpatient medical care, mental health services, substance abuse treatment, and long-term care for eligible veterans through the VA healthcare system. Operates approximately 170 VA medical centers and 1,100+ outpatient clinics.
Medical Services, Veterans Health Admini.b: Enrolled veterans receiving healthcare through the VA medical system

Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense Ag.d: Revolving fund sub-account for defense agencies providing logistics, supply chain management, and support services on a reimbursable basis. Includes the Defense Logistics Agency and other centralized defense support activities.
Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense Ag.b: Defense agencies and military departments purchasing centralized support services

Employees’ Health Benefits Fund, Office.d: Trust fund for the Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) program, the largest employer-sponsored health insurance program in the U.S. Pools government and employee premium contributions to pay health insurance carriers for enrolled federal workers and retirees.
Employees’ Health Benefits Fund, Office.b: Active federal employees, retirees, and their dependents enrolled in FEHB health plans

Supplemental Security Income Program, So.d: Federally-funded means-tested program providing monthly cash payments to aged, blind, and disabled individuals with limited income and resources. Unlike Social Security, SSI is funded from general revenues, not payroll taxes.
Supplemental Security Income Program, So.b: Aged (65+), blind, and disabled individuals with limited income and resources, including children with disabilities

Department of Labor.d: Cabinet-level department overseeing workplace safety (OSHA), unemployment insurance, job training, wage and hour standards, workers’ compensation, and labor statistics. Administers federal unemployment trust fund and workforce development programs.
Department of Labor.b: Workers, unemployed individuals, job seekers, employers, labor unions

Operation and Maintenance, Defense-Wide,.d: Funds day-to-day operating costs for defense agencies and activities not assigned to a specific military service, including SOCOM, DISA, DARPA, missile defense, and joint operations. Covers civilian pay, supplies, equipment maintenance, and contracted services.
Operation and Maintenance, Defense-Wide,.b: Defense agencies, combatant commands, special operations forces, and defense-wide support organizations

Payment Where Earned Income Credit Excee.d: Funds the refundable portion of the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC), the outlay that occurs when the credit amount exceeds the taxpayer’s income tax liability. The EITC incentivizes and supplements earnings for low-to-moderate income working families.
Payment Where Earned Income Credit Excee.b: Low-to-moderate income working individuals and families, particularly those with qualifying children

Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Con.d: Provides cash to liquidate obligations incurred under the Federal-Aid Highway program’s contract authority, primarily from the Highway Trust Fund. Funds construction, reconstruction, and improvement of the National Highway System and other federal-aid roads.
Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Con.b: State departments of transportation, highway users, motorists, and communities benefiting from road infrastructure

Department of State.d: Cabinet-level department conducting U.S. foreign policy and diplomacy, operating embassies and consulates worldwide, issuing passports and visas, and administering foreign assistance programs. Also manages international organizations contributions.
Department of State.b: U.S. citizens abroad, foreign policy establishment, international partners, foreign aid recipients

Environmental Protection Agency.d: Independent agency protecting human health and the environment through regulation and enforcement of clean air, clean water, toxic substances, and waste management laws. Also funds Superfund cleanup, water infrastructure grants, and environmental research.
Environmental Protection Agency.b: General public, communities with pollution or contaminated sites, state environmental agencies, water and wastewater utilities

Payments to Social Security Trust Funds,.d: General fund transfers to the OASI and DI trust funds to reimburse for special payments such as gratuitous military wage credits, unnegotiated checks, and the taxation of Social Security benefits. Ensures trust funds are held harmless for policy decisions.
Payments to Social Security Trust Funds,.b: Social Security trust funds (OASI and DI), indirectly all Social Security beneficiaries

Payment to Civil Service Retirement and.d: Annual general fund transfer to the CSRDF to amortize the unfunded liability of the Civil Service Retirement System and cover the government’s share as employer. This intragovernmental payment ensures the retirement fund remains actuarially sound.
Payment to Civil Service Retirement and.b: Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund, indirectly retired and active federal civilian employees

Department of Justice.d: Cabinet-level department enforcing federal law and administering justice, including the FBI, DEA, ATF, U.S. Marshals, Bureau of Prisons, and U.S. Attorneys. Also oversees immigration courts, civil rights enforcement, and antitrust litigation.
Department of Justice.b: General public, law enforcement communities, federal inmates, crime victims, state and local justice agencies

Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.d: Funds Air Force research, technology development, prototyping, and testing of aircraft, weapons systems, space systems, and advanced technologies. Covers basic/applied research through operational system development and evaluation.
Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.b: U.S. Air Force, defense contractors, research institutions, national security

Department of Commerce.d: Cabinet-level department promoting economic growth through trade, technology, weather forecasting (NOAA), census, patent/trademark protection (USPTO), broadband deployment (NTIA), and standards (NIST). Recently received large broadband infrastructure funding.
Department of Commerce.b: Businesses, exporters, inventors, coastal communities, weather-dependent industries, broadband-underserved communities

Department of the Interior.d: Cabinet-level department managing federal lands, national parks, wildlife refuges, Bureau of Reclamation water projects, geological surveys (USGS), and programs for Native American and Alaska Native communities (BIA). Oversees approximately 500 million acres of federal land.
Department of the Interior.b: National park visitors, Native American tribes, western water users, wildlife, energy and mineral lessees on federal lands

General Services Administration.d: Independent agency managing federal real estate, procurement, and technology services. Operates the Federal Buildings Fund, provides government-wide acquisition contracts, manages the federal vehicle fleet, and runs the Technology Transformation Services.
General Services Administration.b: Federal agencies purchasing goods, services, office space, vehicles, and technology solutions

Student Financial Assistance, Education.d: Funds federal Pell Grants, Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grants (FSEOG), and Federal Work-Study programs for undergraduate students demonstrating financial need. Pell Grants are the largest component.
Student Financial Assistance, Education.b: Low-income undergraduate students attending colleges and universities

Defense Working Capital Fund, Navy.d: Revolving fund sub-account for Navy-managed working capital activities including naval shipyards, supply operations, transportation, and depot maintenance. Operates on a break-even cost-recovery basis from customer orders.
Defense Working Capital Fund, Navy.b: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps units purchasing maintenance, supply, and industrial services

Taxation on OASDI Benefits, HI, Centers.d: Transfers income taxes collected on Social Security benefits to the Medicare Hospital Insurance Trust Fund. Established by the 1993 legislation that directed revenue from taxing up to 85% of OASDI benefits to the HI trust fund.
Taxation on OASDI Benefits, HI, Centers.b: Medicare Hospital Insurance Trust Fund, indirectly Medicare Part A beneficiaries

Disaster Relief Fund, Federal Emergency.d: Primary fund for federal disaster response and recovery under the Stafford Act, providing individual assistance (temporary housing, grants), public assistance (infrastructure repair), and hazard mitigation grants following presidential disaster declarations.
Disaster Relief Fund, Federal Emergency.b: Disaster-affected individuals, households, state/local/tribal governments, and communities recovering from federally declared disasters

Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.d: Funds R&D for defense agencies not assigned to a specific military service, including DARPA, the Missile Defense Agency, DTRA, and other defense-wide technology programs. Covers advanced research, prototyping, and testing of cross-cutting defense capabilities.
Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.b: Defense agencies, DARPA researchers, defense contractors, national security innovation base

Small Business Administration.d: Independent agency providing loan guarantees, disaster loans, small business counseling, contracting assistance, and venture capital support to American small businesses. Also administers the Paycheck Protection Program and disaster lending.
Small Business Administration.b: Small business owners, entrepreneurs, disaster-affected businesses and homeowners

Child Nutrition Programs, Food and Nutri.d: Funds the National School Lunch Program, School Breakfast Program, Child and Adult Care Food Program, Summer Food Service Program, and Special Milk Program. Provides meals and snacks to children in schools, childcare, and summer programs.
Child Nutrition Programs, Food and Nutri.b: Children in schools and childcare facilities, particularly those from low-income families receiving free or reduced-price meals

Corps of Engineers - Civil Works.d: Funds the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers’ civil works mission including flood risk management, navigation (locks, dams, harbors), ecosystem restoration, hydropower, water supply, and recreation. Plans, designs, builds, and maintains water resources infrastructure.
Corps of Engineers - Civil Works.b: Flood-prone communities, inland waterway shippers, port users, hydropower consumers, recreational users of Corps projects

Tenant-Based Rental Assistance, Public a.d: Funds the Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program, the largest federal rental assistance program. Provides portable vouchers to low-income families to rent housing in the private market, paying the difference between 30% of income and fair market rent.
Tenant-Based Rental Assistance, Public a.b: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals using Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers

Federal Communications Commission.d: Independent regulatory commission overseeing interstate and international communications by radio, television, wire, satellite, and cable. Administers spectrum auctions, Universal Service Fund, broadband regulation, and media ownership rules.
Federal Communications Commission.b: Telecommunications consumers, broadcasters, wireless carriers, rural and low-income households receiving Universal Service subsidies

Medical Community Care, Veterans Health.d: Funds healthcare purchased from private-sector community providers for eligible veterans under the VA MISSION Act, when VA facilities cannot provide timely or geographically accessible care. Replaced the former Veterans Choice Program.
Medical Community Care, Veterans Health.b: Veterans receiving VA-authorized care from private-sector community healthcare providers

Acquisition Services Fund, General Servi.d: Revolving fund providing government-wide procurement, IT, and professional services to federal agencies on a fee-for-service basis. Includes the Federal Acquisition Service’s supply chain, fleet management, telecommunications, and assisted acquisition services.
Acquisition Services Fund, General Servi.b: Federal agencies purchasing goods, IT solutions, fleet vehicles, and acquisition support through GSA

Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.d: Funds Navy and Marine Corps research, development, testing, and evaluation of ships, submarines, aircraft, weapons, electronics, and marine technologies. Includes the Office of Naval Research and Naval Research Laboratory programs.
Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.b: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps, naval research laboratories, defense contractors, maritime security

Defense Working Capital Fund, Air Force.d: Revolving fund sub-account for Air Force working capital activities including depot maintenance (air logistics complexes), supply management, and transportation. Operates on a cost-recovery basis from Air Force and other DoD customer orders.
Defense Working Capital Fund, Air Force.b: U.S. Air Force units purchasing depot maintenance, spare parts, and logistics services

National Aeronautics and Space Administr.d: Independent agency conducting space exploration, aeronautics research, Earth science, and technology development. Operates human spaceflight programs (ISS, Artemis), robotic missions, and maintains research centers across the country.
National Aeronautics and Space Administr.b: Scientific community, astronauts, aerospace industry, general public benefiting from space technology spinoffs

Payment Where Child Tax Credit Exceeds L.d: Funds the refundable portion (Additional Child Tax Credit) of the Child Tax Credit, where the credit exceeds the taxpayer’s income tax liability and the excess is paid as a refund. Supports families with qualifying children.
Payment Where Child Tax Credit Exceeds L.b: Low-to-moderate income families with qualifying children under age 17

Agency for International Development.d: Independent agency (USAID) administering U.S. foreign economic and humanitarian assistance to developing countries. Funds programs in global health, food security, democracy promotion, economic growth, education, and disaster relief worldwide.
Agency for International Development.b: Developing country populations, NGOs implementing USAID programs, partner governments, humanitarian crisis-affected populations

Universal Service Fund, Federal Communic.d: Fund administered by USAC providing subsidies for telecommunications and broadband in high-cost/rural areas, low-income households (Lifeline), schools and libraries (E-Rate), and rural healthcare facilities. Funded by assessments on telecom carriers.
Universal Service Fund, Federal Communic.b: Rural telecommunications users, low-income households, schools, libraries, rural healthcare providers

Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment.d: Grant program established by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (2021) to deploy broadband infrastructure to unserved and underserved areas. Distributes formula-based grants to states, territories, and DC for broadband expansion.
Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment.b: Unserved and underserved communities lacking reliable high-speed internet, state broadband offices

Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Sec.d: Funds the stewardship, maintenance, and modernization of the U.S. nuclear weapons stockpile without underground testing. Covers warhead life extension programs, stockpile surveillance, weapons dismantlement, and the nuclear weapons production complex.
Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Sec.b: NNSA weapons laboratories (Los Alamos, Sandia, Lawrence Livermore), nuclear weapons production plants, national security

Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund,.d: Funds the federal crop insurance program that subsidizes premiums and reimburses private insurers for crop and revenue losses. Provides risk management tools to farmers for losses due to weather, disease, price declines, and other perils.
Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund,.b: Farmers and ranchers purchasing federally subsidized crop insurance, approved insurance providers (AIPs)

Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation.d: Federal corporation insuring private-sector defined benefit pension plans. When a company’s pension plan fails, PBGC pays guaranteed benefits to retirees and workers up to statutory limits. Funded by premiums from insured plans, not taxpayer dollars.
Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation.b: Workers and retirees in failed private-sector defined benefit pension plans

Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fun.d: The operating fund of PBGC that receives premiums and pays benefits. Same entity as #89 but shown as the specific fund account within the Department of Labor organizational structure.
Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fun.b: Workers and retirees in failed private-sector defined benefit pension plans

Operations and Support, U.S. Customs and.d: Funds CBP’s primary mission of securing U.S. borders at and between ports of entry, including Border Patrol agents, customs officers, agricultural inspectors, and trade enforcement. Covers personnel compensation, technology, and operational costs.
Operations and Support, U.S. Customs and.b: U.S. border communities, legitimate travelers and traders, CBP personnel

State Children’s Health Insurance Fund,.d: Funds the Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP), providing federal matching funds to states for health coverage of children in families with incomes too high for Medicaid but too low to afford private insurance. Some states also cover pregnant women.
State Children’s Health Insurance Fund,.b: Uninsured children in low-to-moderate income families, and in some states, pregnant women

Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.d: Funds R&D for Space Force capabilities including space launch, satellite systems, space domain awareness, and space-based missile warning. Established when the Space Force was created as a separate service branch in 2019.
Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.b: U.S. Space Force, space industry contractors, national security space operations

Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to t.d: Funds the Treasury’s contribution for concurrent receipt of military retirement pay and VA disability compensation (Combat-Related Special Compensation and Concurrent Retirement and Disability Pay). Eliminated the offset that previously reduced military retired pay dollar-for-dollar for VA disability payments.
Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to t.b: Military retirees with service-connected disabilities who receive both military retirement pay and VA disability compensation

Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund, Depart.d: Mandatory funding established by the PACT Act of 2022 to cover healthcare and benefits costs for veterans exposed to toxic substances (burn pits, Agent Orange, radiation) during military service. Ensures expanded eligibility does not strain VA’s discretionary budget.
Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund, Depart.b: Veterans exposed to burn pits, Agent Orange, radiation, and other toxic substances during military service

Railroad Retirement Board.d: Independent agency administering retirement, survivor, disability, unemployment, and sickness benefits for railroad workers and their families. Functions as a separate Social Security-like system for the railroad industry, funded by rail employer and employee payroll taxes.
Railroad Retirement Board.b: Retired, disabled, and unemployed railroad workers and their spouses, survivors, and dependents

Temporary Assistance for Needy Families,.d: Block grant to states providing cash assistance, work support, childcare, and other services to low-income families with children. Replaced the former Aid to Families with Dependent Children (AFDC) program under the 1996 welfare reform law, with work requirements and time limits.
Temporary Assistance for Needy Families,.b: Low-income families with children, particularly single-parent households meeting work requirements

Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.d: Funds Army research, development, testing, and evaluation of ground combat vehicles, weapons, soldier systems, aviation, missile defense, and communications technologies. Includes Army Research Laboratory, DEVCOM, and major weapons program development.
Research, Development, Test, and Evaluat.b: U.S. Army, Army research laboratories, defense contractors, soldiers benefiting from improved equipment

Project-Based Rental Assistance, Housing.d: Funds Section 8 project-based rental assistance contracts with private landlords who set aside units for low-income tenants. Unlike tenant-based vouchers, the subsidy is attached to the specific housing unit, not the tenant.
Project-Based Rental Assistance, Housing.b: Low-income tenants in privately owned subsidized housing developments with project-based Section 8 contracts

Special Education, Education.d: Funds grants to states under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) to support special education and related services for children with disabilities ages 3-21. Includes Part B state grants and Part C early intervention for infants and toddlers.
Special Education, Education.b: Children and youth with disabilities, state and local education agencies, early intervention programs

Children and Families Services Programs,.d: Funds Head Start/Early Head Start, child welfare services, child abuse prevention, runaway and homeless youth programs, and community services. Head Start is the largest component, providing early childhood education to low-income children.
Children and Families Services Programs,.b: Low-income preschool children (Head Start), at-risk youth, families receiving child welfare and community services

Government Payment for Annuitants, Emplo.d: Funds the government’s (employer) share of health insurance premiums for federal retiree annuitants enrolled in the Federal Employees Health Benefits program. This payment parallels the employer contribution made for active employees.
Government Payment for Annuitants, Emplo.b: Retired federal civilian employees and their dependents enrolled in FEHB health insurance

Formula and Bus Grants, Liquidation of C.d: Provides cash to liquidate obligations for formula-based transit capital and operating grants to states and local transit agencies from the Mass Transit Account of the Highway Trust Fund. Funds bus and rail transit capital investments, preventive maintenance, and operations in urbanized and rural areas.
Formula and Bus Grants, Liquidation of C.b: Public transit agencies, bus and rail riders, urbanized and rural communities with public transportation systems

Salaries and Expenses, Social Security A.d: Administrative budget of SSA covering personnel costs, IT systems, field office operations, and administrative expenses for processing Social Security and SSI claims, issuing Social Security numbers, and conducting disability determinations.
Salaries and Expenses, Social Security A.b: SSA employees, indirectly all Social Security and SSI applicants and beneficiaries served by SSA operations

Readjustment Benefits, Veterans Benefits.d: Funds education and training benefits for veterans including the Post-9/11 GI Bill, Montgomery GI Bill, Vocational Rehabilitation and Employment, and other readjustment programs helping veterans transition to civilian life. Covers tuition, housing allowances, and book stipends.
Readjustment Benefits, Veterans Benefits.b: Post-service veterans pursuing education or training, service members transitioning to civilian employment

Defense Working Capital Fund, Army.d: Revolving fund sub-account for Army working capital activities including depot maintenance (arsenals and depots), supply management, ordnance, and industrial operations. Operates on a cost-recovery basis from Army and other DoD customers.
Defense Working Capital Fund, Army.b: U.S. Army units purchasing depot maintenance, supply, and ammunition services

Operations, Federal Aviation Administrat.d: Funds FAA’s air traffic control system operations, including controller salaries, facility maintenance, flight inspection, and aviation safety oversight. This is FAA’s largest account, keeping the national airspace system running 24/7.
Operations, Federal Aviation Administrat.b: Airlines, pilots, air travelers, the national airspace system, FAA air traffic controllers and safety inspectors

Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers, Civi.d: Revolving fund for the Corps of Engineers’ reimbursable civil works activities including work for other federal agencies, state/local governments, and foreign governments. Recovers costs from customers for engineering, construction, and environmental services.
Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers, Civi.b: Federal, state, and local government agencies and foreign entities purchasing Corps engineering and construction services

Department of Defense, Medicare-Eligible.d: Trust fund financing TRICARE for Life benefits for Medicare-eligible military retirees and their dependents. Established by the National Defense Authorization Act for FY2001 to pre-fund the accruing health benefit liability for future retirees.
Department of Defense, Medicare-Eligible.b: Medicare-eligible military retirees aged 65+ and their dependents enrolled in TRICARE for Life

Operations, Airport and Airway Trust Fun.d: FAA operations funded from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund (financed by aviation excise taxes on tickets, fuel, and cargo). Covers air traffic control and aviation safety activities supported by dedicated aviation user fees and taxes.
Operations, Airport and Airway Trust Fun.b: Airlines, general aviation, air travelers, the aviation system funded by aviation taxes

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Bureau of.d: Funds FBI operations including counterterrorism, counterintelligence, cybercrime, criminal investigations, and laboratory services. Covers agent and support personnel salaries, equipment, travel, and operational costs for the nation’s primary federal law enforcement agency.
Salaries and Expenses, Federal Bureau of.b: FBI agents and staff, federal law enforcement operations, the general public protected by FBI investigations

Diplomatic and Consular Programs, State.d: Primary operating account for the State Department, funding salaries and operations at embassies and consulates worldwide, diplomatic security, passport and visa services, and policy formulation. Supports approximately 275 diplomatic posts globally.
Diplomatic and Consular Programs, State.b: U.S. diplomats, Foreign Service officers, U.S. citizens abroad, visa applicants

Global Health Programs, State.d: Funds U.S. global health initiatives managed by the State Department, primarily PEPFAR (President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief), as well as programs addressing malaria, tuberculosis, maternal and child health, and global health security in developing countries.
Global Health Programs, State.b: Populations in developing countries affected by HIV/AIDS, malaria, TB, and other health challenges

Medical Support and Compliance, Veterans.d: Funds VHA’s administrative, management, and support functions including financial management, human resources, IT, compliance oversight, and other infrastructure supporting the delivery of veteran healthcare services.
Medical Support and Compliance, Veterans.b: VA healthcare system administration, indirectly veterans receiving VA medical care

Operations and Support, Coast Guard, Hom.d: Funds Coast Guard operating expenses including maritime law enforcement, search and rescue, aids to navigation, marine safety, port security, and drug/migrant interdiction. Covers active-duty military and civilian personnel, vessel and aircraft operations.
Operations and Support, Coast Guard, Hom.b: Maritime travelers, boaters, coastal communities, port facilities, Coast Guard personnel

Operations and Support, Transportation S.d: Funds TSA’s airport security screening operations including passenger and baggage screening, Federal Air Marshal Service, vetting programs, and surface transportation security. Covers transportation security officers (screeners) at approximately 440 airports.
Operations and Support, Transportation S.b: Air travelers, airlines, airports, the traveling public

Community Development Fund, Community Pl.d: Funds the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) program providing flexible formula grants to states, cities, and counties for housing, infrastructure, economic development, and public services benefiting low-and-moderate income communities.
Community Development Fund, Community Pl.b: Low-and-moderate income communities, local governments, neighborhood organizations undertaking community development

Risk Adjustment Program Payments, Center.d: ACA risk adjustment program that transfers funds from health insurance plans with lower-risk enrollees to plans with higher-risk enrollees in the individual and small group markets. Designed to stabilize premiums and reduce incentives for insurers to avoid sick enrollees.
Risk Adjustment Program Payments, Center.b: Health insurance issuers in ACA marketplaces, indirectly consumers in individual and small group health insurance markets

Commodity Credit Corporation Fund, Agric.d: Government-owned corporation fund providing price and income support to agricultural producers through loans, purchases, and payments. Also funds conservation programs, export promotion, and commodity donations for food assistance. Has permanent borrowing authority from the Treasury.
Commodity Credit Corporation Fund, Agric.b: Farmers and ranchers receiving commodity price supports, conservation payments, and export assistance

Credit Reform: Interest Paid on Uninvest.d: Indefinite appropriation under the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990 covering interest costs on Treasury funds that have been disbursed for federal credit programs (loans and loan guarantees) but not yet offset by collections. Represents the government’s borrowing cost for credit program cash flows.
Credit Reform: Interest Paid on Uninvest.b: Federal credit program accounts (student loans, housing, SBA, etc.) requiring interest subsidy payments from Treasury

General Operating Expenses, Veterans Ben.d: Administrative account funding VBA’s processing of disability compensation, pension, education, home loan, insurance, and burial benefit claims. Covers staffing, IT systems, and operational costs for adjudicating millions of veteran benefit claims annually.
General Operating Expenses, Veterans Ben.b: VBA employees, indirectly all veterans and dependents applying for VA benefits

Payments for Foster Care and Permanency,.d: Federal matching payments to states for foster care maintenance (Title IV-E), adoption assistance, and kinship guardianship assistance. Reimburses states for room, board, and care of eligible children placed in foster family homes or childcare institutions.
Payments for Foster Care and Permanency,.b: Children in foster care, adoptive families, kinship guardians, state child welfare agencies

Operations and Support, U.S. Immigration.d: Funds ICE’s immigration enforcement operations including detention and removal of undocumented immigrants, Homeland Security Investigations (transnational crime, human trafficking, cybercrime), and student/exchange visitor program oversight.
Operations and Support, U.S. Immigration.b: ICE officers and agents, immigration enforcement operations, communities affected by transnational crime

National Science Foundation.d: Independent agency funding fundamental research and education across all non-medical fields of science and engineering. Awards grants to universities and research institutions, supports STEM education, and funds major research facilities and infrastructure.
National Science Foundation.b: University researchers, graduate students, STEM educators, scientific community across all non-medical disciplines

Exchange Stabilization Fund, Office of t.d: Fund managed by the Treasury Secretary to intervene in foreign exchange markets to stabilize the dollar’s value, provide emergency loans or guarantees, and address international financial crises. Used notably to guarantee money market funds in 2008 and backstop pandemic lending facilities.
Exchange Stabilization Fund, Office of t.b: U.S. and global financial system stability, the dollar’s international standing

Payment to Department of Defense Medicar.d: Annual accrual payment from the general fund (Treasury) and DoD to the Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, representing the actuarially determined normal cost of future TRICARE for Life benefits being earned by current active-duty and reserve members.
Payment to Department of Defense Medicar.b: DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, indirectly future military retirees who will use TRICARE for Life

Expenses, Medical Facilities, Veterans H.d: Funds the operation, maintenance, and minor construction of VA medical facilities including hospitals, clinics, nursing homes, and domiciliaries. Covers utilities, engineering, facility repair, leases, and capital equipment for VA’s healthcare infrastructure.
Expenses, Medical Facilities, Veterans H.b: VA medical facilities, indirectly all veterans receiving care at VA healthcare facilities

Science, Energy Programs, Energy.d: Funds the Department of Energy’s Office of Science, supporting basic research in physical sciences, high-energy physics, nuclear physics, biological and environmental research, advanced scientific computing, and operation of national laboratories.
Science, Energy Programs, Energy.b: National laboratories (Argonne, Brookhaven, Fermilab, Oak Ridge, etc.), university researchers, scientific workforce

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison Sy.d: Covers the operating costs of the Federal Bureau of Prisons, including staff salaries, inmate care, food, medical services, facility maintenance, and contract confinement costs. The BOP was established by an act of Congress in 1930.
Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison Sy.b: Federal inmates (~155,000), BOP staff (~36,000), contract facility operators

Payment to States for the Child Care and.d: Provides federal block grant funding to states, territories, and tribes to subsidize child care for low-income working families and to improve child care quality. Authorized by the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990.
Payment to States for the Child Care and.b: Low-income working families with children under 13, child care providers, state child care agencies

Social Security Equivalent Benefit Accou.d: Pays the portion of railroad retirement benefits equivalent to Social Security benefits that railroad workers would have received had their employment been covered by Social Security. Restructured under the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974.
Social Security Equivalent Benefit Accou.b: Retired railroad workers and their spouses, survivors of deceased railroad employees

Refugee and Entrant Assistance, Administ.d: Provides resettlement assistance to refugees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, asylees, and unaccompanied alien children including cash assistance, medical screening, social services, and shelter. Authorized by the Refugee Act of 1980.
Refugee and Entrant Assistance, Administ.b: Refugees admitted to the U.S., asylees, unaccompanied migrant children, Cuban/Haitian entrants, voluntary resettlement agencies

Public Housing Fund, Public and Indian H.d: Provides operating subsidies and capital funds to approximately 3,000 public housing authorities nationwide for the maintenance, operation, and modernization of roughly 1 million public housing units. Traces origins to the Housing Act of 1937.
Public Housing Fund, Public and Indian H.b: Low-income tenants in public housing, public housing authorities, elderly and disabled residents

Indian Health Services, Indian Health Se.d: Funds health care delivery to approximately 2.6 million American Indians and Alaska Natives through a network of hospitals, clinics, and health stations operated by IHS, tribes, and urban Indian organizations. Established under the Indian Health Care Improvement Act.
Indian Health Services, Indian Health Se.b: Members of federally recognized tribes, Alaska Natives, urban Indian populations

Economic Support Fund, Funds Appropriate.d: Provides economic assistance to foreign countries and regions of strategic importance to U.S. interests, supporting governance, economic reform, health, education, and stabilization programs. Authorized under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961.
Economic Support Fund, Funds Appropriate.b: Partner governments (Israel, Egypt, Jordan, etc.), NGOs, populations in developing and conflict-affected countries

Highway Infrastructure Programs, Federal.d: Provides funding for highway and bridge infrastructure projects beyond the core Federal-Aid Highway Program, including competitive grants for major projects and nationally significant freight and highway projects. Expanded significantly under IIJA (2021).
Highway Infrastructure Programs, Federal.b: State departments of transportation, metropolitan planning organizations, motorists, freight carriers

Construction, Corps of Engineers - Civil.d: Funds construction of new water resources projects by the Army Corps of Engineers including navigation, flood and storm damage reduction, aquatic ecosystem restoration, and water supply projects. The Corps’ civil works mission dates to 1824.
Construction, Corps of Engineers - Civil.b: Communities in flood-prone areas, commercial navigation interests, coastal populations, environmental habitats

Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy,.d: Funds DOE’s Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE), supporting R&D and deployment of solar, wind, geothermal, hydrogen, bioenergy, vehicle, building, and manufacturing energy technologies.
Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy,.b: Clean energy companies, manufacturers, homeowners, vehicle fleet operators, national laboratories, university researchers

Special Supplemental Nutrition Program f.d: Provides supplemental food packages, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and health care referrals to low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, infants, and children up to age 5 who are at nutritional risk.
Special Supplemental Nutrition Program f.b: Low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, children under 5, WIC-authorized food retailers

Exploration, National Aeronautics and Sp.d: Funds NASA’s human exploration programs including the Artemis lunar program, Space Launch System, Orion spacecraft, Gateway lunar space station, and human Mars exploration planning.
Exploration, National Aeronautics and Sp.b: NASA centers, aerospace contractors (Boeing, Lockheed Martin, SpaceX, etc.), astronaut corps, scientific community

International Disaster Assistance, Funds.d: Provides humanitarian assistance for international disaster relief including emergency food, shelter, water, sanitation, and health services in response to natural disasters and complex emergencies worldwide.
International Disaster Assistance, Funds.b: Populations affected by natural disasters and conflicts worldwide, international humanitarian organizations (UNHCR, WFP, ICRC), NGOs

National Sea-Based Deterrence Fund, Navy.d: Finances the Columbia-class ballistic missile submarine (SSBN) program, the replacement for Ohio-class submarines that carry the sea-based leg of the U.S. nuclear triad. Created by the National Defense Authorization Act for FY2015.
National Sea-Based Deterrence Fund, Navy.b: General Dynamics Electric Boat, BWX Technologies, Navy submarine fleet, nuclear deterrence infrastructure

Defense Environmental Cleanup, Environme.d: Funds cleanup of radioactive and hazardous waste contamination at former nuclear weapons production and research sites across the DOE complex, including Hanford, Savannah River, and Idaho National Laboratory.
Defense Environmental Cleanup, Environme.b: Communities near former nuclear weapons sites, environmental remediation contractors, cleanup workers, downwind/downstream populations

National Cancer Institute, National Inst.d: The largest institute within NIH, funding cancer research including basic, translational, and clinical studies on cancer prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. Established by the National Cancer Institute Act of 1937.
National Cancer Institute, National Inst.b: Cancer researchers at universities and medical centers, cancer patients, NCI-designated cancer centers (~71), pharmaceutical/biotech companies

Public Health and Social Services Emerge.d: Provides funding for public health emergency preparedness and response, including BARDA (Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority), pandemic preparedness, medical countermeasure development, and the Strategic National Stockpile.
Public Health and Social Services Emerge.b: State and local health departments, hospitals, pharmaceutical manufacturers, the general public during health emergencies

Program Management, Centers for Medicare.d: Covers the administrative costs of running CMS, including Medicare claims processing, program integrity and fraud prevention, survey and certification of health care providers, research, and IT systems including Healthcare.gov.
Program Management, Centers for Medicare.b: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries (indirectly), CMS employees, health care providers, Medicare Administrative Contractors

Science, National Aeronautics and Space.d: Funds NASA’s Science Mission Directorate covering Earth science, planetary science, astrophysics, heliophysics, and biological/physical sciences, including flagship missions like the James Webb Space Telescope, Mars rovers, and Earth-observing satellites.
Science, National Aeronautics and Space.b: University and NASA scientists, Jet Propulsion Laboratory, aerospace contractors, the global scientific community

Operations, Research and Facilities, Nat.d: Funds NOAA’s core operations including the National Weather Service, satellite systems, ocean and coastal research, fisheries management, marine sanctuaries, and climate research. NOAA was established in 1970.
Operations, Research and Facilities, Nat.b: General public (weather forecasts), fishing industry, coastal communities, aviation/maritime sectors, climate researchers

Grants to States for Medicaid, Centers f.d: This is the administrative/survey and certification component of Medicaid grants to states, separate from the much larger mandatory benefit payments. It supports state Medicaid program administration, eligibility determination, and provider oversight.
Grants to States for Medicaid, Centers f.b: State Medicaid agencies, low-income Medicaid enrollees (indirectly), health care providers participating in Medicaid

Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fun.d: Operates as a federal corporation that insures defined-benefit pension plans in the private sector, paying benefits to retirees when their employer pension plans fail. Created by ERISA (Employee Retirement Income Security Act) of 1974.
Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fun.b: Workers and retirees whose defined-benefit pension plans have been terminated, companies with underfunded pension plans

Procurement of Weapons and Tracked Comba.d: Funds the Army’s procurement of tanks (M1 Abrams upgrades), Bradley Fighting Vehicles, Stryker combat vehicles, artillery systems, small arms, ammunition, and other weapons systems and combat vehicle modifications.
Procurement of Weapons and Tracked Comba.b: Defense contractors (General Dynamics, BAE Systems, Oshkosh Defense), Army combat units, Army depots

Salaries and Expenses, Food and Drug Adm.d: Funds FDA operations including regulation of food safety, drug and biologic approvals, medical device oversight, tobacco regulation, and cosmetics safety. The FDA traces its regulatory origins to the Pure Food and Drug Act of 1906.
Salaries and Expenses, Food and Drug Adm.b: Consumers (food and drug safety), pharmaceutical companies (regulatory pathway), medical device manufacturers, food industry

Refunds and Drawbacks, United States Cus.d: Provides for refunds of customs duties, taxes, and fees that were overpaid, collected in error, or eligible for drawback (refund of duties on imported goods that are subsequently exported). An indefinite appropriation tied to customs law since the founding era.
Refunds and Drawbacks, United States Cus.b: Importers and exporters, customs brokers, manufacturers who re-export imported materials

Information Technology Systems, Departme.d: Funds the VA’s Office of Information and Technology for enterprise-wide IT systems including the electronic health record modernization (Oracle Cerner), VistA, cybersecurity, telehealth platforms, and benefits processing systems.
Information Technology Systems, Departme.b: Veterans (through improved service delivery), VA employees, IT contractors (Oracle/Cerner, etc.)

National Institute of Allergy and Infect.d: Funds research on infectious, immunologic, and allergic diseases including HIV/AIDS, influenza, emerging infectious diseases, biodefense, and autoimmune conditions. NIAID conducts and supports both basic and clinical research.
National Institute of Allergy and Infect.b: Biomedical researchers, patients with infectious and immune-mediated diseases, vaccine and therapeutic developers

Farm Security and Rural Investment Progr.d: Funds NRCS conservation programs authorized under successive farm bills, including EQIP (Environmental Quality Incentives Program), CSP (Conservation Stewardship Program), and ACEP (Agricultural Conservation Easement Program).
Farm Security and Rural Investment Progr.b: Farmers and ranchers implementing conservation practices, private forest landowners, tribal agricultural producers

Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense Co.d: Operates the Defense Commissary Agency which runs approximately 236 commissary stores worldwide, providing groceries and household goods to military members, retirees, and their families at cost plus a 5% surcharge.
Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense Co.b: Active-duty military, military retirees, military families, reserve/guard members, commissary employees

Operations Support, Internal Revenue Ser.d: Funds IRS operational support functions including IT infrastructure, telecommunications, rent and facilities, printing, postage, security, and shared services. Established as a separate account in the IRS restructuring of appropriation accounts.
Operations Support, Internal Revenue Ser.b: IRS employees, U.S. taxpayers (through improved tax administration), IT service providers

Federal-State Partnership for Intercity.d: Provides competitive grants for capital projects to reduce the state of good repair backlog, improve performance, and expand intercity passenger rail service, particularly on the Northeast Corridor. Created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) of 2021.
Federal-State Partnership for Intercity.b: Amtrak, state transportation agencies, commuter rail operators, intercity rail passengers, NEC communities

Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance.d: A Treasury-managed component of the Medicare Part B and Part D trust fund that handles interest payments, transfers, and certain administrative operations related to the Supplementary Medical Insurance program established by Medicare in 1965.
Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance.b: Medicare Part B and Part D beneficiaries, health care providers, prescription drug plan sponsors

Primary Health Care, Health Resources an.d: Funds the Health Center Program (Section 330 grants) supporting approximately 1,400 community health centers that provide primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services to underserved communities regardless of ability to pay.
Primary Health Care, Health Resources an.b: Uninsured and underinsured patients, Medicaid enrollees, community health centers (FQHCs), rural and urban underserved populations

Operation and Maintenance, Corps of Engi.d: Funds the ongoing operation and maintenance of existing Army Corps civil works infrastructure including locks, dams, levees, harbors, waterways, hydropower plants, recreation areas, and regulatory permits programs.
Operation and Maintenance, Corps of Engi.b: Inland waterway navigation industry, communities protected by flood control infrastructure, hydropower consumers, recreation users

Operation and Maintenance, Corps of Engi.d: Duplicate entry for the Corps of Engineers O&M account (slightly different naming convention). Supports the same dredging, lock operations, dam safety, levee maintenance, and environmental stewardship activities.
Operation and Maintenance, Corps of Engi.b: Inland waterway navigation industry, communities protected by flood infrastructure, hydropower consumers

Working Capital Fund, State.d: A revolving fund that provides centralized administrative services to the State Department and other agencies on a reimbursable basis, including IT, logistics, procurement, financial services, and overseas building operations support.
Working Capital Fund, State.b: State Department bureaus and posts, other federal agencies using State Department shared services

Railroad Retirement Account, Railroad Re.d: Pays the industry-funded portion of railroad retirement benefits (Tier II) above the Social Security-equivalent amount, plus supplemental annuities, spouse/survivor benefits, and unemployment/sickness insurance for railroad workers.
Railroad Retirement Account, Railroad Re.b: Retired railroad workers, their spouses and survivors, disabled railroad employees

Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service.d: Funds the U.S. Forest Service’s wildfire suppression, fuels management (prescribed burns, mechanical treatments), preparedness, and post-fire rehabilitation activities across 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands.
Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service.b: Communities in the wildland-urban interface, firefighters, national forest ecosystems, state and local fire agencies

Immigration Examination Fees, Immigratio.d: A fee-funded account supporting U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) operations including processing of immigration benefit applications such as naturalization, green cards, work permits, asylum, and refugee status adjustments.
Immigration Examination Fees, Immigratio.b: Immigration applicants (visa, green card, citizenship), employers sponsoring workers, USCIS employees

Community Development Fund, Community Pl.d: Primarily funds the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) program, providing formula grants to states and local governments for housing, infrastructure, economic development, and public services in low- and moderate-income communities. Authorized by the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974.
Community Development Fund, Community Pl.b: Low- and moderate-income communities, local governments, state agencies, community development organizations

Migration and Refugee Assistance, State.d: Funds U.S. contributions to international refugee protection and assistance through UNHCR, the International Committee of the Red Cross, the International Organization for Migration, and other partners providing protection and durable solutions for refugees worldwide.
Migration and Refugee Assistance, State.b: Refugees worldwide, UNHCR, ICRC, IOM, international and local NGOs providing refugee services

Operation and Maintenance, Space Force.d: Funds the day-to-day operations of the U.S. Space Force including satellite operations, space domain awareness, launch operations, cyber operations, facility sustainment, and civilian personnel. The Space Force was established in December 2019.
Operation and Maintenance, Space Force.b: Space Force guardians and civilian employees, space operations centers, satellite ground stations, all military services dependent on space capabilities

Refunding Internal Revenue Collections,.d: An indefinite/permanent appropriation covering interest payments the government owes to taxpayers on overpaid taxes that were refunded after a statutory period, as required by the Internal Revenue Code.
Refunding Internal Revenue Collections,.b: U.S. taxpayers receiving refunds with interest, primarily corporate taxpayers with large overpayments

Affordable Connectivity Fund, Federal Co.d: Provided $30/month broadband subsidies (up to $75 on tribal lands) to eligible low-income households to help afford internet service. Created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (2021); funding was largely exhausted by mid-2024.
Affordable Connectivity Fund, Federal Co.b: Low-income households, households on tribal lands, participating internet service providers

Payments to States for Child Support Enf.d: Provides federal matching funds to states for operating child support enforcement programs that locate absent parents, establish paternity, establish and enforce child support orders, and collect child support payments. Created under Title IV-D of the Social Security Act.
Payments to States for Child Support Enf.b: Custodial parents and children owed support, state child support enforcement agencies, non-custodial parents (through income withholding systems)

Federal Assistance, Federal Emergency Ma.d: Funds FEMA’s non-disaster grant programs including the State Homeland Security Grant Program, Urban Area Security Initiative, port and transit security grants, Emergency Management Performance Grants, and firefighter assistance grants.
Federal Assistance, Federal Emergency Ma.b: State and local emergency management agencies, fire departments, law enforcement, port authorities, transit agencies

Enforcement, Internal Revenue Service, T.d: Funds IRS enforcement activities including audits, investigations, tax litigation, criminal enforcement, document matching, and collection of delinquent taxes. Separated into its own account as part of IRS appropriation restructuring.
Enforcement, Internal Revenue Service, T.b: U.S. taxpayers (through improved compliance), the federal government (through increased revenue collection)

Consular and Border Security Programs, A.d: Funds the State Department’s consular operations including visa adjudication, passport issuance, American citizen services abroad, and border security screening programs. Largely fee-funded through passport and visa fees.
Consular and Border Security Programs, A.b: U.S. passport applicants, foreign visa applicants, U.S. citizens abroad requiring emergency services

Safety, Security and Mission Services, N.d: Funds NASA’s institutional operations including center management, safety and mission assurance, protective services, IT infrastructure, environmental compliance, and agency-wide mission support services across NASA’s facilities.
Safety, Security and Mission Services, N.b: NASA centers and employees, NASA contractors providing institutional support services

Electricity, Energy Programs, Energy.d: Funds DOE’s Office of Electricity, supporting research and development on grid modernization, energy storage, transmission infrastructure, cybersecurity for the electric grid, and transformer reserves. Significantly expanded under IIJA.
Electricity, Energy Programs, Energy.b: Electric utilities, grid operators, energy storage companies, electricity consumers, rural electric cooperatives

Processing, Research and Marketing, Offi.d: Funds USDA activities related to agricultural commodity processing, marketing services, grading and inspection, agricultural research coordination, and market access programs that support American agricultural producers.
Processing, Research and Marketing, Offi.b: Agricultural producers, food processors, exporters of agricultural commodities, consumers

National Institute on Aging, National In.d: Funds research on aging processes, age-related diseases including Alzheimer’s disease and related dementias, and the biological, social, and behavioral aspects of aging. Established by the Research on Aging Act of 1974.
National Institute on Aging, National In.b: Older Americans, Alzheimer’s disease patients and caregivers, aging researchers, Alzheimer’s Disease Research Centers

Postal Service Contributions for Retiree.d: Covers the federal government’s required contributions for Postal Service retiree health benefits under the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program. Requirements were restructured by the Postal Service Reform Act of 2022.
Postal Service Contributions for Retiree.b: Postal Service retirees enrolled in FEHB, the Postal Service Retiree Health Benefits Fund

Aircraft Procurement, Army.d: Funds procurement and modification of Army rotary-wing and fixed-wing aircraft including Black Hawk helicopters, Apache attack helicopters, Chinook cargo helicopters, and Future Vertical Lift aircraft.
Aircraft Procurement, Army.b: Army aviation units, defense contractors (Sikorsky/Lockheed Martin, Boeing), Army depots, aviation maintenance workforce

Procurement, Space Force, Air Force.d: Funds procurement of Space Force systems including missile warning satellites, GPS satellites, protected communications satellites, space launch services, and ground-based space surveillance systems.
Procurement, Space Force, Air Force.b: Space Force, satellite manufacturers (Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, L3Harris), launch providers (SpaceX, ULA)

National Network Grants to the National.d: Provides federal grants to Amtrak for its national long-distance route network operating and capital needs, supporting approximately 15 long-distance routes serving over 500 communities across 46 states. Restructured under IIJA (2021).
National Network Grants to the National.b: Amtrak, intercity rail passengers especially in rural communities, Amtrak employees

Development Assistance, Agency for Inter.d: Funds USAID’s core development programs in agriculture, education, health, democracy and governance, economic growth, and environment in developing countries. Authorized under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961.
Development Assistance, Agency for Inter.b: Populations in developing countries, NGOs, USAID implementing partners, host-country governments and institutions

National Flood Insurance Fund, Federal E.d: Operates the National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP), providing federally backed flood insurance policies to homeowners, renters, and businesses in participating communities. Created by the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968.
National Flood Insurance Fund, Federal E.b: Property owners in flood-prone areas, mortgage holders, floodplain communities, Write-Your-Own insurance companies

Space Operations, National Aeronautics a.d: Funds NASA’s human spaceflight operations including the International Space Station, commercial crew transportation (SpaceX Crew Dragon, Boeing Starliner), the Space Communications and Navigation network, and launch services.
Space Operations, National Aeronautics a.b: ISS partners and researchers, commercial space companies (SpaceX, Boeing), NASA astronauts, space communications users

Mutual Mortgage Insurance Program Accoun.d: Operates FHA’s single-family mortgage insurance program, insuring home loans made by approved lenders to borrowers who may not qualify for conventional mortgages, particularly first-time and low-to-moderate income homebuyers. Created by the National Housing Act of 1934.
Mutual Mortgage Insurance Program Accoun.b: First-time homebuyers, low-to-moderate income borrowers, FHA-approved mortgage lenders

Salaries and Expenses, United States Pat.d: Funds USPTO operations including examination and issuance of patents and registration of trademarks. Almost entirely fee-funded by patent and trademark applicants. The modern USPTO structure dates to the Patent Act of 1836.
Salaries and Expenses, United States Pat.b: Patent and trademark applicants, inventors, businesses protecting intellectual property, patent examiners

Substance Abuse Treatment, Substance Abu.d: Funds SAMHSA’s substance abuse prevention and treatment programs including the Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Block Grant, opioid treatment programs, and targeted capacity expansion grants. SAMHSA was created in 1992.
Substance Abuse Treatment, Substance Abu.b: Individuals with substance use disorders, state substance abuse agencies, treatment providers, communities affected by opioid crisis

Rehabilitation Services, Education.d: Funds state vocational rehabilitation programs that help individuals with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment, plus independent living services, client assistance programs, and supported employment grants. Authorized under the Rehabilitation Act of 1973.
Rehabilitation Services, Education.b: Individuals with physical and mental disabilities, state vocational rehabilitation agencies, independent living centers

Taxpayer Services, Internal Revenue Serv.d: Funds IRS taxpayer-facing services including processing of tax returns, providing taxpayer assistance (phone, walk-in, online), issuing refunds, pre-filing assistance, and the Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program.
Taxpayer Services, Internal Revenue Serv.b: U.S. taxpayers (individual and business), tax preparers, low-income filers using free file and VITA programs

Low Income Home Energy Assistance, Admin.d: Provides block grants to states to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills, weatherize homes, and address energy-related emergencies. Created by the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Act of 1981 (LIHEAP).
Low Income Home Energy Assistance, Admin.b: Low-income households, elderly persons on fixed incomes, disabled individuals, families with young children

Training and Employment Service, Employm.d: Funds workforce development programs under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA), including adult and dislocated worker training, youth employment programs, Job Corps, YouthBuild, and apprenticeship programs.
Training and Employment Service, Employm.b: Unemployed and underemployed workers, dislocated workers, at-risk youth, Job Corps participants, American Job Centers

State Unemployment Insurance and Employm.d: Provides federal funding to states for administration of unemployment insurance programs, employment services (job matching, career counseling), and labor market information systems. Rooted in the Social Security Act of 1935 and the Wagner-Peyser Act of 1933.
State Unemployment Insurance and Employm.b: Unemployed workers filing UI claims, job seekers using employment services, state workforce agencies

Federal Financing Bank Revolving Fund, T.d: Operates the Federal Financing Bank, a government corporation that purchases obligations issued or guaranteed by other federal agencies, providing them with lower-cost financing by borrowing from the Treasury. Created by the Federal Financing Bank Act of 1973.
Federal Financing Bank Revolving Fund, T.b: Federal agencies and federally-backed borrowers (rural utilities, student loan programs, export financing)

Environmental Programs and Management, E.d: EPA’s primary operating account funding regulatory and compliance programs across air quality, water quality, hazardous waste management, pesticides, toxic substances, environmental justice, and enforcement activities. EPA was created in 1970.
Environmental Programs and Management, E.b: General public (environmental and health protection), regulated industries, state environmental agencies, environmental justice communities

National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institut.d: Funds research on cardiovascular, pulmonary, blood, and sleep disorders, and manages the nation’s blood supply research programs. Originally the National Heart Institute (1948), expanded to cover lung and blood diseases in 1972.
National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institut.b: Patients with heart disease, lung disease, blood disorders, and sleep disorders; cardiovascular researchers; clinical trial participants

Water and Related Resources, Bureau of R.d: Funds the Bureau of Reclamation’s water infrastructure projects in the 17 western states, including dam and reservoir operations, water delivery systems, drought contingency, desalination, water recycling, and rural water projects. Reclamation was established by the Reclamation Act of 1902.
Water and Related Resources, Bureau of R.b: Western farmers and irrigation districts, municipal water users, hydroelectric power consumers, tribal water settlements, endangered species

Working Capital Fund, International Coop.d: A revolving fund that provides shared administrative support services (such as facility maintenance, IT, shipping, local staff management) at U.S. embassies overseas, with costs shared among all U.S. government agencies present at each post.
Working Capital Fund, International Coop.b: U.S. government agencies with overseas presence (State, USAID, DoD, Commerce, etc.), embassy staff

Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Pl.d: Funds competitive and formula grants for homeless assistance including the Continuum of Care program, Emergency Solutions Grants, and rural homelessness programs. Provides funding for shelters, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and homelessness prevention. Originated under the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act of 1987.
Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Pl.b: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness, homeless service providers, Continuums of Care, local governments

Special Benefits, Office of Workers Comp.d: Provides workers’ compensation benefits to federal civilian employees and certain other workers injured on the job, covering medical treatment, wage replacement, vocational rehabilitation, and death benefits under the Federal Employees’ Compensation Act (FECA).
Special Benefits, Office of Workers Comp.b: Injured federal civilian employees, surviving dependents of federal workers killed on duty, postal workers

Employees’ Life Insurance Fund, Office o.d: Operates the Federal Employees’ Group Life Insurance (FEGLI) program, providing group term life insurance coverage to approximately 4 million federal employees and retirees. Established by the Federal Employees’ Group Life Insurance Act of 1954.
Employees’ Life Insurance Fund, Office o.b: Federal employees and retirees enrolled in FEGLI, their designated beneficiaries and survivors

Payments to States from Receipts Under M.d: Distributes to states their statutory share (typically 50%) of revenues collected from mineral extraction (oil, gas, coal, etc.) on federal and acquired military lands within their borders. Mandated by the Mineral Leasing Act of 1920.
Payments to States from Receipts Under M.b: State governments (especially Wyoming, New Mexico, Colorado, Utah, North Dakota), schools, and roads funded by mineral revenues

Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund -.d: A Treasury-managed component of the Medicare Part A (Hospital Insurance) trust fund, handling interest earnings, investment management, and certain administrative transfers for the HI trust fund established by Medicare legislation in 1965.
Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund -.b: Medicare Part A beneficiaries (those 65+ and certain disabled), hospitals, skilled nursing facilities

Capital Investments Grants, Federal Tran.d: Provides competitive federal funding for major transit capital investments including new fixed guideway systems (light rail, heavy rail, bus rapid transit), extensions, and core capacity improvements in existing systems. Known as the New Starts/Small Starts/Core Capacity program.
Capital Investments Grants, Federal Tran.b: Transit agencies building new rail and BRT systems, commuters in metropolitan areas, construction firms

Embassy Security, Construction, and Main.d: Funds the construction, acquisition, maintenance, and security upgrades of U.S. diplomatic facilities worldwide. Created in response to the 1998 embassy bombings in Kenya and Tanzania that highlighted the need for more secure diplomatic compounds.
Embassy Security, Construction, and Main.b: State Department employees overseas, U.S. diplomatic missions, construction and security contractors

Child Care Entitlement to States, Admini.d: Provides mandatory/entitlement funding to states for child care assistance to low-income families, particularly those transitioning from welfare to work. Created under the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 (welfare reform).
Child Care Entitlement to States, Admini.b: Low-income families receiving or transitioning off TANF, working-poor families, child care providers

Higher Education, Department of Educatio.d: Funds federal higher education programs including TRIO (upward bound, talent search, student support services), GEAR UP, Title III/V institutional aid to minority-serving institutions (HBCUs, HSIs, Tribal Colleges), and international education. Authorized under the Higher Education Act of 1965.
Higher Education, Department of Educatio.b: Low-income and first-generation college students, HBCUs, Hispanic-Serving Institutions, Tribal Colleges, TRIO/GEAR UP participants

Bonneville Power Administration Fund, Po.d: Operates the Bonneville Power Administration, which markets wholesale electric power from 31 federal hydroelectric dams on the Columbia River system in the Pacific Northwest, plus transmission over 15,000 miles of high-voltage lines.
Bonneville Power Administration Fund, Po.b: Pacific Northwest electric utilities, industrial customers, ratepayers in OR/WA/ID/MT, salmon recovery programs

SPR Petroleum Account, Energy Programs,.d: Funds the operation, maintenance, and petroleum acquisition for the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, the world’s largest government-owned emergency crude oil stockpile stored in underground salt caverns along the Gulf Coast. Created by the Energy Policy and Conservation Act of 1975.
SPR Petroleum Account, Energy Programs,.b: U.S. consumers and economy (energy security), oil markets during supply disruptions, Gulf Coast storage facility workers

Grants-In-Aid for Airports, Liquidation.d: Provides federal grants to public-use airports for planning, construction, and improvement of runways, taxiways, terminals, and safety/security infrastructure through the Airport Improvement Program (AIP). Funded by the Airport and Airway Trust Fund from aviation excise taxes.
Grants-In-Aid for Airports, Liquidation.b: Public-use airports (especially small and rural), airport operators, airline passengers, general aviation users

Safety, Security and Missions Services,.d: Duplicate entry (slight naming variation) for NASA’s institutional and mission support account. Covers center operations, environmental remediation, security, agency IT, and construction of facilities across NASA’s 10 field centers.
Safety, Security and Missions Services,.b: NASA centers and employees, facility maintenance and construction contractors

Capital Investment Grants, Federal Trans.d: Duplicate entry (slight naming variation) for FTA capital investment grants. Supports the project development pipeline for major transit projects through engineering, final design, and full funding grant agreement stages.
Capital Investment Grants, Federal Trans.b: Transit agencies, metropolitan commuters, communities along new transit corridors

Hazardous Substance Superfund - Treasury.d: Funds the cleanup of the nation’s most contaminated hazardous waste sites (Superfund sites) on the National Priorities List. Created by CERCLA (Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act) of 1980, commonly known as Superfund.
Hazardous Substance Superfund - Treasury.b: Communities near contaminated sites, environmental remediation contractors, state environmental agencies, public health (reduced toxic exposure)

Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Ai.d: Funds FAA capital investments in the air traffic control system including radar and surveillance equipment, navigation aids, communication systems, weather systems, automation, and NextGen air traffic modernization technologies.
Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Ai.b: Airlines, general aviation, air traffic controllers, airline passengers, aviation safety

National Institute of General Medical Sc.d: Funds basic biomedical research that is not targeted at specific diseases, including cell biology, genetics, biophysics, pharmacology, and computational biology. Also administers the IDeA program supporting research in states with historically low NIH funding.
National Institute of General Medical Sc.b: Basic science researchers at universities, graduate students and postdocs, IDeA-eligible states (primarily rural and underserved)

United States Mint Public Enterprise Fun.d: Operates the U.S. Mint as a public enterprise revolving fund, covering all costs of manufacturing and distributing circulating coins, bullion coins (American Eagle, Buffalo), numismatic products, and Congressional gold medals. Established as a PEF by the United States Mint Reauthorization Act of 1995.
United States Mint Public Enterprise Fun.b: Federal Reserve Banks (circulating coin supply), coin collectors, bullion investors, the general public

Interior Franchise Fund, Departmental Ma.d: A revolving fund that provides shared administrative services (IT, financial management, acquisition, HR) to bureaus and offices within the Department of the Interior on a fee-for-service basis. Created under the Government Management Reform Act authority for franchise fund pilots.
Interior Franchise Fund, Departmental Ma.b: Department of Interior bureaus (BLM, NPS, FWS, USGS, BOR, BIA), and other federal agencies purchasing shared services

Program Management, Centers for Medicare.d: Funds the administrative operations of CMS, including program integrity, claims processing, quality oversight, and contractor management for Medicare and Medicaid. Supports the agency’s ability to administer health coverage for over 150 million Americans.
Program Management, Centers for Medicare.b: CMS staff, Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries (seniors, disabled, low-income), Medicare Administrative Contractors, healthcare providers subject to CMS oversight

Operations and Support, United States Se.d: Funds the Secret Service’s dual mission of protecting the President, Vice President, and other dignitaries, as well as investigating financial crimes including counterfeiting, cyber fraud, and identity theft. The account was restructured when Secret Service moved to DHS in 2003.
Operations and Support, United States Se.b: Protected officials (President, VP, former presidents, candidates, foreign dignitaries), the general public (financial crime prevention), Secret Service agents and staff

Indian Health Services, Indian Health Se.d: Funds direct health care services for American Indians and Alaska Natives through a network of hospitals, clinics, and health stations operated by the Indian Health Service. Established by the Indian Health Care Transfer Act of 1954, fulfilling the federal government’s trust responsibility to tribal nations.
Indian Health Services, Indian Health Se.b: Approximately 2.6 million American Indians and Alaska Natives from 574 federally recognized tribes, IHS clinical staff, tribal health programs

Nuclear Energy, Energy Programs, Energy.d: Funds research, development, and demonstration of advanced nuclear energy technologies, including advanced reactor designs, nuclear fuel cycle research, and existing fleet sustainability. Supports DOE’s mission to maintain nuclear power as a carbon-free energy source.
Nuclear Energy, Energy Programs, Energy.b: National laboratories (INL, ANL, ORNL), nuclear energy industry, universities conducting nuclear research, utility companies operating nuclear plants

Operation of the National Park System, N.d: Funds the day-to-day operations of the 400+ units of the National Park System, including resource protection, visitor services, facility maintenance, law enforcement, and interpretation programs. The National Park Service was established by the Organic Act of 1916.
Operation of the National Park System, N.b: Park visitors (over 300 million annually), NPS rangers and staff, local gateway communities, wildlife and natural/cultural resources

Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Pl.d: Funds the Continuum of Care program, Emergency Solutions Grants, and other HUD programs to prevent and end homelessness through shelter, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and rapid re-housing. Originally established under the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act of 1987.
Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Pl.b: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness, veterans, chronically homeless populations, local Continuum of Care organizations, homeless service providers and shelters

Public Health and Social Services Emerge.d: Provides flexible funding for HHS to prepare for and respond to public health emergencies including pandemics, bioterrorism, and natural disasters.
