Supports the Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority (BARDA), the Strategic National Stockpile, and pandemic preparedness activities.
Public Health and Social Services Emerge.b: General public (pandemic/emergency preparedness), hospitals, state and local health departments, pharmaceutical and biotech companies developing medical countermeasures

Mental Health, Substance Abuse and Menta.d: Funds programs to prevent and treat mental illness and substance use disorders, including the Community Mental Health Services Block Grant, the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, and programs addressing serious mental illness. SAMHSA was established in 1992 under the ADAMHA Reorganization Act.
Mental Health, Substance Abuse and Menta.b: Individuals with mental health and substance use disorders, community mental health centers, state mental health authorities, crisis intervention services, children and adolescents with serious emotional disturbance

Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund - Treasury.d: Funded by the Harbor Maintenance Tax (a per-value ad valorem tax on commercial cargo at U.S. ports), this trust fund supports the maintenance dredging, jetty repair, and upkeep of federal navigation channels and harbors. Created by the Water Resources Development Act of 1986.
Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund - Treasury.b: Commercial shipping companies, port authorities, coastal communities, importers/exporters, U.S. Army Corps of Engineers

Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Co.d: The Army’s annual contribution to the Department of Defense Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund (MERHCF), which pre-funds the health care costs of military retirees and their dependents who are eligible for Medicare. Established by the National Defense Authorization Act for FY2001.
Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Co.b: Retired Army service members and their dependents who are Medicare-eligible (age 65+), TRICARE for Life participants

Bureau of Engraving and Printing Fund, T.d: A revolving fund that finances the Bureau of Engraving and Printing’s operations in designing and manufacturing U.S. currency (Federal Reserve Notes), security documents, and other government printed materials. BEP recoups costs by selling its products to the Federal Reserve and other agencies.
Bureau of Engraving and Printing Fund, T.b: Federal Reserve System (primary customer), the general public (currency users), BEP employees at Washington DC and Fort Worth facilities

Salaries and Expenses, United States Att.d: Funds the 94 U.S. Attorney offices across the country, which serve as the principal litigators of the federal government, prosecuting federal crimes, defending the government in civil cases, and collecting debts owed to the United States. The offices were formalized under the Department of Justice Act of 1870.
Salaries and Expenses, United States Att.b: Federal prosecutors (AUSAs), victims of federal crimes, the federal judiciary system, communities benefiting from federal law enforcement

National Institute of Neurological Disor.d: Funds biomedical research on brain and nervous system disorders including stroke, epilepsy, Parkinson’s disease, Alzheimer’s disease, traumatic brain injury, and spinal cord injury. NINDS is the leading federal funder of neurological research, established in 1950.
National Institute of Neurological Disor.b: Patients with neurological disorders, neuroscience researchers, academic medical centers, pharmaceutical companies developing neurological therapies

Office of the Director, National Institu.d: Funds the central leadership and coordination activities of NIH, including the Common Fund for trans-NIH strategic initiatives, the All of Us precision medicine research program, and other cross-cutting research priorities. The OD sets overall NIH policy and direction.
Office of the Director, National Institu.b: Biomedical researchers across all disciplines, All of Us research participants, the scientific community broadly, patients benefiting from cross-cutting research initiatives

Service and Supply Fund, National Instit.d: A revolving fund that provides centralized administrative and support services to NIH institutes and centers on a reimbursable basis, including facilities management, IT services, procurement, and research support. Operates on a fee-for-service model.
Service and Supply Fund, National Instit.b: NIH institutes and centers, NIH researchers, contractors providing support services to the NIH campus

Energy Employees Occupational Illness Co.d: Provides lump-sum compensation and medical benefits to current and former Department of Energy employees, contractors, and subcontractors (or their survivors) who became ill due to exposure to radiation, beryllium, or silica during nuclear weapons production. Created by the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act of 2000.
Energy Employees Occupational Illness Co.b: Former nuclear weapons production workers and uranium miners with occupational illnesses, their survivors, DOE contractor employees

Salaries and Expenses, Drug Enforcement.d: Funds the DEA’s operations in enforcing controlled substance laws and regulations, investigating drug trafficking organizations, managing the diversion control program, and supporting international counter-narcotics efforts. The DEA was established by executive order in 1973.
Salaries and Expenses, Drug Enforcement.b: DEA special agents and staff, communities affected by drug trafficking, law enforcement partners, the criminal justice system

National Forest System, Forest Service,.d: Funds the management, protection, and stewardship of 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands, including recreation management, wildlife habitat, timber sales, watershed protection, and road maintenance. The Forest Service was established within USDA in 1905.
National Forest System, Forest Service,.b: Recreational users, rural communities, timber industry, wildlife, watershed-dependent communities, Forest Service employees (rangers, firefighters)

Aging and Disability Services Programs,.d: Funds programs under the Older Americans Act and other authorities that support older adults and people with disabilities to live independently in their communities, including home-delivered meals (Meals on Wheels), senior centers, caregiver support, and disability rights protection. ACL was created in 2012 by consolidating several HHS offices.
Aging and Disability Services Programs,.b: Older adults (especially those 60+), people with disabilities, family caregivers, Area Agencies on Aging, Meals on Wheels providers, senior centers, independent living centers

Defense Nuclear Nonproliferation, Nation.d: Funds programs to detect, prevent, and reverse the proliferation of nuclear weapons and materials worldwide, including securing vulnerable nuclear materials, monitoring nuclear detonations, converting research reactors from highly enriched uranium, and disposing of surplus weapons-grade plutonium. Part of NNSA established in 2000.
Defense Nuclear Nonproliferation, Nation.b: Global security stakeholders, the IAEA, partner countries with nuclear materials, national laboratories (PNNL, SNL, LANL), the general public (nuclear threat reduction)

Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program, Health Reso.d: Funds a comprehensive system of HIV primary medical care, medications (AIDS Drug Assistance Program), and support services for low-income, uninsured, and underserved people living with HIV. Named after Ryan White and authorized by the Ryan White CARE Act of 1990.
Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program, Health Reso.b: People living with HIV/AIDS who lack adequate health coverage (approximately 500,000+ annually), HIV/AIDS clinics, community health centers, ADAP participants

Health Care Fraud and Abuse Control Acco.d: Funds coordinated federal, state, and local efforts to combat fraud, waste, and abuse in the Medicare and Medicaid programs, including the HHS Office of Inspector General’s investigative activities and the Medicare Fraud Strike Force. Established by the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) of 1996.
Health Care Fraud and Abuse Control Acco.b: Medicare and Medicaid programs (cost savings), taxpayers, legitimate healthcare providers, Medicare beneficiaries, DOJ and HHS-OIG investigators

Research, Development, and Procurement,.d: Funds the development, procurement, and stockpiling of medical countermeasures (vaccines, therapeutics, diagnostics) for public health emergencies and national security threats. ASPR was elevated to an operating division within HHS in 2022, consolidating BARDA and Strategic National Stockpile functions.
Research, Development, and Procurement,.b: The general public (emergency preparedness), pharmaceutical and biotech companies developing countermeasures, hospitals, state health departments

Rural Electrification and Telecommunicat.d: Provides direct and guaranteed loans to rural electric cooperatives, telecommunications companies, and broadband providers to finance infrastructure in rural areas. Originally established by the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 to bring electricity to rural America.
Rural Electrification and Telecommunicat.b: Rural electric cooperatives, rural telecommunications providers, rural broadband providers, rural residents and businesses lacking utility infrastructure

Securities and Exchange Commission.d: Funds the SEC’s operations in regulating securities markets, protecting investors, enforcing securities laws, and overseeing public company disclosures, broker-dealers, investment advisers, and stock exchanges. Created by the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 in response to the 1929 stock market crash.
Securities and Exchange Commission.b: Investors (retail and institutional), publicly traded companies, securities markets, broker-dealers, investment advisers, the financial system broadly

Payment Where American Opportunity Credi.d: Funds the refundable portion of the American Opportunity Tax Credit (AOTC) that exceeds a taxpayer’s income tax liability, providing up to $1,000 in refundable credits for qualifying higher education expenses. Originally enacted as part of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 and later made permanent.
Payment Where American Opportunity Credi.b: College students and their families (especially low- and moderate-income), postsecondary education institutions

National Institute of Diabetes and Diges.d: Funds research on diabetes, endocrine and metabolic diseases, digestive diseases, nutritional disorders, kidney and urologic diseases, and hematologic diseases. NIDDK supports major clinical research networks and is a leading funder of type 1 and type 2 diabetes research.
National Institute of Diabetes and Diges.b: Patients with diabetes, kidney disease, obesity, and digestive disorders; biomedical researchers; academic medical centers; clinical trial participants

Salaries and Expenses, Animal and Plant.d: Funds APHIS operations in protecting U.S. agriculture from pests and diseases, including animal health surveillance, plant pest and disease prevention, wildlife damage management, veterinary diagnostics, and regulation of genetically engineered organisms. APHIS was established as a separate USDA agency in 1972.
Salaries and Expenses, Animal and Plant.b: Farmers, ranchers, and agricultural producers; the livestock industry; international trade partners; wildlife; consumers (food safety)

CDC-Wide Activities and Program Support,.d: Funds cross-cutting public health activities at CDC including public health preparedness, global health security, health statistics and surveillance, laboratory science, and agency-wide program support. Provides the CDC Director flexibility for emerging health threats and core infrastructure.
CDC-Wide Activities and Program Support,.b: The general public (disease prevention), state and local health departments, global health partners, epidemiologists and public health researchers

Procurement, Acquisition and Constructio.d: Funds NOAA’s major capital investments including weather satellites (GOES, JPSS), research vessels, weather radar systems, supercomputers for weather modeling, and facility construction. Supports the infrastructure that underpins weather forecasting and ocean observation.
Procurement, Acquisition and Constructio.b: The general public (weather forecasting), aviation and maritime industries, farmers, emergency managers, NOAA scientists, satellite industry contractors

Victims Compensation Fund, General Legal.d: Provides compensation to individuals (or their families) who suffered physical harm or death as a result of the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks or from exposure to debris and toxins at crash sites. Reauthorized and extended by the Never Forget the Heroes Act of 2019 through 2090.
Victims Compensation Fund, General Legal.b: 9/11 survivors, first responders (FDNY, NYPD, Port Authority), recovery workers, residents and workers in the exposure zones, families of those killed

Operations and Support, Cybersecurity an.d: Funds CISA’s mission to protect the nation’s critical infrastructure and federal civilian networks from cyber threats, coordinate national cybersecurity efforts, and secure elections infrastructure. CISA was established as a standalone agency within DHS by the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency Act of 2018.
Operations and Support, Cybersecurity an.b: Federal civilian agencies, critical infrastructure operators (energy, water, healthcare), state and local governments (election security), private sector cybersecurity partners

Surveys, Investigations and Research, U..d: Funds USGS’s scientific research and monitoring programs including earthquake and volcano monitoring, water resources assessment, biological surveys, mapping and geospatial data, mineral resources, and natural hazards research. USGS was established by Congress in 1879.
Surveys, Investigations and Research, U..b: The general public (natural hazard warnings), scientists and researchers, land and water managers, emergency managers, mining and energy industries, state geological surveys

Student and Administration, Education.d: Funds the Department of Education’s administrative costs for managing federal student aid programs (the largest source of student financial aid in the country), including loan servicing, program oversight, financial management, and the Federal Student Aid office operations.
Student and Administration, Education.b: Student loan borrowers, college students receiving financial aid, Federal Student Aid (FSA) staff, loan servicers

Food for Peace Title II Grants, Foreign.d: Provides U.S. agricultural commodities as food aid to developing countries for emergency relief and development programs, administered primarily through USAID. Established by the Agricultural Trade Development and Assistance Act of 1954 (P.L. 480).
Food for Peace Title II Grants, Foreign.b: Populations in food-insecure developing countries, humanitarian organizations (WFP, CARE, Catholic Relief Services), U.S. agricultural producers (commodity procurement)

Northeast Corridor Grants to the Nationa.d: Provides federal grants specifically for capital improvements, maintenance, and operations on Amtrak’s Northeast Corridor (Boston-New York-Washington), the most heavily used passenger rail line in the country. Authorized under the Passenger Rail Investment and Improvement Act of 2008.
Northeast Corridor Grants to the Nationa.b: Amtrak NEC riders, commuter rail agencies using the corridor (NJ Transit, MARC, SEPTA, Metro-North), Northeast Corridor communities, Amtrak employees

State and Private Forestry, Forest Servi.d: Funds Forest Service programs that provide financial and technical assistance to state forestry agencies, tribal governments, and private landowners for forest stewardship, urban forestry, wildfire prevention, forest health monitoring, and community forestry. Supports cooperative programs beyond National Forest lands.
State and Private Forestry, Forest Servi.b: State forestry agencies, private forest landowners, tribal forest managers, urban communities (urban forestry), rural fire departments (volunteer fire assistance)

National Institute of Mental Health, Nat.d: Funds research to understand, treat, and prevent mental disorders including depression, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, anxiety disorders, autism spectrum disorder, and PTSD. NIMH is the lead federal agency for research on mental illness.
National Institute of Mental Health, Nat.b: People with mental health disorders, psychiatric researchers, clinical trial participants, academic institutions, mental health clinicians

Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of.d: Funds the Bureau of Indian Affairs’ responsibilities to federally recognized tribes including tribal government support, natural resource management, law enforcement, social services, and economic development on Indian reservations. BIA is one of the oldest federal agencies, established in 1824.
Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of.b: Members of 574 federally recognized tribes, tribal governments, tribal courts, BIA law enforcement, Indian communities on reservations

Indian Health Facilities, Indian Health.d: Funds the construction, renovation, maintenance, and improvement of Indian Health Service hospitals, clinics, health stations, and staff quarters serving American Indian and Alaska Native communities. Also supports sanitation facilities (water and sewer) for tribal communities.
Indian Health Facilities, Indian Health.b: American Indians and Alaska Natives, tribal health programs, IHS hospitals and clinics, tribal communities lacking water and sanitation infrastructure

Flood Control and Coastal Emergencies, C.d: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers’ emergency response to floods and coastal storms, including emergency levee repairs, sandbagging operations, pumping, and post-disaster rehabilitation of flood control works. Activated when the President declares a flood emergency.
Flood Control and Coastal Emergencies, C.b: Communities in flood zones, property owners protected by federal levees and flood control structures, state and local emergency managers

Working Capital Fund, Justice.d: A revolving fund providing centralized administrative services to DOJ components on a reimbursable basis, including IT infrastructure, telecommunications, financial management systems, building operations, and shared litigation support. Achieves economies of scale across DOJ.
Working Capital Fund, Justice.b: DOJ components (FBI, DEA, ATF, BOP, USMS, EOUSA), DOJ employees, IT service contractors

Supply Fund, Veterans Affairs.d: A revolving fund that provides centralized supply chain, logistics, and procurement services for the Department of Veterans Affairs, including medical supplies, pharmaceuticals, equipment, and prosthetics distribution to VA medical centers and facilities.
Supply Fund, Veterans Affairs.b: VA medical centers, veteran patients, VA healthcare staff, medical supply and pharmaceutical vendors

Career, Technical, and Adult Education,.d: Funds career and technical education (CTE) programs in secondary and postsecondary institutions through the Carl D. Perkins Act, as well as adult education and literacy programs. The federal role in vocational education dates to the Smith-Hughes Act of 1917.
Career, Technical, and Adult Education,.b: High school and community college CTE students, adult learners, workers seeking skills upgrades, state CTE agencies, community colleges, career centers

Federal Prisoner Detention, United State.d: Funds the housing, transportation, and care of federal prisoners and detainees in the custody of the U.S. Marshals Service prior to sentencing, including costs for contract detention facilities, state and local jail agreements, and medical care. The USMS is the nation’s oldest federal law enforcement agency.
Federal Prisoner Detention, United State.b: Federal pretrial detainees, the U.S. Marshals Service, private detention facility operators, state and local jails holding federal detainees

Federal Prisoner Detention, Justice.d: The broader DOJ account for federal prisoner detention costs covering pre-trial and pre-sentencing custody of individuals in the federal criminal justice system. Funds contract facilities and intergovernmental agreements for detention services.
Federal Prisoner Detention, Justice.b: Federal detainees awaiting trial or sentencing, the federal judiciary, detention facility operators, USMS

Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Co.d: The Navy’s annual accrual payment to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, pre-funding the future health care costs of Navy and Marine Corps retirees and dependents who will become Medicare-eligible. Established by the FY2001 NDAA.
Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Co.b: Retired Navy and Marine Corps personnel and their dependents who are Medicare-eligible, TRICARE for Life beneficiaries

Build America Bond Payments - Recovery A.d: Funds the federal subsidy payments to state and local governments that issued Build America Bonds under the 2009 Recovery Act, where the Treasury makes direct payments equal to 35% of interest costs on these taxable municipal bonds. The program ran from April 2009 to December 2010 but payments continue for the life of outstanding bonds.
Build America Bond Payments - Recovery A.b: State and local governments that issued BABs, municipal bond investors, taxpayers in issuing jurisdictions (lower borrowing costs for infrastructure)

Resource Management, United States Fish.d: Funds the FWS’s core operations including management of the National Wildlife Refuge System (560+ refuges), endangered species conservation, migratory bird management, fisheries restoration, habitat conservation, and law enforcement against wildlife trafficking. FWS was established in its current form in 1940.
Resource Management, United States Fish.b: Wildlife and endangered species, refuge visitors, hunters and anglers, state wildlife agencies, conservation organizations, migratory bird populations

Transit Infrastructure Grants, Federal T.d: Funds capital investments in public transit systems including bus and rail fleet replacement, transit facility construction and modernization, new fixed-guideway projects (light rail, BRT), and state of good repair. Rooted in the Urban Mass Transportation Act of 1964.
Transit Infrastructure Grants, Federal T.b: Public transit agencies, transit riders (especially low-income and urban commuters), metropolitan areas, transit equipment manufacturers

National Infrastructure Investments, Off.d: The competitive discretionary grant program originally known as TIGER grants (now RAISE grants - Rebuilding American Infrastructure with Sustainability and Equity), funding multimodal surface transportation projects of national or regional significance. Created in the 2009 Recovery Act.
National Infrastructure Investments, Off.b: State and local governments, metropolitan planning organizations, transit agencies, port authorities, rural communities with significant transportation needs

State and Local Law Enforcement Assistan.d: Provides federal grants to state, local, and tribal law enforcement and criminal justice agencies for a range of programs including the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grants, violence reduction initiatives, body-worn camera programs, and forensic science improvement. Originated with the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968.
State and Local Law Enforcement Assistan.b: State and local police departments, prosecutors, courts, corrections agencies, crime victims, tribal law enforcement, community-based violence intervention programs

Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Co.d: The Air Force’s annual accrual contribution to the DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, pre-funding health care obligations for retired Air Force personnel and their Medicare-eligible dependents under TRICARE for Life.
Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Fund Co.b: Retired Air Force service members and their dependents eligible for Medicare, TRICARE for Life participants

Franchise Fund, Veterans Affairs.d: A revolving fund providing shared administrative services to VA components on a fee-for-service basis, including financial management, human resources, IT services, and debt management. Created under the Government Management Reform Act franchise fund pilot authority.
Franchise Fund, Veterans Affairs.b: VA offices and administrations (VHA, VBA, NCA), VA employees, other federal agencies purchasing VA shared services

Operations and Support, Management Direc.d: Funds DHS headquarters management and oversight functions including the Office of Intelligence and Analysis, departmental operations coordination, financial management, procurement, human capital, and the DHS Chief Information Officer. Supports the department-wide management backbone.
Operations and Support, Management Direc.b: DHS component agencies, DHS workforce, intelligence community partners, federal cybersecurity coordination

Airport Infrastructure Grants, Federal A.d: Provides federal grants to airports (through the Airport Improvement Program and supplemental programs) for runway, taxiway, and terminal construction and improvement, safety and security enhancements, and noise mitigation. Originally rooted in the Federal Airport Act of 1946.
Airport Infrastructure Grants, Federal A.b: Public-use airports (commercial and general aviation), airline passengers, airport authorities, surrounding communities (noise mitigation), aviation safety

Salaries and Expenses, Agricultural Rese.d: Funds the USDA’s primary intramural scientific research agency, operating over 90 research locations conducting studies on crop and animal production, food safety, nutrition, natural resource conservation, and agricultural sustainability. ARS was established in 1953.
Salaries and Expenses, Agricultural Rese.b: Farmers and ranchers, the food industry, consumers (food safety and nutrition), agricultural scientists, plant and animal breeders

Veterans Housing Benefit Program Fund, V.d: Funds the VA Home Loan Guaranty program which guarantees a portion of home mortgage loans made by private lenders to eligible veterans, active duty service members, and surviving spouses, enabling home purchases with no down payment and no private mortgage insurance. Established by the Servicemen’s Readjustment Act (GI Bill) of 1944.
Veterans Housing Benefit Program Fund, V.b: Veterans and active duty service members purchasing homes, surviving spouses, private mortgage lenders, the housing market

Clean Vehicle Credit, Internal Revenue S.d: Funds the refundable tax credit for purchases of new and used clean vehicles (electric vehicles, plug-in hybrids, and fuel cell vehicles) as established by the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022, providing up to $7,500 for new and $4,000 for used clean vehicles meeting domestic content requirements.
Clean Vehicle Credit, Internal Revenue S.b: Consumers purchasing qualifying electric and clean vehicles, EV manufacturers and dealers, domestic battery and critical mineral supply chain

Federal Protective Service, Management D.d: Funds the Federal Protective Service, which provides law enforcement and security services for over 9,000 federal buildings and facilities, including contract guard forces, criminal investigations, and risk assessments. FPS is funded through security fees charged to tenant federal agencies.
Federal Protective Service, Management D.b: Federal employees working in government buildings, federal agencies (as tenants), visitors to federal facilities, contract security guard force

International Narcotics Control and Law.d: Funds U.S. assistance to foreign countries for counter-narcotics operations, law enforcement capacity building, judicial reform, anti-corruption programs, and rule of law development. Major recipients include countries in Latin America (Plan Colombia successor programs), Afghanistan, and other drug-source/transit countries.
International Narcotics Control and Law.b: Partner country law enforcement and judicial systems, populations in drug-source countries, U.S. counter-narcotics interests, INL-funded implementing organizations

U.S. International Development Finance C.d: Funds America’s development finance institution, which provides loans, guarantees, equity investments, political risk insurance, and technical assistance to private sector projects in developing countries. Created by the BUILD Act of 2018 (operational 2019), consolidating OPIC and USAID’s Development Credit Authority.
U.S. International Development Finance C.b: Private sector companies investing in developing countries, populations in low- and middle-income countries, U.S. foreign policy and development interests

Operating Expenses of the United States.d: Funds USAID’s administrative and operational costs, including staff salaries, overseas mission operations, IT systems, security, and the infrastructure needed to manage U.S. foreign development assistance programs worldwide. USAID was established by executive order in 1961.
Operating Expenses of the United States.b: USAID staff (domestic and overseas), foreign service officers, USAID missions in developing countries, development program management

Wildland Fire Management, Department-Wid.d: Funds the Department of Interior’s wildland fire suppression, hazardous fuels reduction, prescribed burning, and fire preparedness activities across BLM, NPS, FWS, and BIA lands. Restructured after the 2000 National Fire Plan in response to severe wildfire seasons.
Wildland Fire Management, Department-Wid.b: Communities in the wildland-urban interface, Interior agency firefighters, wildlife and ecosystem health, western states affected by wildfire

Fossil Energy, Energy Programs, Energy.d: Funds research and development on fossil energy technologies including carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), hydrogen from fossil sources, advanced coal and natural gas power systems, and the Strategic Petroleum Reserve operations. Increasingly focused on decarbonization of fossil energy use.
Fossil Energy, Energy Programs, Energy.b: Fossil energy industry (coal, oil, natural gas), national laboratories, CCUS technology developers, communities dependent on fossil energy, Strategic Petroleum Reserve

Naval Reactors, National Nuclear Securit.d: Funds the Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program, which designs, builds, operates, and maintains nuclear propulsion systems for the Navy’s aircraft carriers and submarines. Oversees the entire lifecycle of naval nuclear reactors from R&D through decommissioning, with a perfect safety record since Admiral Rickover’s leadership.
Naval Reactors, National Nuclear Securit.b: U.S. Navy (submarine and carrier fleet), Bettis and Knolls national laboratories, naval shipyards, nuclear-trained Navy personnel

Office of Job Corps, Employment and Trai.d: Funds the Job Corps program, which provides free residential education and vocational training to at-risk youth ages 16-24 at over 120 centers nationwide. Established as part of the Economic Opportunity Act of 1964 under the War on Poverty, it is the nation’s largest residential career training program for disadvantaged youth.
Office of Job Corps, Employment and Trai.b: At-risk and disadvantaged youth (16-24), Job Corps center operators (both federal and contractor-run), employers hiring Job Corps graduates

Contributions for International Peacekee.d: Funds the United States’ assessed contributions to United Nations peacekeeping operations worldwide, covering the U.S. share (approximately 25-27%) of UN peacekeeping budgets for missions in conflict zones. The U.S. is the largest financial contributor to UN peacekeeping.
Contributions for International Peacekee.b: Populations in conflict zones with UN peacekeeping missions, the United Nations, U.S. foreign policy interests in international stability

Nonrecurring Expense Fund, Office of the.d: Collects unobligated balances from expiring HHS discretionary accounts and makes them available for one-time capital investments in IT modernization, facilities, and infrastructure projects across the department. Created by the Consolidated Appropriations Act of 2008.
Nonrecurring Expense Fund, Office of the.b: HHS operating divisions, HHS IT systems modernization, federal health program infrastructure

Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institut.d: Funds research on fertility, pregnancy, child development, intellectual and developmental disabilities, and rehabilitation science. NICHD conducts and supports research across the lifespan from conception through adulthood, including maternal health, SIDS, and population dynamics. Established in 1962 at the urging of Eunice Kennedy Shriver.
Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institut.b: Pregnant women, infants and children, people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, fertility patients, population health researchers

Refugee and Entrant Assistance, Administ.d: Funds the Office of Refugee Resettlement (ORR) which provides transitional assistance to refugees, asylees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, victims of trafficking, and unaccompanied migrant children, including cash assistance, medical screening, social services, and foster care. Authorized by the Refugee Act of 1980.
Refugee and Entrant Assistance, Administ.b: Refugees and asylees admitted to the U.S., unaccompanied migrant children, trafficking victims, refugee resettlement agencies (VOLAGS), state refugee coordinators

Salaries and Expenses, United States Mar.d: Funds the operations of the U.S. Marshals Service, the nation’s oldest federal law enforcement agency, responsible for fugitive apprehension, court security, witness protection (WITSEC), prisoner transport, asset forfeiture, and serving federal court orders. Established by the Judiciary Act of 1789.
Salaries and Expenses, United States Mar.b: Federal courts (judicial security), federal judges and prosecutors, witnesses in the WITSEC program, communities (fugitive apprehension), USMS deputies

National Institute on Drug Abuse, Nation.d: Funds research on drug use and addiction, including the neuroscience of substance use disorders, development of addiction treatments and prevention strategies, and the health consequences of drug use including the opioid crisis. NIDA was established in 1974.
National Institute on Drug Abuse, Nation.b: People with substance use disorders, addiction researchers, clinicians treating addiction, communities affected by drug abuse, the opioid crisis response

Millennium Challenge Corporation.d: Funds the MCC, an independent U.S. foreign aid agency that provides large, time-limited grants (compacts) to developing countries that meet rigorous eligibility criteria for good governance, economic freedom, and investing in their people. Created by the Millennium Challenge Act of 2003 (operational 2004).
Millennium Challenge Corporation.b: Developing countries meeting MCC eligibility criteria, populations in compact countries (through infrastructure, agriculture, education investments), U.S. development policy

Expenses, Millennium Challenge Corporati.d: The administrative and program expense account for MCC covering compact development, due diligence, monitoring and evaluation, and threshold program implementation in eligible developing countries. Supports MCC’s evidence-based approach to development assistance.
Expenses, Millennium Challenge Corporati.b: MCC staff, partner country governments, program implementers, populations benefiting from MCC-funded projects

Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Alcohol.d: Funds ATF’s operations in regulating the firearms and explosives industries, investigating firearms trafficking, arson, and explosives crimes, and enforcing federal alcohol and tobacco tax laws. ATF became a DOJ law enforcement agency in 2003 (previously Treasury), with roots in the 1972 establishment as a separate bureau.
Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Alcohol.b: Public safety (firearms violence reduction), law enforcement partners, licensed firearms dealers, the explosives industry (regulation), communities affected by gun violence

Defense Counterintelligence and Security.d: Funds the Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA), which conducts background investigations for security clearances across the federal government and provides industrial security oversight for classified defense contracts. DCSA was established in 2019 to replace the National Background Investigations Bureau.
Defense Counterintelligence and Security.b: Federal employees and contractors requiring security clearances, DoD and other agencies (investigation services), defense industrial base, national security

Primary Health Care, Health Resources an.d: Funds the Health Center Program (Section 330 of the Public Health Service Act) which supports approximately 1,400 community health centers providing comprehensive primary care to underserved populations regardless of ability to pay. These Federally Qualified Health Centers serve over 30 million patients annually.
Primary Health Care, Health Resources an.b: Uninsured and underserved patients, community health centers (FQHCs), rural and urban medically underserved communities, migrant farmworkers, homeless individuals

Conservation Operations, Natural Resourc.d: Funds NRCS’s technical assistance to farmers, ranchers, and private landowners in implementing conservation practices for soil health, water quality, and wildlife habitat on working agricultural lands. NRCS (originally the Soil Conservation Service) was established after the Dust Bowl in 1935.
Conservation Operations, Natural Resourc.b: Farmers and ranchers, private landowners, soil and water conservation districts, the environment (soil, water, wildlife), rural communities

Training and Employment Services, Employ.d: Funds workforce development programs under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA), including adult training, dislocated worker assistance, youth employment programs, and the American Job Center network. Supports workers in acquiring skills for in-demand occupations.
Training and Employment Services, Employ.b: Unemployed and underemployed workers, dislocated workers, youth, American Job Centers (One-Stop Career Centers), state workforce agencies, employers seeking skilled workers

Advanced Research Projects Agency for He.d: Funds ARPA-H, a new agency modeled on DARPA that pursues high-risk, high-reward biomedical and health research breakthroughs that cannot be readily accomplished through traditional NIH funding mechanisms. Focuses on transformative health technologies including cancer detection, mRNA platforms, and precision medicine.
Advanced Research Projects Agency for He.b: Patients with difficult-to-treat diseases, biomedical researchers and innovators, biotech startups, academic institutions, the health technology sector

Clean Energy Demonstrations, Energy Prog.d: Funds large-scale demonstration projects for clean energy technologies including clean hydrogen hubs, carbon capture demonstrations, advanced nuclear, long-duration energy storage, and industrial decarbonization. Established by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (Bipartisan Infrastructure Law) of 2021.
Clean Energy Demonstrations, Energy Prog.b: Clean energy technology developers, communities hosting demonstration projects, national laboratories, energy transition workers, industrial decarbonization efforts

Other Defense Activities, Environmental.d: Funds the cleanup of legacy nuclear weapons production sites and other defense-related environmental remediation managed by DOE’s Office of Environmental Management. Addresses radioactive and hazardous waste contamination at sites like Hanford, Savannah River, and Oak Ridge.
Other Defense Activities, Environmental.b: Communities near former nuclear weapons production sites, environmental cleanup workers, environmental remediation contractors, the environment (groundwater, soil)

Surveys, Investigations, and Research, G.d: A second fiscal year appropriation for USGS scientific activities including earthquake early warning systems, streamflow monitoring, landsat satellite operations, mineral resource assessments, and biological science supporting wildlife management and ecosystem health.
Surveys, Investigations, and Research, G.b: The public (natural hazard preparedness), water resource managers, scientists, land managers, emergency responders, mining and energy industries

Working Capital Fund, Agriculture.d: A revolving fund providing centralized administrative services to USDA agencies on a reimbursable basis, including IT systems (including the USDA data center), financial management, telecommunications, procurement, and human resources support.
Working Capital Fund, Agriculture.b: USDA agencies and missions areas, USDA employees, IT service providers, shared services customers

Salaries and Expenses, General Legal Act.d: Funds the DOJ’s litigating divisions including the Civil Division, Civil Rights Division, Criminal Division, Antitrust Division, Tax Division, and Environment and Natural Resources Division, which handle the government’s most significant legal matters. Covers attorney salaries, litigation costs, and legal operations.
Salaries and Expenses, General Legal Act.b: The federal government (legal representation), civil rights communities, antitrust enforcement, environmental protection, tax compliance, DOJ attorneys

Contribution to the International Develo.d: Funds the U.S. contribution to the International Development Association (IDA), the World Bank’s concessional lending arm that provides grants and zero/low-interest loans to the world’s poorest countries for poverty reduction and development. IDA was established in 1960; the U.S. is historically its largest contributor.
Contribution to the International Develo.b: The world’s 75 poorest countries, populations in IDA-eligible nations (sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia), global development goals

Operation of Indian Education Programs,.d: Funds the Bureau of Indian Education’s operation of approximately 180 schools and dormitories serving Native American students on or near reservations, as well as tribal colleges and universities and Johnson-O’Malley supplemental education programs. Fulfills the federal trust responsibility for Indian education.
Operation of Indian Education Programs,.b: Native American and Alaska Native students (approximately 46,000), BIE-operated and tribally-controlled schools, tribal colleges, families on reservations

Veterans Electronic Health Record, Depar.d: Funds the VA’s effort to deploy a modern electronic health record system (Oracle Cerner/now Oracle Health) to replace the legacy VistA system, aiming for interoperability with the Department of Defense’s MHS GENESIS system for seamless care transitions between military and veteran status.
Veterans Electronic Health Record, Depar.b: Veterans receiving VA healthcare, VA clinicians and staff, Oracle Health (Cerner) as the contractor, DoD (interoperability)

Rental Assistance Program, Rural Housing.d: Provides rental subsidies to low-income tenants living in USDA-financed rural multifamily housing projects (Section 521 rental assistance), ensuring that tenants pay no more than 30% of their income toward rent. Administered by the Rural Housing Service within USDA.
Rental Assistance Program, Rural Housing.b: Low-income rural renters, elderly and disabled tenants in rural housing, owners of USDA-financed rural rental properties, rural communities

Training and Employment Service.d: Funds job training, employment services, and workforce development programs administered by the Employment and Training Administration under the Department of Labor.
Training and Employment Service.b: Unemployed and underemployed workers, dislocated workers, youth, veterans seeking employment assistance

Social Services Block Grant (SSBG).d: Provides flexible federal funding to states for a wide range of social services including child care, protective services, home-based care, and community-based support. Established under Title XX of the Social Security Act.
Social Services Block Grant (SSBG).b: Low-income individuals and families, children, elderly, disabled persons

Assets Forfeiture Fund.d: Receives proceeds from the forfeiture of assets seized in federal criminal investigations and uses them to fund law enforcement operations, victim restitution, and equitable sharing with state/local agencies.
Assets Forfeiture Fund.b: Federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies; crime victims receiving restitution

Management of Lands and Resources.d: Funds the Bureau of Land Management’s core operations including management of 245+ million surface acres of public lands, resource planning, cadastral surveys, and environmental assessments.
Management of Lands and Resources.b: General public, ranchers and grazing permittees, mining claimants, recreation users on public lands

Salaries and Expenses, Farm Service Agen.d: Covers administrative costs of the Farm Service Agency including salaries of field office staff who deliver farm commodity, conservation, credit, and disaster assistance programs.
Salaries and Expenses, Farm Service Agen.b: American farmers and ranchers accessing USDA program services

Operations and Support, FEMA.d: Funds FEMA’s base operations, staffing, IT infrastructure, preparedness grants management, and readiness for disaster response coordination across the nation.
Operations and Support, FEMA.b: General public in disaster-prone areas, state and local emergency management agencies

Hermit’s Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Assistanc.d: Created by the Hermit’s Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Assistance Act to compensate individuals, businesses, and tribal nations for losses caused by the 2022 prescribed fire that escaped federal control in New Mexico.
Hermit’s Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Assistanc.b: Residents, businesses, tribal communities, and local governments affected by the 2022 Hermit’s Peak/Calf Canyon Fire in New Mexico

Operation of Indian Programs.d: Funds the Bureau of Indian Affairs’ operation of programs for federally recognized tribes including tribal government support, social services, natural resources management, law enforcement, and education.
Operation of Indian Programs.b: Members of 574+ federally recognized American Indian and Alaska Native tribes

Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund.d: Funded by fees on active coal production under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977, used to reclaim lands and waters damaged by coal mining before 1977.
Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund.b: Communities near abandoned coal mines, states and tribes with legacy mining environmental damage

Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund Progr.d: Provides direct and guaranteed loans to farmers and ranchers who cannot obtain commercial credit, covering operating expenses, farm ownership, and emergency needs through the Farm Service Agency.
Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund Progr.b: Beginning, socially disadvantaged, and family farmers and ranchers unable to obtain private credit

United States Emergency Refugee and Migr.d: Presidential emergency fund providing rapid-response humanitarian assistance for unexpected refugee crises worldwide, administered through the State Department.
United States Emergency Refugee and Migr.b: Refugees and displaced persons worldwide facing sudden humanitarian emergencies

Contributions to International Organizat.d: Pays assessed U.S. membership dues and contributions to international organizations such as the United Nations, NATO, WHO, and other multilateral bodies.
Contributions to International Organizat.b: International organizations; indirectly, global populations benefiting from multilateral cooperation

Clean Energy Demonstrations.d: Created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (Bipartisan Infrastructure Law) to fund large-scale demonstrations of clean energy technologies including clean hydrogen hubs, carbon capture, advanced nuclear, and long-duration energy storage.
Clean Energy Demonstrations.b: Clean energy technology developers, utilities, communities near demonstration projects, workers in emerging energy sectors

Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities.d: Funds the Government National Mortgage Association’s guarantee of mortgage-backed securities backed by federally insured or guaranteed loans (FHA, VA, USDA), ensuring capital flows into housing finance markets.
Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities.b: Homebuyers using FHA/VA/USDA loans, mortgage lenders, investors in government-backed MBS

Treasury Franchise Fund.d: A revolving fund providing shared administrative services (IT, HR, procurement, financial management) on a fee-for-service basis to Treasury bureaus and other federal agencies.
Treasury Franchise Fund.b: Treasury bureaus and other federal agencies purchasing shared services

Health Workforce.d: Supports training, education, and placement of health professionals in underserved areas through programs like the National Health Service Corps, nursing workforce development, and graduate medical education.
Health Workforce.b: Health professional students and trainees, medically underserved communities, rural health facilities

Consumer Financial Protection Bureau.d: Independent bureau created by the Dodd-Frank Act to regulate consumer financial products and services, enforce fair lending laws, and educate consumers. Funded through Federal Reserve transfers, not congressional appropriations.
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau.b: American consumers using financial products including mortgages, credit cards, student loans, and banking services

Veterans Housing Benefit Program Fund.d: Administers VA home loan guarantees enabling veterans to obtain mortgages with favorable terms (no down payment, no PMI). Originally created under the GI Bill.
Veterans Housing Benefit Program Fund.b: Veterans, active-duty service members, eligible surviving spouses purchasing or refinancing homes

Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities.d: Second budget line for Ginnie Mae’s MBS guarantee program, likely reflecting a different fiscal year or subsidy reestimate.

Working Capital Fund, Departmental Manag.d: A revolving fund providing centralized administrative services (IT, facilities, procurement) to offices within a department on a reimbursable basis, eliminating duplication of support functions.
Working Capital Fund, Departmental Manag.b: Departmental offices and bureaus receiving consolidated administrative support

Marketing Services, Agricultural Marketi.d: Funds the AMS’s grading, inspection, certification, and market news services for agricultural commodities, including organic certification, commodity purchasing for federal feeding programs, and market oversight.
Marketing Services, Agricultural Marketi.b: Farmers, food processors, consumers, school lunch programs, commodity purchasers

Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Pr.d: Funds CDC programs targeting chronic diseases including heart disease, cancer, diabetes, obesity, and tobacco use through surveillance, research, community-based prevention, and state grants.
Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Pr.b: General public, particularly populations at risk for chronic diseases; state and local health departments

Food Safety and Inspection Service.d: Funds FSIS’s mandatory continuous inspection of domestic and imported meat, poultry, and processed egg products to ensure they are safe, wholesome, and correctly labeled.
Food Safety and Inspection Service.b: American consumers; meat, poultry, and egg product industries

Management Fund, National Institutes of.d: Provides centralized funding for NIH-wide administrative support, facilities management, IT infrastructure, and cross-cutting research support services across the 27 institutes and centers.
Management Fund, National Institutes of.b: NIH institutes and centers; indirectly, biomedical researchers and patients benefiting from research

State and Private Forestry.d: Funds Forest Service programs that provide technical and financial assistance to state, tribal, and private landowners for forest management, wildfire prevention, urban forestry, and forest health protection.
State and Private Forestry.b: State forestry agencies, private forest landowners, tribal nations, urban communities

Operation and Maintenance, Navy Reserve.d: Funds day-to-day operating costs of Navy Reserve forces including training, equipment maintenance, base operations, facilities upkeep, and logistics support for reserve readiness.
Operation and Maintenance, Navy Reserve.b: Navy Reserve personnel and units maintaining combat readiness

Telecommunications Relay Services Fund.d: Established under the Americans with Disabilities Act, funds the Telecommunications Relay Service that enables people with hearing or speech disabilities to communicate by telephone through relay operators and modern IP-based services.

Tenant-Based Rental Assistance (Section.d: Funds the Housing Choice Voucher program providing portable rental subsidies to very low-income families, enabling them to rent privately owned housing by paying the difference between 30% of tenant income and fair market rent.
Tenant-Based Rental Assistance (Section.b: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals renting in the private market

HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, STDs, and TB.d: Funds CDC’s surveillance, prevention, testing, and public health response programs for HIV/AIDS, viral hepatitis B and C, sexually transmitted diseases, and tuberculosis.
HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, STDs, and TB.b: Populations at risk for or living with HIV/AIDS, viral hepatitis, STDs, and TB; state and local health departments

Innovation and Improvement, Education.d: Funds competitive grant programs supporting educational innovation, charter schools, magnet schools, evidence-based interventions, and teacher/principal quality improvement initiatives within the Department of Education.
Innovation and Improvement, Education.b: K-12 students, charter and magnet schools, teachers, school districts piloting innovative education approaches

Salaries and Expenses, Small Business Ad.d: Covers SBA’s administrative costs including staff salaries, district office operations, counseling services, and oversight of loan programs, contracting assistance, and entrepreneurial development.
Salaries and Expenses, Small Business Ad.b: Small business owners and entrepreneurs accessing SBA services, counseling, and loan programs

Forest Service Operations.d: Funds the administrative and operational costs of the U.S. Forest Service including regional and national office operations, strategic planning, law enforcement, and management of 193 million acres of National Forest System lands.
Forest Service Operations.b: General public using national forests, rural communities, Forest Service workforce

National Surface Transportation Finance.d: Created under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act to manage innovative financing tools for surface transportation projects, including TIFIA loans and other credit assistance from the Highway Trust Fund.
National Surface Transportation Finance.b: State and local transportation agencies, toll authorities, transit agencies using federal credit assistance for infrastructure projects

Procurement, Construction, and Improveme.d: Funds the Coast Guard’s acquisition of vessels, aircraft, shore facilities, and other capital assets needed for maritime safety, security, and environmental protection missions.
Procurement, Construction, and Improveme.b: Coast Guard personnel; maritime industries; coastal communities protected by Coast Guard operations

Career, Technical, and Adult Education.d: Funds state formula grants under the Perkins Act for career and technical education, as well as adult education and literacy programs under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act.
Career, Technical, and Adult Education.b: High school and postsecondary CTE students, adult learners, workforce development programs

Financial Agent Services, Bureau of the.d: Reimburses financial institutions and Federal Reserve Banks that serve as fiscal agents of the U.S. government, processing payments, collections, and managing Treasury securities.
Financial Agent Services, Bureau of the.b: Federal agencies using fiscal agent services; indirectly, taxpayers and Treasury security holders

Fossil Energy, Energy Programs.d: Funds research, development, and demonstration of fossil energy technologies including carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), natural gas technologies, and unconventional oil and gas resource characterization.
Fossil Energy, Energy Programs.b: Energy industry researchers, fossil fuel sector workers, communities near fossil energy infrastructure

Native American Programs, HUD.d: Funds the Indian Housing Block Grant (IHBG) and other programs providing housing assistance to American Indian and Alaska Native families through the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act (NAHASDA).
Native American Programs, HUD.b: American Indian and Alaska Native families and tribal housing entities

Funds for Strengthening Markets, Income,.d: Uses customs receipts to purchase surplus agricultural commodities for domestic feeding programs, support farm prices, and fund USDA nutrition assistance. Authorized under Section 32 of the Act of August 24, 1935.
Funds for Strengthening Markets, Income,.b: Farmers facing surplus commodity prices, school lunch and food bank recipients

International Narcotics Control and Law.d: Funds State Department programs to combat international drug trafficking, strengthen foreign criminal justice systems, support counternarcotics operations, and build law enforcement capacity in partner nations.
International Narcotics Control and Law.b: Foreign law enforcement and judicial institutions; U.S. communities benefiting from reduced drug trafficking

General Fund Payment to National Surface.d: Covers upward subsidy reestimates for federal credit programs (like TIFIA) administered through the Highway Trust Fund, reflecting revised cost projections for outstanding transportation loans.
General Fund Payment to National Surface.b: Highway Trust Fund solvency; state and local transportation project sponsors

Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Ai.d: Funds modernization of the national airspace system including air traffic control facilities, navigation equipment, communication systems, and airport surveillance technology.
Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Ai.b: Airlines, general aviation, air travelers, air traffic controllers

Scientific and Technical Research and Se.d: Funds core research and measurement science programs at the National Institute of Standards and Technology, including development of standards, calibration services, cybersecurity frameworks, and advanced manufacturing research.
Scientific and Technical Research and Se.b: U.S. industries relying on measurement standards, technology companies, cybersecurity practitioners, manufacturers

Crime Victims Fund.d: Established by the Victims of Crime Act (VOCA), funded by federal criminal fines and penalties (not taxpayer dollars), provides grants to states for victim compensation and assistance programs.
Crime Victims Fund.b: Victims of federal and state crimes; domestic violence shelters, rape crisis centers, child abuse programs

Assessment Funds, Office of the Comptrol.d: Funds OCC operations through assessments levied on national banks and federal savings associations that OCC supervises — not taxpayer funded. Covers bank examination, supervision, and regulation.

HHS Service and Supply Fund.d: An intragovernmental revolving fund through which the HHS Program Support Center provides shared services (accounting, grants management, IT, human resources) to HHS operating divisions on a reimbursable basis.

Military Personnel, Space Force.d: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, permanent change of station moves, and other personnel costs for United States Space Force military members, the newest branch of the armed forces.
Military Personnel, Space Force.b: Active-duty Space Force guardians and their families

National Railroad Retirement Investment.d: Independent trust that manages and invests railroad retirement assets in a diversified portfolio of stocks, bonds, and other financial instruments to fund benefits for retired railroad workers.

STEM Education, National Science Foundat.d: Funds NSF programs that strengthen science, technology, engineering, and mathematics education at all levels, including K-12, undergraduate, and graduate education, teacher training, and broadening participation.
STEM Education, National Science Foundat.b: STEM students and educators at all levels, underrepresented minorities in STEM fields

Research and Education Activities, Natio.d: Funds competitive and capacity grants for agricultural research and higher education through NIFA, including the Agriculture and Food Research Initiative (AFRI), 1862/1890 land-grant university capacity funds, and research fellowships.

Maternal and Child Health.d: HRSA-administered Title V Maternal and Child Health Block Grant and related programs providing health services, infrastructure, and systems-building for mothers, infants, children, and adolescents, including children with special health care needs.

U.S. Coronavirus Refundable Credits.d: Funds outlays for COVID-19-related refundable tax credits including Employee Retention Credits, paid sick/family leave credits, and recovery rebates enacted under CARES Act, ARPA, and related legislation.

Treasury Forfeiture Fund.d: Receives proceeds from forfeitures by Treasury law enforcement bureaus (IRS-CI, Secret Service, CBP) and funds law enforcement operations, victim restitution, and equitable sharing with state/local partners.

Foreign Service Retirement and Disabilit.d: Mandatory funding for the retirement and disability pension system for Foreign Service officers and certain other State Department employees, analogous to the military retirement system.
Foreign Service Retirement and Disabilit.b: Retired Foreign Service officers and their survivors

Medical Services, Veterans Health Admini.d: Funds core inpatient and outpatient health care services delivered through the VA’s network of medical centers, clinics, and Vet Centers to eligible veterans.
Medical Services, Veterans Health Admini.b: Enrolled veterans receiving VA health care

Executive Office of the President.d: Funds the White House Office, National Security Council, Office of Management and Budget, Council of Economic Advisers, and other EOP entities supporting presidential decision-making and policy coordination.

Quality Improvement Organizations, CMS.d: Funds contracts with Quality Improvement Organizations (QIOs) that review medical care provided to Medicare beneficiaries to ensure quality, appropriateness, and necessity of services.
Quality Improvement Organizations, CMS.b: Medicare beneficiaries; health care providers receiving quality improvement technical assistance

Federal Aid to Wildlife Restoration (Pit.d: Distributes excise taxes on firearms, ammunition, and archery equipment to states for wildlife habitat restoration, hunter education, and shooting range development under the Pittman-Robertson Act.
Federal Aid to Wildlife Restoration (Pit.b: State fish and wildlife agencies, hunters, recreational shooters, wildlife populations

Procurement, Acquisition and Constructio.d: Funds NOAA’s acquisition of satellites, ships, aircraft, supercomputers, and construction of facilities essential for weather forecasting, ocean monitoring, and environmental observation.
Procurement, Acquisition and Constructio.b: General public (weather forecasts), maritime industries, fisheries, environmental researchers

Contributions for International Peacekee.d: Pays the U.S. assessed share of United Nations peacekeeping operations worldwide, funding uniformed peacekeepers, civilian staff, and logistics for UN missions in conflict zones.
Contributions for International Peacekee.b: Populations in conflict zones protected by UN peacekeeping missions; the international peace and security framework

Home Investment Partnerships Program (HO.d: Provides formula grants to states and localities for building, buying, or rehabilitating affordable housing or providing rental assistance to low-income households. Created under the Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act.

Procurement, Construction, and Improveme.d: Funds CBP’s acquisition of border surveillance technology, non-intrusive inspection equipment, facilities construction, tactical communications, and vehicle fleet upgrades.
Procurement, Construction, and Improveme.b: CBP agents and officers; border communities; national security

Ready Reserve Force, Maritime Administra.b: Department of Defense (sealift capacity), national defense logistics

Housing for the Elderly (Section 202).d: Provides capital advances and project rental assistance to nonprofit sponsors for the construction and operation of supportive housing for very low-income elderly persons.

STEM Education, NSF.d: Second budget line for NSF STEM education programs.
