National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and.b: Researchers, clinicians treating alcohol use disorders, individuals and families affected by alcohol misuse

Contingency Fund for State Welfare Progr.d: Provides supplemental TANF funding to states experiencing economic downturns or caseload increases, authorized by the 1996 welfare reform law (Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act).
Contingency Fund for State Welfare Progr.b: States facing rising welfare caseloads, low-income families with children receiving TANF

Extension Activities, National Institute.d: Supports the Cooperative Extension System, delivering research-based education through land-grant universities to farmers, ranchers, communities, and youth (4-H) on agriculture, nutrition, natural resources, and community development.
Extension Activities, National Institute.b: Farmers, rural communities, 4-H youth, land-grant universities, consumers

Public Broadcasting Fund, Corporation fo.d: Federal appropriation to the Corporation for Public Broadcasting distributing funds to local public television and radio stations for programming, operations, interconnection, and the Ready To Learn educational media initiative.
Public Broadcasting Fund, Corporation fo.b: Local PBS and NPR member stations, public media audiences, rural/underserved communities

Administrative Services Franchise Fund,.d: Revolving fund enabling FAA to deliver shared administrative services (accounting, HR, IT, procurement) to FAA lines of business and external federal customers on a fee-for-service basis.
Administrative Services Franchise Fund,.b: FAA organizational units, other federal agencies purchasing FAA shared services

Promoting Safe and Stable Families, Admi.d: Formula grants to states for family preservation, family reunification, adoption promotion, and support services preventing child abuse and unnecessary foster care placements under Title IV-B, subpart 2 of the Social Security Act.
Promoting Safe and Stable Families, Admi.b: At-risk families, children in or near the child welfare system, state child welfare agencies, adoptive families

National Institute on Minority Health an.d: Leads and supports research to understand and eliminate health disparities affecting racial/ethnic minorities, rural populations, sexual/gender minorities, and socioeconomically disadvantaged groups, studying social determinants of health.
National Institute on Minority Health an.b: Health disparities researchers, minority populations, rural communities, medically underserved groups

Interagency Crime and Drug Enforcement,.d: Funds the Organized Crime Drug Enforcement Task Forces (OCDETF) coordinating investigations and prosecutions by DEA, FBI, ATF, IRS-CI, and U.S. Attorneys targeting major drug trafficking organizations and money laundering.
Interagency Crime and Drug Enforcement,.b: Federal law enforcement agencies, communities affected by drug trafficking, U.S. Attorneys

Facilities and Equipment, Federal Aviati.d: Procurement, installation, and sustainment of FAA air traffic control systems, navigation equipment, weather systems, and communications infrastructure from general fund appropriations (separate from Trust Fund F&E).
Facilities and Equipment, Federal Aviati.b: National Airspace System, air traffic controllers, airlines, general aviation, flying public

National Institute on Deafness and Other.d: Funds research on hearing, balance, taste, smell, voice, speech, and language disorders, supporting studies on cochlear implants, hearing loss prevention, auditory processing, and communication rehabilitation.
National Institute on Deafness and Other.b: People with hearing/speech/language/balance disorders, audiologists, speech-language pathologists, researchers

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Energy Re.d: Funds FERC’s regulation of interstate electricity transmission, wholesale power markets, natural gas pipelines, LNG terminals, and hydropower licensing. Costs are fully offset by fees on regulated entities.
Salaries and Expenses, Federal Energy Re.b: Energy consumers, electric utilities, natural gas pipeline operators, hydropower licensees, energy market participants

Research and Education Activities, Natio.d: Funds competitive research grants (Agriculture and Food Research Initiative - AFRI), capacity grants to land-grant universities, and higher education programs in agriculture, food science, natural resources, and bioenergy.
Research and Education Activities, Natio.b: Agricultural researchers, land-grant universities, graduate students, farmers benefiting from applied research

National Institute of Dental and Craniof.d: Supports research on dental caries, periodontal disease, oral cancer, cleft lip/palate, TMJ disorders, salivary gland dysfunction, and craniofacial development and regeneration.
National Institute of Dental and Craniof.b: Dental patients, oral health researchers, dentists, oral surgeons, individuals with craniofacial conditions

Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Ho.d: Funds evidence-based home visiting programs (Nurse-Family Partnership, Parents as Teachers, Healthy Families America) for at-risk pregnant women and parents with young children, supporting health, parenting, and child development.
Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Ho.b: At-risk pregnant women, new parents, infants and toddlers, tribal communities, home visiting workforce

National Library of Medicine, NIH, HHS.d: Operates the world’s largest biomedical library and major databases: PubMed/MEDLINE, ClinicalTrials.gov, MedlinePlus, GenBank (NCBI), and funds biomedical informatics and data science research.
National Library of Medicine, NIH, HHS.b: Biomedical researchers, clinicians, public health professionals, patients, bioinformatics community, global health information access

Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy.d: Funds high-risk, high-reward research in transformative energy technologies too early for private investment: advanced batteries, grid-scale storage, fusion, carbon removal, sustainable fuels, and novel generation concepts.
Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy.b: Energy researchers, startups, national laboratories, clean energy innovation, long-term U.S. energy competitiveness

Consolidated Rail Infrastructure and Saf.d: CRISI competitive grants for rail projects: track rehabilitation, grade crossing safety, positive train control, short-line upgrades, rail safety technology, and passenger rail improvements.
Consolidated Rail Infrastructure and Saf.b: Freight railroads, short-line/regional railroads, Amtrak, commuter rail, communities along rail corridors

National Institute of Biomedical Imaging.d: Funds research integrating engineering with life sciences: advanced imaging technologies (MRI, optical, ultrasound), biosensors, point-of-care diagnostics, tissue engineering, drug delivery, and computational modeling for medicine.
National Institute of Biomedical Imaging.b: Biomedical engineers, medical device developers, clinicians using advanced diagnostics, patients benefiting from imaging/device innovations

CDC-Wide Activities and Program Support,.d: Cross-cutting CDC account for public health infrastructure, laboratory science, data modernization, IT systems, workforce development, global health security, buildings/facilities, and administrative support across all CDC centers.
CDC-Wide Activities and Program Support,.b: National public health infrastructure, state/local/tribal health departments, global health security, CDC operations

Community Service Employment for Older A.d: Funds the Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP): subsidized part-time community service jobs and training for low-income persons 55+ to transition to unsubsidized employment, administered through national and state grantees.
Community Service Employment for Older A.b: Low-income Americans 55+, host community organizations receiving senior workers, aging workforce integration

National Cemetery Administration, Vetera.d: Duplicate entry for the account operating national cemeteries providing burial benefits to eligible veterans and family members.
National Cemetery Administration, Vetera.b: Eligible veterans and dependents, surviving families

Procurement, Acquisition and Constructio.d: Finances NOAA capital assets: weather satellites (GOES-R, JPSS), research/survey vessels, hurricane hunter aircraft, high-performance computing, and facility construction for weather, ocean, and fisheries missions.
Procurement, Acquisition and Constructio.b: Weather forecasting, satellite data users, maritime community, fisheries enforcement, climate observation

Operation of Indian Education Programs,.d: Funds the Bureau of Indian Education’s operation of ~183 schools and dormitories serving approximately 46,000 Native American students on or near reservations, plus tribally controlled postsecondary schools support.
Operation of Indian Education Programs,.b: Native American students in BIE schools, tribal education systems, tribal communities

Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of.d: BIA’s main operating account funding federal trust responsibilities to 574 federally recognized tribes: tribal governance support, natural resource management, law enforcement, social services, real estate services, and economic development.
Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of.b: Federally recognized tribes, tribal members on reservations, tribal courts, Indian country law enforcement

Customs and Border Protection, Transfer.d: CBP operations in Puerto Rico covering customs inspection, trade enforcement, and agricultural quarantine at Puerto Rican ports of entry, historically funded through transferred collections.
Customs and Border Protection, Transfer.b: Puerto Rico trade and travel, CBP enforcement operations in Puerto Rico, agricultural protection

Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Con.d: Provides cash (liquidating appropriations) from the Highway Trust Fund to reimburse states for expenditures on authorized Federal-Aid Highway projects, converting contract authority into Treasury disbursements.
Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Con.b: State DOTs receiving federal highway reimbursements, highway construction, motoring public

Natural Resource Damage Assessment and R.d: Funds Interior’s trustee role assessing natural resource injuries from oil spills and hazardous substance releases, and restoring injured resources using damages recovered from responsible parties under CERCLA and OPA.
Natural Resource Damage Assessment and R.b: Injured natural resources (fish, wildlife, habitats), affected communities, restoration ecology

Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturi.d: Direct loans to auto manufacturers and suppliers for retooling facilities to produce advanced technology vehicles (EVs, hybrids, fuel-efficient vehicles) and components, authorized by EISA 2007 and expanded by IRA 2022.
Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturi.b: Auto manufacturers, EV/advanced vehicle companies, auto workers, advanced vehicle component suppliers

Capital Magnet Fund, CDFI, Departmental.d: Competitive awards to CDFIs and qualified nonprofits leveraging private investment (minimum 10:1 ratio) for affordable housing development/preservation and related community development activities in underserved areas.
Capital Magnet Fund, CDFI, Departmental.b: Low-income families needing affordable housing, CDFIs, nonprofit housing developers, underserved communities

Working Capital Fund, NASA.d: Revolving fund providing shared IT services, scientific/engineering workstations, telecommunications, and administrative support across NASA centers on a reimbursable basis.
Working Capital Fund, NASA.b: NASA centers, mission support operations, agency-wide IT infrastructure

Contract Support Costs, BIA/BIE, Interio.d: Reimburses federally recognized tribes for administrative overhead costs (indirect costs) of operating federal programs assumed under Indian Self-Determination Act (PL 93-638) contracts and compacts, as mandated by statute and court rulings.
Contract Support Costs, BIA/BIE, Interio.b: Self-determination tribes, tribal administrative capacity, programs transferred from BIA to tribal operation

Salaries and Expenses, Federal Payment t.d: Federal appropriation covering judicial salaries, court security, technology, and operating expenses of the District of Columbia court system (Superior Court and Court of Appeals).
Salaries and Expenses, Federal Payment t.b: D.C. judges, court staff, D.C. justice system, litigants and residents of the District of Columbia

Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Pl.d: Additional year/sub-account entry for HUD’s homelessness programs including Continuum of Care, Emergency Solutions Grants, and related assistance.
Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Pl.b: People experiencing homelessness, shelters, permanent supportive housing

Natural Resource Damage Assessment and R.d: Additional entry for Interior’s assessment and restoration of natural resources injured by contamination events.

Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturi.d: Additional year entry for ATVM loans to auto manufacturers retooling for advanced vehicles.
Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturi.b: Auto manufacturers, EV sector, advanced vehicle workers

Working Capital Fund, Bureau of Land Man.d: Additional entry for BLM’s revolving fund providing centralized services.
Working Capital Fund, Bureau of Land Man.b: BLM field operations, public land management

Children and Families Services Programs,.d: Additional entry for ACF discretionary programs serving vulnerable children and families.
Children and Families Services Programs,.b: Low-income families, at-risk youth, domestic violence survivors

Salaries and Expenses, Foreign Agricultu.d: Additional entry for FAS overseas operations and agricultural trade promotion.
Salaries and Expenses, Foreign Agricultu.b: U.S. agricultural trade promotion, farmers, exporters

Management Fund, NIH (batch 6 entry).d: Additional entry for NIH shared services revolving fund.
Management Fund, NIH (batch 6 entry).b: NIH Institutes and Centers, administrative operations

Capital Magnet Fund, CDFI, Treasury (bat.d: Additional entry for the affordable housing leveraged investment program.

Working Capital Fund, Departmental Manag.d: Additional entry for Commerce Department shared administrative services.

Nuclear Energy, Energy Programs, Energy.d: Additional entry for nuclear energy research and development.

Buildings and Facilities, NIH, HHS (batc.d: Additional entry for NIH campus construction and renovation.

National Sea-Based Deterrence Fund, Navy.d: Additional entry for Columbia-class submarine procurement.

Payments to the Patient-Centered Outcome.d: Additional entry for PCORI Trust Fund mandatory payments.
Payments to the Patient-Centered Outcome.b: Clinical effectiveness research, patients, health system evidence

Working Capital Fund, NASA (batch 6 entr.d: Additional entry for NASA shared IT and administrative services.

Contract Support Costs, BIA/BIE, Interio.d: Additional entry for tribal self-determination contract overhead reimbursement.

Emergency Rental Assistance, Departmenta.d: Provides emergency rental assistance to households unable to pay rent or utilities due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Funds are distributed through state, local, and tribal governments.
Emergency Rental Assistance, Departmenta.b: Low-income renters and landlords facing pandemic-related financial hardship

Emergency Watershed Protection Program,.d: Provides funding to safeguard lives and property from floods, drought, and the effects of erosion caused by natural disasters. Helps communities recover from watershed emergencies.
Emergency Watershed Protection Program,.b: Rural landowners, farmers, and communities affected by natural disasters impacting watersheds

Emerging and Zoonotic Infectious Disease.d: Supports surveillance, prevention, and control of infectious diseases that emerge from animal-to-human transmission or other novel pathways. Funds laboratory capacity and outbreak response.
Emerging and Zoonotic Infectious Disease.b: General public, public health agencies, and healthcare providers

Energy Assistance Payments, Rural Busine.d: Provides payments to rural energy providers and cooperatives to help reduce energy costs for rural communities. Supports energy efficiency improvements in rural areas.
Energy Assistance Payments, Rural Busine.b: Rural electric cooperatives, rural businesses, and rural households

Energy Employees Occupational Illness Co.d: Provides lump-sum compensation and medical benefits to current and former employees of the Department of Energy and its contractors who became ill due to exposure to radiation, beryllium, or silica. Administered under the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act.
Energy Employees Occupational Illness Co.b: Former DOE employees, contractors, and uranium workers (or their survivors) with occupational illnesses

Energy Security and Infrastructure Moder.d: Finances modernization of U.S. energy infrastructure to enhance reliability, resilience, and security against physical and cyber threats. Created under the Inflation Reduction Act and related energy legislation.
Energy Security and Infrastructure Moder.b: Energy utilities, grid operators, and communities dependent on energy infrastructure

Energy Security, Internal Revenue Servic.d: Administers energy-related tax credits and incentives under the Inflation Reduction Act, including clean energy production and investment tax credits. Ensures compliance with energy tax provisions.
Energy Security, Internal Revenue Servic.b: Clean energy producers, businesses investing in renewable energy, and consumers receiving energy tax credits

Enhanced Inspectional Services, U.S. Cus.d: Funds overtime and enhanced customs inspection services at ports of entry requested by private sector stakeholders. Costs are reimbursed by the requesting parties.
Enhanced Inspectional Services, U.S. Cus.b: International trade entities, airports, seaports, and border communities requiring additional customs processing capacity

Environmental Compliance and Restoration.d: Funds the investigation, assessment, and remediation of environmental contamination at current and former Coast Guard facilities. Ensures compliance with federal and state environmental laws.
Environmental Compliance and Restoration.b: Communities near contaminated Coast Guard sites and the environment

Environmental Dispute Resolution Fund, M.d: Provides funding for the U.S. Institute for Environmental Conflict Resolution to assist in resolving federal environmental disputes through mediation and collaboration. Helps avoid costly litigation.
Environmental Dispute Resolution Fund, M.b: Federal agencies, state and local governments, tribes, and private parties involved in environmental disputes

Environmental Improvement and Restoratio.d: Receives and distributes funds from Outer Continental Shelf oil and gas leasing revenues for coastal and marine environmental improvement. Supports habitat restoration and conservation efforts.
Environmental Improvement and Restoratio.b: Coastal communities, marine ecosystems, and fisheries

Environmental Restoration, Army.d: Funds the cleanup of contamination from past military activities at Army installations, formerly used defense sites, and Base Realignment and Closure locations. Addresses hazardous waste and unexploded ordnance.
Environmental Restoration, Army.b: Communities near current and former Army installations, and the environment

Environmental Reviews, Energy Programs,.d: Conducts environmental impact assessments and compliance reviews for energy projects under the National Environmental Policy Act. Ensures DOE activities meet environmental requirements.
Environmental Reviews, Energy Programs,.b: Communities affected by energy projects, environmental stakeholders, and project developers

Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institut.d: Conducts and supports research on fertility, pregnancy, childhood development, and rehabilitation science. Studies birth defects, intellectual and developmental disabilities, and demographic and behavioral processes.
Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institut.b: Children, pregnant women, families, and individuals with developmental disabilities

Executive Office for Immigration Review,.d: Houses the immigration court system that adjudicates immigration cases including removal proceedings and asylum claims. Includes the Board of Immigration Appeals.
Executive Office for Immigration Review,.b: Immigrants, asylum seekers, and respondents in immigration court proceedings

Executive Offices, Management and Admini.d: Funds the executive leadership and management offices of the Department of Housing and Urban Development. Supports the Secretary and senior officials in policy direction and oversight.
Executive Offices, Management and Admini.b: HUD leadership and staff, and indirectly all HUD program beneficiaries

Expenses and Refunds, Inspection of Farm.d: Funds the inspection of meat, poultry, and egg products to ensure they are safe, wholesome, and properly labeled. Covers related expenses and refunds.
Expenses and Refunds, Inspection of Farm.b: Consumers, meatpackers, poultry processors, and the food industry

Expenses of Transportation Audit Contrac.d: Funds auditing of freight and passenger transportation bills paid by the federal government to ensure carriers are paid correctly. Recovers overcharges and deductions.
Expenses of Transportation Audit Contrac.b: Federal agencies and taxpayers through cost recovery on transportation expenditures

Expenses, Brush Disposal, Forest Service.d: Covers the costs of disposing of brush and other debris from timber sales and forest management activities on national forests. Reduces wildfire fuel loads.
Expenses, Brush Disposal, Forest Service.b: Communities near national forests and forest ecosystems

Expenses, Customer Protection, Commodity.b: Investors, traders, and participants in futures and derivatives markets

Expenses, Denali Commission.d: Funds the operating expenses of the Denali Commission, an independent federal agency designed to provide critical utilities, infrastructure, and economic support throughout Alaska. Focuses on rural Alaska communities.
Expenses, Denali Commission.b: Remote and rural communities in Alaska

Expenses, Disposal of Surplus Real and R.d: Covers costs associated with the disposal of surplus federal real property and related personal property. Includes appraisals, environmental assessments, and transfer activities.
Expenses, Disposal of Surplus Real and R.b: Federal agencies disposing of surplus property and communities receiving transferred properties

Expenses, Government-Wide Policy, Genera.d: Funds GSA’s development and implementation of government-wide policies for federal property management, travel, fleet, and technology. Promotes efficiency across the federal government.
Expenses, Government-Wide Policy, Genera.b: All federal agencies and taxpayers through improved government operations

Expenses, James Madison Memorial Fellows.d: Covers operating expenses of the foundation that awards fellowships to secondary school teachers to pursue graduate study of the U.S. Constitution. Strengthens constitutional education.
Expenses, James Madison Memorial Fellows.b: Secondary school teachers of American history and government, and their students

Expenses, Office of the Chief Financial.b: USDA programs and offices, and taxpayers through improved financial management

Expenses, Office of the Chief Informatio.d: Supports information technology management and cybersecurity for the Department of Agriculture. Provides IT services, policy guidance, and technology modernization across USDA.
Expenses, Office of the Chief Informatio.b: USDA agencies, employees, and stakeholders who depend on department IT systems

Expenses, Road Maintenance Deposits, Bur.d: Manages deposits from timber purchasers and other users for maintenance and repair of roads on Bureau of Land Management lands. Ensures road infrastructure supports resource management.
Expenses, Road Maintenance Deposits, Bur.b: Timber purchasers, recreational users, and communities with access needs on BLM lands

Expenses, Rural Housing Assistance Grant.d: Provides grants for very low-income rural homeowners to repair and rehabilitate their dwellings, and for rental assistance in rural areas. Helps preserve safe rural housing stock.
Expenses, Rural Housing Assistance Grant.b: Very low-income rural homeowners, elderly rural residents, and rural renters

FDA Innovation, CURES Act, Food and Drug.d: Provides dedicated funding under the 21st Century Cures Act for FDA to advance regulatory science, modernize clinical trials, and accelerate medical product development. Supports innovation in drug and device review.
FDA Innovation, CURES Act, Food and Drug.b: Patients, medical product developers, and the healthcare industry

FDA Working Capital Fund, Food and Drug.d: Operates as a revolving fund to provide shared administrative services to FDA centers and offices on a cost-reimbursable basis. Covers IT, facilities, financial, and human resource services.
FDA Working Capital Fund, Food and Drug.b: FDA organizational components and indirectly the public through efficient FDA operations

FHA-General and Special Risk Insurance F.d: Manages the liquidation of FHA mortgage insurance commitments made before fiscal year 1992 under the General and Special Risk programs. Handles claims, property disposition, and loan servicing.
FHA-General and Special Risk Insurance F.b: Mortgage lenders, borrowers with FHA-insured loans, and the federal government’s financial position

Facilities Management and Construction,.d: Funds construction, maintenance, and management of telecommunications research facilities operated by NTIA. Supports the nation’s spectrum management infrastructure.
Facilities Management and Construction,.b: Federal agencies and telecommunications stakeholders dependent on spectrum management

Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Ai.d: Funds modernization and improvement of the national airspace system, including air traffic control facilities and equipment, funded through the Airport and Airway Trust Fund. Critical for aviation safety and capacity.
Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Ai.b: Airlines, pilots, air travelers, and air traffic controllers

Fair Housing Activities, Fair Housing an.d: Funds enforcement of fair housing laws and programs to prevent discrimination in housing based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or familial status. Supports investigations and education.
Fair Housing Activities, Fair Housing an.b: Individuals and families subjected to housing discrimination

Falcon and Amistad Operating and Mainten.d: Covers the operating and maintenance costs of the Falcon and Amistad dams and hydroelectric power plants on the Rio Grande, jointly operated with Mexico. Revenue is generated from power sales.
Falcon and Amistad Operating and Mainten.b: Electricity consumers in the Texas border region and communities dependent on Rio Grande water management

Family Housing Operation and Maintenance.d: Provides for the operation and maintenance of military family housing serving defense agencies that are not service-specific. Covers shared housing infrastructure costs.
Family Housing Operation and Maintenance.b: Military families of defense agency personnel living in government-provided housing

Farm Production and Conservation Busines.d: Provides centralized business and administrative services (IT, financial management, human resources) to USDA’s farm production and conservation mission area agencies. Improves operational efficiency.
Farm Production and Conservation Busines.b: USDA farm program agencies (FSA, NRCS, RMA) and the farmers they serve

Farm Storage Facility Loans Program Acco.d: Provides low-interest loans to farmers and ranchers to build or upgrade on-farm storage and handling facilities for eligible commodities. Helps producers manage harvest timing and marketing.
Farm Storage Facility Loans Program Acco.b: Farmers and ranchers needing commodity storage and handling infrastructure

Federal Aid to Wildlife Restoration, Uni.b: State fish and wildlife agencies, hunters, and wildlife populations

Federal Assistance, U.S. Citizenship and.d: Funds grants to states and communities for citizenship and integration services, including English language training and civics instruction for immigrants. Supports lawful immigration processing.
Federal Assistance, U.S. Citizenship and.b: Immigrants pursuing citizenship and communities providing integration services

Federal Election Commission.d: Administers and enforces federal campaign finance laws, including disclosure of campaign contributions and expenditures, public funding of presidential elections, and contribution limits. Independent regulatory agency.
Federal Election Commission.b: Voters, political candidates, parties, and political action committees

Federal Financial Institutions Examinati.d: Prescribes uniform principles, standards, and report forms for the federal examination of financial institutions. Coordinates interagency supervisory activities among banking regulators.
Federal Financial Institutions Examinati.b: Financial institutions, banking regulators, and depositors

Federal Housing Administration Fund, Hou.d: Operates the FHA mortgage insurance programs that enable homeownership by insuring lenders against losses on mortgage loans. The largest government mortgage insurance program.
Federal Housing Administration Fund, Hou.b: Homebuyers (especially first-time and low-to-moderate income), mortgage lenders, and the housing market

Federal Interest Liabilities to the Stat.d: Pays interest to states when the federal government is late in transferring grant funds or other payments owed to states. Ensures timely federal financial obligations to states are met.
Federal Interest Liabilities to the Stat.b: State governments awaiting federal fund transfers

Federal Labor Relations Authority.b: Federal employees, federal employee unions, and federal agency management

Federal Land Disposal Account, Bureau of.b: Conservation interests, land trusts, and communities near acquired conservation lands

Federal Maritime Commission.d: Regulates the U.S. international ocean transportation system for the benefit of exporters, importers, and the consumer. Ensures competitive and reliable ocean shipping services.
Federal Maritime Commission.b: U.S. exporters, importers, ocean carriers, and consumers of imported goods

Federal Mediation and Conciliation Servi.d: Provides mediation and conflict resolution services to prevent and resolve labor-management disputes in the private sector, public sector, and international arena. Independent agency promoting industrial peace.
Federal Mediation and Conciliation Servi.b: Employers, labor unions, and workers involved in labor disputes

Federal Mine Safety and Health Review Co.d: Provides adjudicative review of mine safety and health enforcement actions initiated by the Mine Safety and Health Administration. Independent quasi-judicial agency deciding contested cases.
Federal Mine Safety and Health Review Co.b: Mine operators, miners, and mining industry stakeholders

Federal Payment for Emergency Planning a.d: Provides federal payments to the District of Columbia to cover extraordinary costs of emergency planning and security related to the District’s role as the nation’s capital. Covers events of national significance.
Federal Payment for Emergency Planning a.b: District of Columbia government and residents, and visitors to the national capital

Federal Payment for Judicial Commissions.d: Provides federal funding to support the operations of judicial commissions in the District of Columbia, including the Commission on Judicial Disabilities and Tenure. Ensures judicial accountability.
Federal Payment for Judicial Commissions.b: The D.C. court system, litigants, and D.C. residents

Federal Payment for Resident Tuition Sup.d: Funds the DC Tuition Assistance Grant Program (DC TAG), which helps District of Columbia residents attend public universities in any state at in-state tuition rates. Expands higher education access.
Federal Payment for Resident Tuition Sup.b: District of Columbia residents attending out-of-state public colleges and universities

Federal Payment for School Improvement,.d: Provides federal funding to improve educational outcomes in District of Columbia public and charter schools. Supports academic programs and school reform initiatives.
Federal Payment for School Improvement,.b: D.C. public school and charter school students, teachers, and families

Federal Payment for Testing and Treatmen.d: Funds HIV/AIDS testing, treatment, and prevention programs in the District of Columbia. Addresses the disproportionately high HIV/AIDS rates in the District.
Federal Payment for Testing and Treatmen.b: D.C. residents at risk of or living with HIV/AIDS

Federal Payment for Water and Sewer Serv.d: Reimburses the District of Columbia for water and sewer services provided to federal properties in the District. Covers costs that would otherwise be borne by D.C. ratepayers.
Federal Payment for Water and Sewer Serv.b: D.C. Water and Sewer Authority and D.C. ratepayers

Federal Payment for the District of Colu.d: Provides a federal contribution to the District of Columbia National Guard to support its unique responsibilities as the guard force for the nation’s capital. Funds personnel and operational costs.
Federal Payment for the District of Colu.b: D.C. National Guard members and the security of the national capital region

Federal Payment to Morris K. Udall Schol.d: Provides the federal contribution to the Udall Foundation supporting scholarships for students pursuing environmental and Native American health careers. Also funds environmental conflict resolution.
Federal Payment to Morris K. Udall Schol.b: College students in environmental studies and Native American health fields

Federal Payment to the Criminal Justice.d: Funds the D.C. Criminal Justice Coordinating Council to coordinate criminal justice policy and information sharing among D.C. agencies and federal law enforcement. Improves public safety coordination.
Federal Payment to the Criminal Justice.b: D.C. criminal justice agencies, law enforcement, and D.C. residents

Federal Payment to the District of Colum.d: Provides the federal contribution to the retirement annuity fund for survivors of deceased D.C. judges. Ensures continued financial support for judicial families.
Federal Payment to the District of Colum.b: Surviving spouses and dependents of deceased District of Columbia judges

Federal Payment to the District of Colum.d: Provides direct federal capital contributions to DC Water for major infrastructure projects, including combined sewer overflow remediation. Supports clean water compliance.
Federal Payment to the District of Colum.b: DC Water and Sewer Authority and D.C. area residents and waterways

Federal Payment to the Federal District.d: Covers the federal government’s share of pension obligations for D.C. police, firefighters, teachers, and judges hired before 1987. Assumed by the federal government under the 1997 Revitalization Act.
Federal Payment to the Federal District.b: Retired D.C. police officers, firefighters, teachers, and judges hired before 1987

Federal Payments to the Railroad Retirem.d: Transfers federal funds to the Railroad Retirement Trust Fund to cover the portion of railroad retirement benefits equivalent to Social Security. Ensures railroad retirees receive comparable benefits.
Federal Payments to the Railroad Retirem.b: Retired railroad workers and their families

Federal Reserve Bank Reimbursement Fund,.d: Reimburses Federal Reserve Banks for services performed as fiscal agents of the United States, including processing government checks, savings bonds, and Treasury securities.
Federal Reserve Bank Reimbursement Fund,.b: Federal Reserve Banks performing fiscal agency services and Treasury operations

Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance.d: Manages the trust fund financing Medicare Part B (physician and outpatient services) and Part D (prescription drug coverage). Funded by beneficiary premiums and general revenues.
Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance.b: Medicare beneficiaries receiving Part B and Part D benefits

Federal Unemployment Benefits and Allowa.d: Provides trade adjustment assistance and extended unemployment benefits to workers adversely affected by foreign trade, including retraining allowances and relocation assistance under the Trade Act.
Federal Unemployment Benefits and Allowa.b: Workers who have lost jobs due to foreign trade competition

Federal Unemployment Compensation Progra.b: Unemployed former federal civilian workers and military veterans

Financial Agent Services, Bureau of the.d: Reimburses financial institutions designated as financial agents of the federal government for services performed, including processing government payments, collections, and managing federal accounts.
Financial Agent Services, Bureau of the.b: Financial institutions serving as fiscal agents and the efficient operation of federal financial services

Financial Assistance Oversight and Techn.d: Provides oversight of railroad financial assistance programs and technical assistance to railroads and states for rail safety and infrastructure improvements. Supports effective use of federal rail investments.
Financial Assistance Oversight and Techn.b: Railroad operators, states receiving federal rail grants, and rail passengers

Financial Management Capital, Office of.d: Funds modernization of financial management systems and shared services across the Department of Transportation. Ensures accurate financial reporting and compliance.
Financial Management Capital, Office of.b: DOT agencies and the public through improved financial accountability

First Responder Network Authority, Natio.d: Operates FirstNet, the nationwide broadband network dedicated to public safety communications, created by the Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act. Ensures first responders have reliable communications.
First Responder Network Authority, Natio.b: Police, fire, EMS, and other first responders nationwide

Fisheries Disaster Assistance, National.d: Provides financial assistance to fishing communities and fishermen affected by commercial fishery failures or disasters. Helps communities recover from catastrophic fishery losses.
Fisheries Disaster Assistance, National.b: Commercial fishermen, fishing communities, and related businesses affected by fishery disasters

Fisheries Enforcement Asset Forfeiture F.d: Receives proceeds from the forfeiture of property seized in connection with violations of fisheries laws. Funds are used to support fisheries enforcement operations.
Fisheries Enforcement Asset Forfeiture F.b: NOAA fisheries enforcement operations and sustainable fisheries management

Fisheries Finance Program Account, Natio.d: Provides direct loans for the acquisition, construction, or reconstruction of fishing vessels and shoreside facilities, as well as for fisheries refinancing. Supports the fishing industry’s capital needs.
Fisheries Finance Program Account, Natio.b: Commercial fishing vessel owners, aquaculture operators, and fishing shoreside facility operators

Fishermen’s Contingency Fund, National O.d: Compensates commercial fishermen for damage to or loss of fishing gear and vessels caused by oil and gas exploration and development on the Outer Continental Shelf.
Fishermen’s Contingency Fund, National O.b: Commercial fishermen whose gear or vessels are damaged by offshore energy activities

Flexible Benefits Plan Reserve, Office o.d: Maintains the reserve fund for the Federal Flexible Benefits Plan (FEHB-related flexible spending accounts) that allows federal employees to set aside pre-tax dollars for health and dependent care expenses.
Flexible Benefits Plan Reserve, Office o.b: Federal employees participating in flexible spending account programs

Food Supply Chain and Agriculture Pandem.d: Provided pandemic-related assistance to maintain the food supply chain, support agricultural producers, and distribute food to those in need during COVID-19 disruptions.
Food Supply Chain and Agriculture Pandem.b: Farmers, ranchers, food processors, distributors, and food-insecure households

Food and Nutrition, Development Assistan.d: Funds international food and nutrition programs as part of U.S. development assistance to promote food security in developing countries. Supports agricultural development and nutrition initiatives abroad.
Food and Nutrition, Development Assistan.b: Populations in developing countries facing food insecurity and malnutrition

Foreign Military Financing Program, Fund.d: Provides grants and loans to foreign governments for the acquisition of U.S. defense equipment, services, and training. A primary tool of U.S. security assistance policy.
Foreign Military Financing Program, Fund.b: Allied and partner nation militaries purchasing U.S. defense articles and services

Foreign National Employees Separation Li.d: Funds the accrued separation pay liability for foreign national employees of USAID upon their departure from employment. Ensures orderly payment of severance obligations abroad.
Foreign National Employees Separation Li.b: Foreign national employees of USAID who are separated from service

Foreign National Employees Separation Pa.d: Covers separation pay obligations for foreign national employees of the Department of Defense who are terminated from their positions at overseas installations.
Foreign National Employees Separation Pa.b: Foreign national employees of DOD at overseas military installations

Foreign Service National Defined Contrib.d: Manages a defined contribution retirement plan for foreign service national employees (locally employed staff) of the Department of State at overseas posts.
Foreign Service National Defined Contrib.b: Locally employed staff at U.S. embassies and consulates worldwide

Foreign Service National Separation Liab.d: Funds the accrued separation pay liability for foreign national employees of APHIS working at overseas posts, typically on agricultural inspection and quarantine activities.
Foreign Service National Separation Liab.b: Foreign national employees of APHIS who are separated from service

Foreign Service National Separation Liab.d: Covers separation pay liabilities for foreign national employees of the Foreign Agricultural Service at U.S. agricultural trade offices and embassies abroad.
Foreign Service National Separation Liab.b: Locally employed FAS staff at U.S. agricultural offices overseas

Foreign Service National Separation Liab.d: Funds separation pay obligations for locally employed staff of the Department of State at embassies and consulates worldwide. Provides for severance costs when local employees are separated.
Foreign Service National Separation Liab.b: Locally employed staff at U.S. diplomatic missions globally

Foreign Service National Separation Liab.d: Covers accrued separation pay liabilities for foreign national employees of the U.S. Agency for Global Media (formerly Broadcasting Board of Governors) at overseas broadcast facilities.
Foreign Service National Separation Liab.b: Foreign national employees of USAGM-supported broadcast operations abroad

Foreign Service Retirement and Disabilit.d: Provides retirement, disability, and survivor benefits to retired Foreign Service officers and their families. Operates similarly to other federal employee retirement systems.
Foreign Service Retirement and Disabilit.b: Retired Foreign Service officers, their surviving spouses, and dependents

Forest Ecosystems Health and Recovery, B.d: Funds forest health treatments and ecosystem restoration activities on BLM-managed public lands, including hazardous fuels reduction and insect and disease management.
Forest Ecosystems Health and Recovery, B.b: Public lands, communities near fire-prone BLM forests, and forest ecosystems

Formerly Utilized Sites Remedial Action.d: Cleans up sites contaminated with radioactive waste from early nuclear weapons development activities of the Manhattan Project and the Atomic Energy Commission. Known as FUSRAP.
Formerly Utilized Sites Remedial Action.b: Communities near formerly utilized nuclear sites and the environment

Free Clinics Malpractice Claims Fund, He.d: Provides medical malpractice coverage for free clinics and their health care professionals, enabling them to provide care to uninsured patients without the burden of malpractice insurance costs.
Free Clinics Malpractice Claims Fund, He.b: Free clinic operators, volunteer health professionals, and uninsured patients

Funds for Strengthening Markets, Income,.d: Provides permanent appropriations from customs receipts to encourage the domestic consumption of agricultural commodities through purchases, diversion programs, and export subsidies. One of the oldest farm support mechanisms.
Funds for Strengthening Markets, Income,.b: Agricultural producers, food assistance program recipients, and the farm economy

GSE Mortgage-Backed Securities Purchase.d: Established during the 2008 financial crisis to purchase mortgage-backed securities guaranteed by Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and Ginnie Mae to stabilize mortgage markets. Now in wind-down.
GSE Mortgage-Backed Securities Purchase.b: Mortgage borrowers, the housing market, and financial market stability

General Departmental Management, Departm.d: Supports the Secretary of HHS and senior officials in providing department-wide leadership, policy development, and management oversight of all HHS programs and agencies.
General Departmental Management, Departm.b: HHS leadership and the public served by HHS health and human service programs

General Fund Payment To National Surface.d: Covers upward reestimates of credit program costs for surface transportation loans and grants administered through the Highway Trust Fund. Ensures accurate credit subsidy accounting.
General Fund Payment To National Surface.b: State and local transportation agencies receiving federal highway and transit funding

General Fund Payment to the Trade Enforc.d: Transfers general fund resources to support enhanced trade enforcement activities, including monitoring and enforcement of U.S. trade agreements and trade preference programs.
General Fund Payment to the Trade Enforc.b: U.S. industries, workers, and exporters affected by unfair trade practices

General Fund Payment, Armed Forces Retir.d: Provides the federal contribution to the Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund, which operates retirement communities for eligible enlisted military veterans in Washington, D.C. and Gulfport, Mississippi.
General Fund Payment, Armed Forces Retir.b: Retired and disabled enlisted military veterans residing at Armed Forces Retirement Homes

General Gift Fund, USCG, Homeland Securi.d: Receives and expends gifts, bequests, and donations made to the U.S. Coast Guard. Funds may support Coast Guard missions, morale, welfare, and community relations activities.
General Gift Fund, USCG, Homeland Securi.b: Coast Guard personnel, operations, and community programs

General Post Fund, National Homes, Veter.d: Manages funds received from veterans and other sources at VA medical centers and domiciliary facilities for the welfare and comfort of veteran patients and residents. Supports recreation and amenities.
General Post Fund, National Homes, Veter.b: Veteran patients and residents at VA medical centers and domiciliaries

General and Special Risk Program Account.d: Insures mortgages for hospitals, nursing homes, multifamily housing, and other specialized properties under FHA’s General and Special Risk programs. Supports credit-subsidized lending.
General and Special Risk Program Account.b: Hospitals, nursing homes, multifamily housing developers, and their communities

Geothermal Lease Revenues, Payment to Co.d: Distributes a share of federal geothermal energy leasing revenues to counties where geothermal resources are located. Compensates local governments for impacts of energy development.
Geothermal Lease Revenues, Payment to Co.b: County governments in areas with federal geothermal energy development

Gifts and Bequests, Agriculture.d: Receives and administers gifts, bequests, and donations made to the Department of Agriculture for purposes consistent with USDA’s mission. Funds are used as specified by donors.
Gifts and Bequests, Agriculture.b: USDA programs and the agricultural community as designated by donors

Gifts and Bequests, Departmental Managem.d: Accepts and utilizes monetary and in-kind gifts and bequests donated to the Department of Commerce in support of its programs and missions.
Gifts and Bequests, Departmental Managem.b: Commerce Department programs as designated by donors

Gifts and Bequests, Maritime Administrat.d: Receives gifts and bequests for the benefit of the Maritime Administration’s programs supporting the U.S. merchant marine and maritime industry.
Gifts and Bequests, Maritime Administrat.b: The U.S. merchant marine, maritime industry, and maritime training programs

Gifts and Bequests, Treasury.d: Accepts monetary gifts and bequests to the Department of the Treasury for purposes specified by donors or for the general benefit of Treasury operations.
Gifts and Bequests, Treasury.b: Treasury programs and operations as directed by donors

Gifts and Contributions, Corporation for.d: Receives gifts and contributions from individuals and organizations to support AmeriCorps and other national service programs. Supplements appropriated funds for community service.
Gifts and Contributions, Corporation for.b: National service programs, AmeriCorps members, and communities they serve

Gifts and Contributions, Peace Corps.d: Accepts gifts and contributions to support Peace Corps volunteer activities and programs in developing countries. Supplements appropriated funds for international development and service.
Gifts and Contributions, Peace Corps.b: Peace Corps volunteers and communities they serve in developing nations

Gifts and Donations, African Development.d: Receives gifts and donations to support the African Development Foundation’s mission of providing grants to community-based organizations in Africa for grassroots economic development.
Gifts and Donations, African Development.b: African community organizations and grassroots development initiatives

Gifts and Donations, Agency for Internat.d: Accepts gifts and donations to supplement USAID’s international development and humanitarian assistance programs. Supports partnership between private donors and U.S. foreign assistance.
Gifts and Donations, Agency for Internat.b: International development programs and populations in developing countries

Gifts and Donations, Bureau of Indian Af.d: Receives gifts and donations for the benefit of programs serving American Indian and Alaska Native communities through the Bureau of Indian Affairs.
Gifts and Donations, Bureau of Indian Af.b: American Indian and Alaska Native communities and BIA programs

Gifts and Donations, Bureau of Indian Af.d: Accepts gifts and donations for programs supporting American Indian and Alaska Native education, tribal governance, and natural resource management under BIA and BIE.
Gifts and Donations, Bureau of Indian Af.b: American Indian and Alaska Native tribal members and communities

Gifts and Donations, Centers for Disease.d: Receives gifts and donations from private entities to support CDC’s public health mission including disease prevention, health promotion, and emergency preparedness activities.
Gifts and Donations, Centers for Disease.b: CDC research and public health programs and the general public

Gifts and Donations, Commission for the.d: Accepts gifts and donations to support the Commission’s mission of preserving cemeteries, monuments, and historic buildings in Eastern and Central Europe associated with the American heritage.
Gifts and Donations, Commission for the.b: Preservation of American cultural heritage sites in Eastern and Central Europe

Gifts and Donations, Denali Commission.d: Receives gifts and donations to supplement the Denali Commission’s work providing infrastructure and economic development in remote Alaska communities.
Gifts and Donations, Denali Commission.b: Rural Alaska communities and the Denali Commission’s infrastructure programs

Gifts and Donations, Department Manageme.d: Accepts gifts and donations for use by the Department of Homeland Security in carrying out its missions of border security, disaster response, and counterterrorism.
Gifts and Donations, Department Manageme.b: DHS programs and the public safety mission of the department

Gifts and Donations, Energy.d: Receives gifts and donations to support the Department of Energy’s missions in energy research, nuclear security, and environmental cleanup.
Gifts and Donations, Energy.b: DOE programs in energy, science, and national security

Gifts and Donations, Institute of Museum.d: Accepts gifts and donations to support IMLS programs that strengthen museums and libraries, fund research, and expand access to cultural and educational resources nationwide.
Gifts and Donations, Institute of Museum.b: Museums, libraries, and the communities they serve across the United States

Gifts and Donations, National Endowment.d: Receives gifts and donations from the private sector to supplement NEA grant-making activities supporting arts education, creativity, and public access to the arts.
Gifts and Donations, National Endowment.b: Artists, arts organizations, and communities across the country

Gifts and Donations, National Endowment.d: Accepts gifts and donations to augment NEH’s support for research, education, and public programs in the humanities including history, philosophy, and literature.
Gifts and Donations, National Endowment.b: Scholars, educators, cultural institutions, and the general public interested in the humanities

Gifts and Donations, the White House Con.d: Receives private gifts and donations to support activities related to White House conferences and initiatives addressing drug abuse and drug control policy.
Gifts and Donations, the White House Con.b: Anti-drug abuse programs and drug policy initiatives

Gifts, Crime Victims Fund, Office of Jus.d: Accepts gifts and donations to supplement the Crime Victims Fund, which supports victim assistance and compensation programs through fines and penalties collected from federal offenders.
Gifts, Crime Victims Fund, Office of Jus.b: Victims of crime and victim services organizations

Gifts, Donations and Contributions, Inte.d: Receives gifts, donations, and contributions to support the Inter-American Foundation’s grassroots development programs in Latin America and the Caribbean.
Gifts, Donations and Contributions, Inte.b: Community organizations and grassroots development in Latin America and the Caribbean

Global Agriculture and Food Security Pro.d: Provides the U.S. contribution to the multilateral Global Agriculture and Food Security Program trust fund, which finances agricultural development and food security in low-income countries.
Global Agriculture and Food Security Pro.b: Smallholder farmers and food-insecure populations in developing countries

Global HIV/AIDS Initiative, State.d: Funds the President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) activities managed by the State Department, supporting HIV/AIDS prevention, treatment, and care programs worldwide.
Global HIV/AIDS Initiative, State.b: People living with and at risk of HIV/AIDS in partner countries, primarily in sub-Saharan Africa

Global Security Contingency Fund, State.d: Provides flexible funding for the Departments of State and Defense to respond quickly to emerging security challenges, including training and equipping foreign security forces. Supports security sector assistance.
Global Security Contingency Fund, State.b: Foreign security forces and partner nations facing urgent security threats

Government Payment for Annuitants, Emplo.d: Covers the government’s share of life insurance premiums for retired federal employees and their survivors under the Federal Employees Group Life Insurance program.
Government Payment for Annuitants, Emplo.b: Retired federal employees and survivors receiving government life insurance benefits

Grants for Construction of State Extende.d: Provides matching grants to states for the construction or renovation of state veterans nursing homes, domiciliary facilities, and adult day health care centers.
Grants for Construction of State Extende.b: Veterans in state-operated extended care facilities and state veterans affairs departments

Grants for Construction of State Veteran.d: Provides grants to states for the establishment, expansion, and improvement of state veterans cemeteries. Ensures dignified burial options for veterans closer to their families.
Grants for Construction of State Veteran.b: Veterans, their families, and states operating veterans cemeteries

Grants-In-Aid for Airports, Liquidation.d: Liquidates contract authority obligations for airport improvement grants from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund. Provides cash disbursements for previously authorized airport construction projects.
Grants-In-Aid for Airports, Liquidation.b: Airports receiving federal improvement grants and the traveling public

Grassroots Source Water Protection Progr.d: Provides grants to grassroots organizations working to protect source water areas from contamination in rural communities. Supports local, voluntary, community-driven conservation efforts.
Grassroots Source Water Protection Progr.b: Rural communities, local water utilities, and grassroots conservation organizations

Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Developm.d: Operates and maintains the U.S. portion of the St. Lawrence Seaway, a deep-draft waterway connecting the Great Lakes to the Atlantic Ocean. Supports commercial shipping and navigation.
Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Developm.b: Commercial shipping interests, Great Lakes port communities, and international trade

Green and Resilient Retrofit Program for.d: Provides grants and loans to retrofit HUD-assisted multifamily housing for improved energy efficiency, climate resilience, and indoor air quality. Created under the Inflation Reduction Act.
Green and Resilient Retrofit Program for.b: Residents of HUD-assisted multifamily housing and property owners

Grid Deployment, Energy Programs, Energy.d: Funds deployment of electric grid infrastructure including transmission lines, smart grid technologies, and grid resilience improvements under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
Grid Deployment, Energy Programs, Energy.b: Electric utilities, grid operators, and electricity consumers

Guam World War II Claims Fund, Treasury.d: Provides compensation to residents of Guam who suffered death, personal injury, forced labor, or internment during the Japanese occupation in World War II. Addresses long-standing claims.
Guam World War II Claims Fund, Treasury.b: Guam residents and their survivors who suffered during WWII Japanese occupation

Guaranteed Student Loans, Liquidating Ac.d: Manages the liquidation of pre-1992 Federal Family Education Loan Program guaranteed student loan commitments. Handles defaulted loan collections and lender claims.
Guaranteed Student Loans, Liquidating Ac.b: The federal government through recovery of defaulted student loan funds

Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities.d: Supports Ginnie Mae’s guarantee of timely payment of principal and interest on mortgage-backed securities backed by federally insured or guaranteed loans. Ensures liquidity in the mortgage market.
Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities.b: Mortgage investors, lenders, and homebuyers benefiting from liquid mortgage markets

Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities.d: Accounts for the credit subsidy costs of Ginnie Mae’s mortgage-backed securities guarantee program under FCRA. Supports ongoing government guarantees of MBS pools.
Guarantees of Mortgage-Backed Securities.b: Mortgage lenders issuing Ginnie Mae securities and homebuyers accessing affordable mortgages

Gulf Coast Ecosystem Restoration Science.d: Funds NOAA’s scientific research, monitoring, and technology development to support Gulf Coast ecosystem restoration after the Deepwater Horizon oil spill. Informs restoration planning.
Gulf Coast Ecosystem Restoration Science.b: Gulf Coast ecosystems, fishing communities, and restoration decision-makers

H-1B and L Fraud Prevention and Detectio.d: Receives fraud prevention fees from H-1B and L visa petitions to fund anti-fraud activities and compliance reviews by USCIS. Supports investigations of visa fraud.
H-1B and L Fraud Prevention and Detectio.b: USCIS fraud detection programs and the integrity of the H-1B/L visa system

H-1B and L Fraud Prevention and Detectio.d: Funds the State Department’s share of H-1B and L visa fraud prevention and detection activities at consular posts abroad. Supports pre-screening and compliance monitoring.
H-1B and L Fraud Prevention and Detectio.b: U.S. consular operations and the integrity of the employment visa system

H-1B and L Fraud Protection and Detectio.d: Funds Department of Labor enforcement activities to detect and prevent fraud and abuse in the H-1B and L visa programs, including wage and workplace violations investigations.
H-1B and L Fraud Protection and Detectio.b: U.S. and foreign workers protected from H-1B/L visa fraud and wage abuse

HHS Accrual Contribution to the Uniforme.d: Covers HHS’s accrual contribution for the future health care costs of uniformed service members (Commissioned Corps) currently serving within HHS. Required by the National Defense Authorization Act.
HHS Accrual Contribution to the Uniforme.b: U.S. Public Health Service Commissioned Corps members and future retirees

HHS Service and Supply Fund, Program Sup.d: Operates as a revolving fund providing shared administrative services to HHS agencies including financial management, human resources, IT, and procurement on a fee-for-service basis.
HHS Service and Supply Fund, Program Sup.b: HHS agencies using shared services and the public through efficient HHS operations

HIRE Vets Medallion Award Fund, Departme.d: Funds the HIRE Vets Medallion Program which recognizes employers who recruit, employ, and retain veterans through a tiered award system. Encourages veteran hiring in the private sector.
HIRE Vets Medallion Award Fund, Departme.b: Military veterans seeking employment and employers committed to hiring veterans

HIV/AIDS Working Capital Fund, Agency fo.d: Operates as a revolving fund to provide efficient management and procurement of commodities and services for PEPFAR-related HIV/AIDS programs administered by USAID.
HIV/AIDS Working Capital Fund, Agency fo.b: HIV/AIDS program recipients in developing countries supported by PEPFAR

HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, Sexually Tran.d: Supports CDC’s domestic prevention programs for HIV/AIDS, viral hepatitis, STDs, and tuberculosis through surveillance, testing, partner services, and public health interventions.
HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, Sexually Tran.b: Individuals at risk of or living with HIV, hepatitis, STDs, or TB in the United States

Harbor Maintenance Fee Collection, USCS,.d: Collects the harbor maintenance fee (ad valorem tax) on commercial cargo imported through U.S. ports to fund the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for port and harbor dredging and maintenance.
Harbor Maintenance Fee Collection, USCS,.b: U.S. ports, the shipping industry, and domestic and international trade

Hardwood Technology Transfer and Applied.d: Funds research and technology transfer focused on hardwood forest management and utilization in the eastern United States. Supports sustainable hardwood industry practices.
Hardwood Technology Transfer and Applied.b: Hardwood timber industry, forest landowners, and forest researchers

Harry S Truman Scholarship Foundation.d: Awards merit-based scholarships to college students committed to careers in public service. Provides up to $30,000 for graduate study and leadership development opportunities.
Harry S Truman Scholarship Foundation.b: Outstanding college juniors pursuing careers in government or public service

Harry S. Truman Memorial Scholarship Tru.d: Manages the trust fund whose investment income finances the Truman Scholarship program. The endowment was established by Congress to perpetually fund scholarships for future public servants.
Harry S. Truman Memorial Scholarship Tru.b: Truman Scholars and aspiring public servants

Hazardous Materials Management, Agricult.d: Funds the identification, assessment, and cleanup of hazardous materials contamination at USDA facilities and on USDA-managed lands. Ensures compliance with environmental regulations.
Hazardous Materials Management, Agricult.b: USDA facilities, neighboring communities, and the environment

Hazardous Materials Safety, Pipeline and.d: Regulates the safe transportation of hazardous materials by all modes of transport (highway, rail, air, and water) and funds safety research, training, and emergency response planning.
Hazardous Materials Safety, Pipeline and.b: Communities near hazardous materials transportation routes, shippers, carriers, and first responders

Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest Syst.d: Operates the national electronic manifest system (e-Manifest) for tracking hazardous waste shipments from generation to disposal. Funded by user fees from waste handlers.
Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest Syst.b: Hazardous waste generators, transporters, treatment facilities, and EPA enforcement

Health Centers Malpractice Claims, Healt.d: Provides medical malpractice coverage under the Federal Tort Claims Act for community health centers and their practitioners, reducing insurance costs and enabling care in underserved areas.
Health Centers Malpractice Claims, Healt.b: Federally qualified health center practitioners and the patients they serve

Health Education Assistance Loans Liquid.d: Manages the liquidation of pre-1992 Health Education Assistance Loan commitments, including collections on defaulted loans to students in health professions programs.
Health Education Assistance Loans Liquid.b: The federal government through recovery of defaulted health education loan funds

Health Education Assistance Loans Progra.d: Accounts for post-1991 credit reform costs of the Health Education Assistance Loan program providing federally insured loans to graduate students in health professions schools.
Health Education Assistance Loans Progra.b: Graduate students in medical, dental, veterinary, and other health professions programs

Health Resources and Services, Health Re.d: Funds HRSA programs that improve access to health care for underserved populations, including maternal and child health, organ transplantation, and health workforce development.
Health Resources and Services, Health Re.b: Underserved and vulnerable populations, maternal and child health, and health care providers

Health Surveillance and Program Support,.d: Supports SAMHSA’s data collection, surveillance, and program management activities for behavioral health including the National Survey on Drug Use and Health. Informs substance abuse and mental health policy.
Health Surveillance and Program Support,.b: Behavioral health policymakers, researchers, and populations affected by substance abuse and mental illness

Healthcare Research and Quality, Agency.d: Funds research to improve the quality, safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of health care for all Americans. Supports evidence-based clinical practice guidelines and patient safety initiatives.
Healthcare Research and Quality, Agency.b: Health care providers, patients, health systems, and policymakers

Healthy Foods Financing Initiative, Rura.d: Provides financial assistance to increase access to healthy, affordable food in underserved urban and rural communities, commonly called food deserts. Supports grocery stores and food retail projects.
Healthy Foods Financing Initiative, Rura.b: Residents of food deserts and food retail businesses in underserved communities

Helium Fund, Bureau of Land Management.d: Manages the federal helium reserve and related operations including storage, processing, and sale of crude helium. Transitioning toward privatization of federal helium assets.
Helium Fund, Bureau of Land Management.b: Scientific research institutions, medical facilities, defense and aerospace industries dependent on helium

Hermit’s Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Assistanc.d: Provides claims payments and assistance to individuals, businesses, and communities affected by the Hermit’s Peak/Calf Canyon Fire in New Mexico, which was caused by federal prescribed burn operations.
Hermit’s Peak/Calf Canyon Fire Assistanc.b: Individuals, tribes, businesses, and local governments in New Mexico affected by the 2022 fire

High Energy Cost Grants, Rural Utilities.d: Provides grants to reduce the high cost of energy in rural communities where energy costs significantly exceed the national average. Supports energy generation, transmission, and efficiency projects.
High Energy Cost Grants, Rural Utilities.b: Rural communities with exceptionally high energy costs, particularly in Alaska and remote areas

Higher Education Facilities Loans and In.d: Managed the now-inactive program of direct loans and loan insurance for construction, renovation, and improvement of academic facilities at institutions of higher education.
Higher Education Facilities Loans and In.b: Colleges and universities (program is in liquidation/inactive status)

Highway Traffic Safety Grants, Liquidati.d: Liquidates obligations for highway traffic safety grants from the Highway Trust Fund to states for programs reducing traffic crashes, deaths, and injuries. Covers behavioral safety programs.
Highway Traffic Safety Grants, Liquidati.b: State highway safety offices, drivers, and the traveling public

Highway Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, T.d: Manages the investment and cash flow of the Highway Trust Fund, which finances federal highway and transit programs through motor fuel taxes and other transportation-related fees.
Highway Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, T.b: State and local transportation agencies, highway users, and transit riders

Historically Black College and Universit.d: Provides low-cost capital financing to Historically Black Colleges and Universities for construction, renovation, and infrastructure improvements through federally guaranteed loans.
Historically Black College and Universit.b: HBCUs and their students through improved campus facilities and infrastructure

Home Investment Partnerships Program, Co.d: Provides formula grants to states and localities for a wide range of affordable housing activities including construction, rehabilitation, and rental assistance for low-income households. Known as HOME.
Home Investment Partnerships Program, Co.b: Low-income families, homebuyers, and renters in participating jurisdictions

Home Ownership Preservation Entity Fund.d: Created during the housing crisis to support entities working to prevent foreclosures and preserve homeownership through counseling and loan modification assistance.
Home Ownership Preservation Entity Fund.b: Homeowners facing foreclosure and housing counseling organizations

Homeowner Assistance Fund, Departmental.d: Provides financial assistance to homeowners who experienced financial hardship due to the COVID-19 pandemic, helping with mortgage payments, property taxes, utilities, and insurance. Created by the American Rescue Plan Act.
Homeowner Assistance Fund, Departmental.b: Homeowners at risk of mortgage default or foreclosure due to COVID-19 hardship

Homeowners Assistance Fund - Recovery Ac.d: Expanded the Homeowners Assistance Program under the Recovery Act to help military and civilian DOD personnel who suffered home value losses due to BRAC closures or the economic downturn.
Homeowners Assistance Fund - Recovery Ac.b: Military members and DOD civilians who experienced home value loss near military installations

Homeowners Assistance Fund, Defense.d: Assists DOD military and civilian employees who are unable to sell their homes due to base realignment and closure actions or other military relocations that reduce local property values.
Homeowners Assistance Fund, Defense.b: Military service members and DOD civilians affected by base closures or realignments

Host Country Resident Contractors Separa.d: Funds accrued separation pay liabilities for host country resident contractors employed by the Peace Corps at overseas posts. Ensures proper payment upon contract termination.
Host Country Resident Contractors Separa.b: Host country contractors working for the Peace Corps at overseas posts

Host Nation Support for U.S. Relocation.d: Receives and manages contributions from host nations (primarily Japan) supporting the relocation and realignment of U.S. military forces, particularly in the Pacific region.
Host Nation Support for U.S. Relocation.b: U.S. military forces undergoing relocation and host nation communities

Housing Certificate Fund, Public and Ind.d: Manages legacy Section 8 housing certificate and voucher program funding for project-based and tenant-based rental assistance contracts. Being consolidated into the Housing Choice Voucher program.
Housing Certificate Fund, Public and Ind.b: Low-income tenants receiving Section 8 rental assistance and participating landlords

Housing Counseling Assistance, Housing P.d: Provides grants to HUD-approved housing counseling agencies to deliver homeownership counseling, rental counseling, and foreclosure prevention services to consumers.
Housing Counseling Assistance, Housing P.b: Homebuyers, homeowners, renters, and individuals facing housing challenges

Housing Opportunities for Persons with A.d: Provides housing assistance and supportive services for low-income persons living with HIV/AIDS and their families through grants to states, cities, and nonprofit organizations. Known as HOPWA.
Housing Opportunities for Persons with A.b: Low-income individuals and families living with HIV/AIDS

Housing and Other Credit Guaranty Progra.d: Manages the liquidation of legacy housing and credit guarantee commitments made by the former Overseas Private Investment Corporation in developing countries.
Housing and Other Credit Guaranty Progra.b: The federal government through recovery of overseas housing and credit guarantee obligations

Housing for the Elderly or Handicapped F.d: Manages the legacy loan portfolio for elderly and handicapped housing projects financed under the Section 202 program prior to 1990 reforms. In liquidation mode.
Housing for the Elderly or Handicapped F.b: Elderly and disabled residents of legacy Section 202 housing projects

Housing, Program Office Salaries and Exp.d: Funds the salaries and administrative expenses of HUD’s Office of Housing, which oversees FHA mortgage insurance, multifamily housing programs, and manufactured housing standards.
Housing, Program Office Salaries and Exp.b: HUD Housing Office staff and beneficiaries of HUD housing programs

Human Space Flight, National Aeronautics.d: Funds NASA’s human spaceflight programs including the International Space Station, commercial crew transportation, and deep space exploration initiatives such as Artemis.
Human Space Flight, National Aeronautics.b: Astronauts, the aerospace industry, scientific researchers, and the general public

IT Modernization and Working Capital, Of.d: Funds the modernization of OPM’s legacy information technology systems and provides working capital for IT operations supporting federal human resources management.
IT Modernization and Working Capital, Of.b: OPM, federal agencies, and federal employees and retirees who depend on OPM systems

IT Modernization, Departmental Managemen.d: Funds the modernization of the Department of Labor’s information technology infrastructure to improve service delivery, cybersecurity, and operational efficiency across DOL agencies.
IT Modernization, Departmental Managemen.b: DOL agencies, workers, employers, and beneficiaries of DOL programs

Immigration Enforcement Account, Border.d: Receives proceeds from fines, penalties, and forfeitures related to immigration enforcement activities and uses them to fund additional enforcement operations.
Immigration Enforcement Account, Border.b: Immigration enforcement operations and border security programs

Immigration Services, H-1 B Funded, Home.d: Uses H-1B visa fee revenues to fund USCIS adjudication services and grants for education, training, and scholarship programs to develop the domestic workforce in STEM fields.
Immigration Services, H-1 B Funded, Home.b: USCIS operations, and U.S. workers and students in STEM education and training programs

Independent Counsel, Justice.d: Provided funding for independent counsels appointed to investigate and prosecute high-ranking government officials. The Independent Counsel statute expired in 1999 and this account is largely inactive.
Independent Counsel, Justice.b: The justice system and government accountability (account largely inactive)

Indian Arts and Crafts Fund, Departmenta.d: Supports the Indian Arts and Crafts Board in promoting and protecting the economic development of American Indian and Alaska Native arts and crafts. Combats counterfeit Indian-made products.
Indian Arts and Crafts Fund, Departmenta.b: American Indian and Alaska Native artists and craftspeople

Indian Direct Loan Program Account, Bure.d: Provides direct loans from the Bureau of Indian Affairs for economic development, housing, and other purposes on Indian reservations where private credit is not available.
Indian Direct Loan Program Account, Bure.b: American Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and individual Indians seeking development financing

Indian Energy Policy and Programs, Energ.d: Supports energy development and efficiency on tribal lands through technical assistance, grants, and loan guarantees. Helps tribes develop conventional and renewable energy resources.
Indian Energy Policy and Programs, Energ.b: American Indian tribes and Alaska Native communities developing energy resources

Indian Guaranteed Loan Program Account,.d: Guarantees loans made by private lenders to Indian individuals, tribes, and organizations for business and economic development on or near reservations. Encourages private lending.
Indian Guaranteed Loan Program Account,.b: Indian entrepreneurs, tribal businesses, and economic development on reservations

Indian Health Service Gift Fund, Health.d: Receives gifts and donations for the benefit of the Indian Health Service and the American Indian and Alaska Native populations it serves. Supplements appropriated health care funding.
Indian Health Service Gift Fund, Health.b: American Indian and Alaska Native communities served by IHS health facilities

Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund Progr.d: Guarantees private sector mortgage loans to American Indian and Alaska Native families and tribes for home purchase, construction, and rehabilitation in Indian areas. Known as Section 184.
Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund Progr.b: American Indian and Alaska Native families seeking homeownership on or near tribal lands

Indian Land Consolidation, Bureau of Ind.d: Funds the purchase of fractionated land interests from willing sellers to consolidate Indian land ownership and reduce the administrative burden of managing highly fractionated allotments.
