Supports border security technology to verify the departure of foreign nationals from the United States.
9-11 Response and Biometric Exit Account.b: U.S. Customs and Border Protection, general public through enhanced national security

APEC Business Travel Card Account, Custo.d: Finances the issuance and management of APEC Business Travel Cards, which allow pre-cleared business travelers expedited entry into APEC member economies. Funded through user fees collected from applicants.
APEC Business Travel Card Account, Custo.b: U.S. business travelers engaged in trade with APEC member countries

Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund, Office.d: Created by the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 to reclaim and restore land and water resources adversely affected by past coal mining operations. Funded by a fee assessed on current coal production.
Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund, Office.b: Communities near abandoned coal mines, state and tribal reclamation programs, environmental restoration efforts

Access Board.d: An independent federal agency that promotes equality for people with disabilities through the development of accessibility guidelines and standards for the built environment, transportation, communication, and information technology. Originally established as the Architectural and Transportation Barriers Compliance Board.
Access Board.b: People with disabilities, architects, designers, federal agencies, and the general public

Acquisition Workforce Training Fund, Gen.d: Provides resources for the recruitment, hiring, and training of the federal acquisition workforce to improve the government’s ability to manage contracts and procurement. Funded by a portion of fees collected from federal contracts.
Acquisition Workforce Training Fund, Gen.b: Federal acquisition professionals, government agencies seeking improved procurement capabilities

Acquisition of Lands for National Forest.d: Supports the purchase of privately held lands within or adjacent to National Forest boundaries under the authority of the Weeks Act and subsequent legislation. Protects watersheds, wildlife habitat, and public recreation resources.
Acquisition of Lands for National Forest.b: General public, recreational users, conservation interests, rural communities near national forests

Acquisition of Lands to Complete Land Ex.d: Funds the equalization payments needed to complete land exchanges between the Forest Service and private or state landowners. Facilitates the consolidation of federal land ownership within national forest boundaries.
Acquisition of Lands to Complete Land Ex.b: Forest Service, private landowners engaged in exchanges, conservation interests

Administration of Rights-of-Way and Othe.d: Covers costs associated with processing and administering permits, easements, and rights-of-way across National Forest System lands. Funded by fees paid by applicants seeking to use forest land for roads, pipelines, power lines, and similar infrastructure.
Administration of Rights-of-Way and Othe.b: Utility companies, infrastructure developers, state and local governments requiring access across federal forest lands

Administrative Conference of the U.S..d: An independent federal agency that studies the efficiency, adequacy, and fairness of administrative procedures used by federal agencies and recommends improvements. Issues recommendations to improve government operations and regulatory processes.
Administrative Conference of the U.S..b: Federal agencies, regulated industries, citizens interacting with federal administrative processes

Administrative Expenses, Children’s Heal.d: Funds the administrative costs associated with operating the Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP), which provides health coverage to eligible children in families with incomes too high to qualify for Medicaid. Covers enrollment, outreach, and program management costs.
Administrative Expenses, Children’s Heal.b: Low-income children and families, state health agencies administering CHIP

Administrative Expenses, Federal Transit.d: Covers the salaries, expenses, and operational costs of the Federal Transit Administration in carrying out federal transit programs. Supports oversight of grants, technical assistance, and research for public transportation systems.
Administrative Expenses, Federal Transit.b: Public transit agencies, commuters, and communities served by public transportation

Administrative Services Franchise Fund,.d: A revolving fund that provides common administrative services to the Federal Aviation Administration on a fee-for-service basis. Covers shared services such as accounting, human resources, and information technology.
Administrative Services Franchise Fund,.b: FAA offices and programs receiving centralized administrative support

Administrative Support Offices, Manageme.d: Funds the internal management, administrative support, and overhead functions of the Department of Housing and Urban Development. Supports financial management, human resources, IT, procurement, and other departmental services.
Administrative Support Offices, Manageme.b: HUD program offices and their beneficiaries through more efficient program delivery

Administrative and Operating Expenses, O.d: Covers the administrative costs of managing risk management programs under the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation and the Risk Management Agency. Supports the delivery of crop insurance products to American farmers.
Administrative and Operating Expenses, O.b: American farmers and ranchers participating in federal crop insurance programs

Advanced Manufacturing Investment Credit.d: Provides a tax credit for investments in semiconductor manufacturing and related advanced manufacturing facilities in the United States, established by the CHIPS and Science Act. Incentivizes domestic production of semiconductors and related equipment.
Advanced Manufacturing Investment Credit.b: Semiconductor manufacturers, advanced manufacturing companies investing in U.S. production facilities

Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy.d: Funds high-risk, high-reward research and development of transformational energy technologies that are too early-stage for private sector investment. Modeled after DARPA to accelerate breakthroughs in clean energy.
Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy.b: Energy researchers, technology startups, universities, national laboratories, and ultimately energy consumers

Advances from State Cooperating Agencies.d: Receives and manages advance payments from state agencies, foreign governments, and other federal agencies for highway-related cooperative work performed by the Federal Highway Administration. Enables collaborative infrastructure projects.
Advances from State Cooperating Agencies.b: State transportation departments, cooperating federal agencies, foreign governments with bilateral agreements

Advisory Council on Historic Preservatio.d: An independent federal agency that promotes the preservation, enhancement, and productive use of the nation’s historic resources. Advises the President and Congress on historic preservation matters and reviews federal projects affecting historic properties under Section 106 of the National Historic Preservation Act.
Advisory Council on Historic Preservatio.b: Historic property owners, state and tribal historic preservation offices, communities with historic resources

Aeronautics, National Aeronautics and Sp.d: Funds NASA’s aeronautics research programs to develop technologies that improve aircraft safety, efficiency, noise reduction, and environmental performance. Supports fundamental aeronautics research, integrated aviation systems, and advanced air vehicle programs.
Aeronautics, National Aeronautics and Sp.b: Aviation industry, commercial airlines, aircraft manufacturers, passengers, and the general public

African Development Foundation.d: An independent federal agency that invests in grassroots African enterprises and community-based organizations to promote economic development and self-sufficiency. Provides grants and technical assistance to small and medium enterprises across Sub-Saharan Africa.
African Development Foundation.b: African entrepreneurs, small enterprises, community-based organizations in Sub-Saharan Africa

Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund Progr.d: Provides direct and guaranteed loans to farmers and ranchers who cannot obtain commercial credit at reasonable terms. Supports farm ownership loans, operating loans, and emergency loans to agricultural producers.
Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund Progr.b: Beginning farmers, socially disadvantaged farmers, family farmers unable to obtain commercial credit

Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund, Liqu.d: Manages the portfolio of agricultural loans made before credit reform legislation (pre-1992), collecting repayments and managing defaults on legacy farm loans. Winds down the older portfolio of direct farm credit obligations.
Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund, Liqu.b: Farm borrowers with pre-1992 agricultural loans

Agricultural Disaster Relief Trust Fund,.d: Provides assistance to agricultural producers affected by natural disasters through supplemental revenue, livestock, and tree assistance programs. Funded by customs duties and established by the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008.
Agricultural Disaster Relief Trust Fund,.b: Farmers and ranchers suffering crop, livestock, or tree losses from natural disasters

Agriculture Buildings and Facilities, Ag.d: Funds the construction, repair, improvement, and maintenance of buildings and facilities used by the Department of Agriculture. Covers capital projects at USDA research centers, offices, and laboratories.
Agriculture Buildings and Facilities, Ag.b: USDA employees, agricultural researchers, program delivery operations

Agriculture Wool Apparel Manufacturers T.d: Provides grants to domestic manufacturers of wool fabric, yarn, and apparel to offset the cost of purchasing domestically produced wool. Established to support the U.S. wool industry and domestic apparel manufacturing.
Agriculture Wool Apparel Manufacturers T.b: Domestic wool apparel manufacturers, U.S. wool producers, textile industry workers

Air Carrier Worker Support, Departmental.d: Provided payroll support payments to air carriers and contractors to protect the wages and benefits of airline workers during the COVID-19 pandemic. Established under the CARES Act and subsequent pandemic relief legislation.
Air Carrier Worker Support, Departmental.b: Airline employees, air carrier companies, airline contractors

Air and Marine Operations, U.S. Customs.d: Funds CBP’s air and marine interdiction and law enforcement operations to detect, interdict, and prevent the unlawful entry of people, narcotics, and other contraband. Operates aircraft and marine vessels along U.S. borders and coastal waters.
Air and Marine Operations, U.S. Customs.b: General public through enhanced border security, law enforcement agencies

Airport Checkpoint Screening Fund, Trans.d: Receives security fees collected from airline passengers to fund aviation security screening operations at airports. Supports TSA checkpoint screening personnel and equipment.
Airport Checkpoint Screening Fund, Trans.b: Air travelers, airports, airlines, general public through aviation security

Airport Terminal Program, Federal Aviati.d: Provides competitive grants for airport terminal development projects including construction, renovation, and modernization of airport terminal facilities. Established through the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
Airport Terminal Program, Federal Aviati.b: Airports, air travelers, airport workers, surrounding communities

Airport and Airway Trust Fund - Treasury.d: The primary funding mechanism for the federal aviation system, financed by aviation excise taxes on airline tickets, cargo, and aviation fuel. Supports FAA operations, airport improvement grants, and air traffic control modernization.
Airport and Airway Trust Fund - Treasury.b: Airports, airlines, air travelers, general aviation community, air traffic controllers

Alaska Resupply Program, Bureau of India.d: Funds the delivery of essential goods, fuel, and supplies to remote Alaska Native villages that are inaccessible by road. Ensures year-round resupply to communities dependent on barge, aircraft, or other non-road transportation.
Alaska Resupply Program, Bureau of India.b: Alaska Native villages and communities in remote areas without road access

All Stations Accessibility Program, Fede.d: Provides competitive grants for legacy transit agencies to upgrade older rail stations to meet ADA accessibility standards. Established by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to make public transit accessible to people with disabilities.
All Stations Accessibility Program, Fede.b: Transit riders with disabilities, older adults, transit agencies with legacy rail stations

Allowances and Office Staff for Former P.d: Funds pensions, office space, staff, travel, and other allowances for former Presidents of the United States as authorized by the Former Presidents Act. Also provides funding for the transition of outgoing presidents.
Allowances and Office Staff for Former P.b: Former Presidents of the United States and their office staff

American Battle Monuments Commission.d: An independent federal agency that designs, constructs, maintains, and operates permanent American military cemeteries and memorials on foreign soil. Manages 26 overseas military cemeteries and 32 federal memorials, monuments, and markers.
American Battle Monuments Commission.b: Families of fallen service members, veterans, the American public, foreign visitors

American Printing House for the Blind, S.d: Provides annual federal appropriations to the American Printing House for the Blind to produce and distribute educational materials in accessible formats for students who are blind or visually impaired. Supplies braille, large print, and audio materials to schools nationwide.
American Printing House for the Blind, S.b: Students who are blind or visually impaired, schools serving visually impaired students

American Sections, International Commiss.d: Funds the U.S. participation in international boundary and fisheries commissions, including the International Boundary Commission (U.S.-Canada) and various international fisheries commissions. Supports diplomatic and technical cooperation on shared natural resources.
American Sections, International Commiss.b: U.S.-Canada border communities, commercial fishing industries, international cooperation efforts

Appalachian Regional Development Program.d: Funds specific economic and community development programs administered by the Appalachian Regional Commission, including highway construction, area development, and local development district assistance. Targets the most economically distressed areas within the Appalachian region.
Appalachian Regional Development Program.b: Appalachian communities, local development districts, state and local governments in the region

Armed Forces Retirement Home.d: An independent federal agency that provides residences and related services for certain retired and former members of the U.S. Armed Forces. Operates the Armed Forces Retirement Home facilities in Washington, D.C. and Gulfport, Mississippi.
Armed Forces Retirement Home.b: Eligible retired and former enlisted military members and warrant officers

Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund,.d: The trust fund that finances operations of the Armed Forces Retirement Home, funded by monthly payroll deductions from enlisted members, fines, forfeitures, and investment income. Supports residential care, medical services, and facility maintenance.
Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund,.b: Residents of Armed Forces Retirement Home facilities, eligible military retirees

Assessment Funds, Office of the Comptrol.d: Collects assessments from national banks and federal savings associations to fund the operations of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency. Entirely self-funded through bank examinations and assessments without congressional appropriations.
Assessment Funds, Office of the Comptrol.b: National banks, federal savings associations, banking customers through regulatory oversight

Asset Proceeds and Space Management Fund.d: Receives proceeds from the sale or exchange of surplus federal real property and uses them for acquisition, improvement, and management of federal space. Supports the efficient management of the federal real property portfolio.
Asset Proceeds and Space Management Fund.b: Federal agencies occupying government-owned or leased space

Assets Forfeiture Fund, Justice.d: Receives proceeds from the forfeiture of assets seized by federal law enforcement agencies in connection with criminal activity. Funds are used to support law enforcement operations, pay for asset management and disposal costs, and provide restitution to crime victims.
Assets Forfeiture Fund, Justice.b: Federal law enforcement agencies, crime victims receiving restitution, state and local law enforcement through equitable sharing

Assistance for the Independent States of.d: Provides economic and democratic development assistance to the newly independent states following the dissolution of the Soviet Union under the FREEDOM Support Act. Supports market reforms, democratic governance, and civil society development.
Assistance for the Independent States of.b: Citizens and institutions in former Soviet states undergoing democratic and economic transitions

Assistance to Shipyards, Maritime Admini.d: Provides federal assistance for the modernization and improvement of U.S. shipyard facilities to maintain the domestic shipbuilding industrial base. Supports capital improvements and equipment upgrades at small and medium-sized shipyards.
Assistance to Shipyards, Maritime Admini.b: U.S. shipyards, shipyard workers, the domestic maritime industry

Assisted Housing Inspections and Risk As.d: Funds inspections and risk assessments of HUD-assisted multifamily housing and public housing properties to ensure compliance with physical condition standards. Supports housing quality and safety for federally assisted tenants.
Assisted Housing Inspections and Risk As.b: Residents of HUD-assisted housing, public housing tenants, housing authorities

Aviation Insurance Revolving Fund, Trans.d: Provides aviation insurance and reinsurance when commercial insurance is not available on reasonable terms for U.S. air carriers and aircraft. Activated during national emergencies or when the commercial insurance market cannot provide adequate coverage.
Aviation Insurance Revolving Fund, Trans.b: U.S. air carriers, aircraft operators, aviation industry during emergencies or market disruptions

Aviation User Fees, Federal Aviation Adm.d: Collects fees from operators of aircraft that are neither combatant nor combatant-support aircraft for air traffic control and related services provided by the FAA. Applies primarily to fees charged to foreign-registered aircraft.
Aviation User Fees, Federal Aviation Adm.b: FAA operations, the national airspace system, general aviation safety

Barry Goldwater Scholarship and Excellen.d: An independent federal agency that awards scholarships to outstanding college sophomores and juniors who intend to pursue careers in mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering. Honors Senator Barry Goldwater’s commitment to science education.
Barry Goldwater Scholarship and Excellen.b: Undergraduate STEM students, future scientists, engineers, and mathematicians

Biomass Research and Development, Nation.d: Supports research and development of technologies for converting biomass into bioenergy, biofuels, and bioproducts. Coordinates federal biomass R&D efforts across agencies to advance the bioeconomy.
Biomass Research and Development, Nation.b: Bioenergy researchers, agricultural producers, rural communities, energy consumers

Biorefinery Assistance Program Account,.d: Provides loan guarantees for the development, construction, and retrofitting of commercial-scale biorefineries that produce advanced biofuels. Supports the development of domestic renewable fuel production capacity.
Biorefinery Assistance Program Account,.b: Biorefinery developers, advanced biofuel producers, rural communities, energy consumers

Black Lung Disability Trust Fund - Treas.d: Provides compensation and medical benefits to coal miners totally disabled by pneumoconiosis (black lung disease) and their survivors when no responsible mine operator can be identified. Funded by an excise tax on coal.
Black Lung Disability Trust Fund - Treas.b: Disabled coal miners with black lung disease, surviving dependents of deceased miners

Blackfeet Water Settlement Implementatio.d: Funds the implementation of the Blackfeet Water Rights Settlement Act, which resolved water rights claims of the Blackfeet Tribe in Montana. Supports the construction and rehabilitation of water infrastructure on the Blackfeet Reservation.
Blackfeet Water Settlement Implementatio.b: Blackfeet Tribe, Blackfeet Reservation residents, Montana water users

Border Security Fencing, Infrastructure,.d: Funds the construction and maintenance of border fencing, roads, lighting, and surveillance technology along U.S. land borders. Supports tactical infrastructure and technology deployments to secure the border.
Border Security Fencing, Infrastructure,.b: Border communities, Customs and Border Protection agents, general public through enhanced border security

Breach Bond/Detention Fund, Border and T.d: Collects and manages cash bonds and breach bond penalties from immigration cases where aliens fail to comply with conditions of release. Funds are used to support immigration detention and removal operations.
Breach Bond/Detention Fund, Border and T.b: Immigration enforcement operations, ICE detention programs

Broadband Connectivity Fund, National Te.d: Provides funding to expand broadband internet access and adoption, particularly in underserved and unserved areas of the United States. Established under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
Broadband Connectivity Fund, National Te.b: Underserved rural and urban communities lacking broadband access, internet service providers, digital inclusion programs

Broadcasting Capital Improvements, Broad.d: Funded capital improvements and maintenance of broadcasting infrastructure used by U.S. international broadcasting entities including Voice of America and Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty. Supported transmission facilities, studios, and broadcast equipment.
Broadcasting Capital Improvements, Broad.b: U.S. international broadcasting operations, foreign audiences of U.S. government-funded media

Buildings and Facilities, Animal and Pla.d: Funds the construction, renovation, and maintenance of laboratory and office facilities used by APHIS for animal and plant health inspection, veterinary diagnostics, and pest detection. Supports critical biosecurity infrastructure.
Buildings and Facilities, Animal and Pla.b: APHIS employees, U.S. agricultural producers protected from pests and diseases

Buildings and Facilities, Centers for Di.d: Funds construction, renovation, and maintenance of CDC laboratories, research facilities, and office buildings. Supports infrastructure critical to public health surveillance, disease investigation, and emergency response.
Buildings and Facilities, Centers for Di.b: CDC researchers, the public health workforce, the general public through improved disease surveillance infrastructure

Buildings and Facilities, Environmental.d: Funds the construction, repair, and improvement of EPA laboratory and office facilities used for environmental research and regulatory operations. Supports the physical infrastructure necessary for environmental protection activities.
Buildings and Facilities, Environmental.b: EPA employees, environmental researchers, communities benefiting from environmental protection

Buildings and Facilities, Food and Drug.d: Funds the construction, renovation, and improvement of FDA laboratories, offices, and testing facilities used for ensuring the safety of food, drugs, medical devices, and other regulated products. Supports modernization of FDA’s physical infrastructure.
Buildings and Facilities, Food and Drug.b: FDA staff, consumers protected by FDA regulatory activities, regulated industries

Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection.d: The primary funding account for the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, receiving transfers from the Federal Reserve System rather than congressional appropriations. Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection.b: Consumers of financial products and services, particularly those vulnerable to unfair or abusive practices

Bureau of Reclamation Loans Program Acco.d: Provides direct and guaranteed loans for water infrastructure projects in the western United States, including dam safety, water recycling, and rural water supply systems. Supports Reclamation’s mission of water resource management.
Bureau of Reclamation Loans Program Acco.b: Western U.S. water districts, municipalities, irrigators, rural water systems

Business Assistance Trust Fund, Small Bu.d: A trust fund within the Small Business Administration that provides assistance to small businesses, including advisory and counseling services. Supports small business development centers and entrepreneurial development programs.
Business Assistance Trust Fund, Small Bu.b: Small business owners, entrepreneurs, small business development centers

Business Loan and Investment Fund, Liqui.d: Manages the portfolio of SBA business loans made before the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990, collecting repayments and handling defaults on legacy loans. Winds down the pre-reform direct loan portfolio.

Business Loans Program Account - Recover.d: Provided enhanced small business lending under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009, including increased SBA loan guarantees and reduced fees during the economic recovery period. A temporary stimulus-era expansion of SBA lending programs.
Business Loans Program Account - Recover.b: Small businesses needing credit during the 2008-2009 economic downturn

Business Systems Modernization, Internal.d: Funds the modernization and upgrade of the IRS’s information technology systems and business processes to improve tax administration and taxpayer services. Supports large-scale IT projects and system replacements.
Business Systems Modernization, Internal.b: Taxpayers through improved services, IRS operations through modernized systems

CDC Working Capital Fund, Centers for Di.d: A revolving fund that provides common administrative and support services to CDC programs on a fee-for-service basis. Covers centralized services such as information technology, financial management, and procurement.
CDC Working Capital Fund, Centers for Di.b: CDC programs and centers receiving shared administrative services

Cable Security Fleet, Maritime Administr.d: Provides stipend payments to maintain a fleet of commercially viable, privately owned U.S.-flagged cable laying and maintenance vessels to ensure national security and critical undersea cable infrastructure capabilities. Cable Security Fleet, Maritime Administr.b: U.S.-flagged cable ship operators, national security interests, undersea cable infrastructure

California Bay-Delta Restoration, Bureau.d: Funds federal participation in the CALFED Bay-Delta Program to restore the ecological health of the San Francisco Bay/Sacramento-San Joaquin River Delta while improving water supply reliability. California Bay-Delta Restoration, Bureau.b: California water users, Bay-Delta ecosystem, agricultural and urban water districts, endangered species

Capital Magnet Fund, Community Developme.d: Provides competitively awarded grants to Community Development Financial Institutions and nonprofit housing organizations to attract private capital for affordable housing and community development. Capital Magnet Fund, Community Developme.b: Low-income families needing affordable housing, CDFIs, nonprofit housing developers, underserved communities

Carbon Dioxide Transportation Infrastruc.d: Provides loans, loan guarantees, and other financial assistance for the development of carbon dioxide transportation infrastructure including pipelines for carbon capture and storage. Established under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
Carbon Dioxide Transportation Infrastruc.b: Carbon capture and storage project developers, CO2 pipeline operators, communities benefiting from emissions reduction

Carson City Special Account, Bureau of L.d: Receives proceeds from the sale of public lands in the Carson City, Nevada district under the Southern Nevada Public Land Management Act. Funds are used for capital improvements, conservation initiatives, and land acquisition in Nevada.
Carson City Special Account, Bureau of L.b: Carson City and surrounding Nevada communities, conservation programs, public lands users

Census Working Capital Fund, Bureau of t.d: A revolving fund that provides reimbursable statistical services and surveys to other federal agencies and entities on a cost-recovery basis. Allows the Census Bureau to conduct specialized surveys beyond its core demographic programs.
Census Working Capital Fund, Bureau of t.b: Federal agencies needing statistical data, policymakers, researchers relying on Census-produced data

Centennial Challenge, National Park Serv.d: A matching grant program that leverages private donations with federal funds to support signature projects and programs in the National Park System. Launched in conjunction with the National Park Service’s centennial in 2016.
Centennial Challenge, National Park Serv.b: National parks, park visitors, philanthropic partners, conservation organizations

Center for Middle Eastern-Western Dialog.d: A trust fund that supports dialogue and exchange programs between the United States and Middle Eastern countries to promote mutual understanding. Funds educational and cultural exchange activities managed by the State Department.
Center for Middle Eastern-Western Dialog.b: U.S. and Middle Eastern scholars, students, cultural organizations, diplomatic understanding

Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Innova.d: Tests innovative payment and service delivery models to reduce health care costs while preserving or enhancing quality of care for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries. Established by the Affordable Care Act with authority to scale successful models nationwide.
Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Innova.b: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries, healthcare providers, the health care system through cost savings

Central Hazardous Materials Fund, Depart.d: Funds the assessment, cleanup, and remediation of hazardous materials contamination on Department of Interior lands. Addresses environmental contamination from legacy mining, industrial, and government activities.
Central Hazardous Materials Fund, Depart.b: Communities near contaminated Interior lands, environmental restoration, public health protection

Central Utah Project Completion Account,.d: Funds the completion of the Central Utah Project, a large-scale water development project that diverts water from the Colorado River basin to the Bonneville basin for municipal, industrial, and agricultural use. Central Utah Project Completion Account,.b: Utah water users, municipalities, irrigators, environmental mitigation programs

Central Valley Project Restoration Fund,.d: Funds habitat restoration, fish and wildlife protection, and environmental mitigation activities associated with California’s Central Valley Project. Financed by charges on CVP water and power users to offset environmental impacts of the project.
Central Valley Project Restoration Fund,.b: Fish and wildlife populations in the Central Valley, environmental restoration, CVP water users

Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe Terrestrial W.d: A trust fund established to compensate the Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe for terrestrial wildlife habitat losses caused by the construction of the Oahe Dam on the Missouri River. Funds wildlife habitat restoration on the reservation.
Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe Terrestrial W.b: Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe, wildlife habitat restoration on the reservation

Child Care Entitlement to States, Admini.d: Provides mandatory federal funding to states, territories, and tribes for child care subsidies for low-income families, particularly those transitioning from welfare to work. Child Care Entitlement to States, Admini.b: Low-income working families, families transitioning off welfare, children receiving subsidized child care

Child Survival and Health Programs Fund,.d: Funds international health programs focused on reducing child mortality, combating infectious diseases, and improving maternal health in developing countries. Child Survival and Health Programs Fund,.b: Children and mothers in developing countries, global health organizations, USAID partner countries

Child and Dependent Care Tax Credit, Int.d: Provides a tax credit to working families for expenses incurred for the care of qualifying children under age 13 or dependents who are physically or mentally incapable of self-care. Offsets a portion of child care costs to support workforce participation.
Child and Dependent Care Tax Credit, Int.b: Working parents and families with child care expenses, caregivers of disabled dependents

Children’s Research and Technical Assist.d: Funds research, evaluation, and technical assistance on programs serving children and families, including child welfare, Head Start, child care, and family support services. Supports evidence-based policy development for children’s programs.
Children’s Research and Technical Assist.b: Children and families served by ACF programs, researchers, program administrators, policymakers

Civilian Board of Contract Appeals, Gene.d: An independent tribunal that hears and decides contract disputes between government contractors and civilian federal agencies under the Contract Disputes Act. Consolidated the various agency boards of contract appeals into one entity.
Civilian Board of Contract Appeals, Gene.b: Government contractors, civilian federal agencies involved in contract disputes

Claims and Treaty Obligations, Bureau of.d: Funds payments owed to Indian tribes and individuals pursuant to treaties, court judgments, and settlement agreements with the federal government. Covers obligations arising from historical agreements and legal claims.
Claims and Treaty Obligations, Bureau of.b: Indian tribes and individual tribal members with treaty rights and legal claims against the federal government

Claims for Damages (Indefinite), Bureau.d: Provides indefinite budget authority to pay claims against the federal government for damages caused by government activities, including tort claims and property damage. A permanent appropriation for settling civil claims.
Claims for Damages (Indefinite), Bureau.b: Individuals and entities with valid claims for damages caused by federal government actions

Coastal Impact Assistance, Fish and Wild.d: Provides financial assistance to oil and gas producing coastal states and their political subdivisions to mitigate the impacts of offshore energy development. Funded from federal offshore oil and gas revenues under the Energy Policy Act of 2005.
Coastal Impact Assistance, Fish and Wild.b: Coastal states with offshore energy production, coastal communities, conservation programs

Coastal Wetlands Restoration Trust Fund,.d: Funds the restoration and protection of coastal wetlands in Louisiana and other coastal states through the Coastal Wetlands Planning, Protection and Restoration Act. Financed by a portion of Sport Fish Restoration revenues.
Coastal Wetlands Restoration Trust Fund,.b: Coastal communities, Louisiana wetlands ecosystems, fisheries, storm protection beneficiaries

College Housing Loans, Education.d: A legacy program that provided low-interest federal loans to institutions of higher education for the construction of student housing and related facilities. No new loans have been made for decades; the account manages the remaining portfolio.
College Housing Loans, Education.b: Colleges and universities with outstanding federal housing loans, students in campus housing

College Housing and Academic Facilities.d: A legacy program that provided federal loans for the construction of academic facilities, libraries, and student housing at colleges and universities under the Higher Education Facilities Act. Manages the remaining loan portfolio from historical lending.
College Housing and Academic Facilities.b: Higher education institutions with outstanding federal facility loans

Colorado River Basins Power Marketing Fu.d: Manages the generation, transmission, and marketing of hydroelectric power from Colorado River dams including Hoover, Glen Canyon, and others. Revenues from power sales repay project construction costs and fund operations.
Colorado River Basins Power Marketing Fu.b: Electric utilities and consumers in the western U.S., preference customers (municipalities, cooperatives)

Commission for the Preservation of Ameri.d: An independent federal agency that identifies and reports on cemeteries, monuments, and historic buildings in Eastern and Central Europe associated with the American heritage, particularly those of U.S. citizens’ ancestral communities. Commission for the Preservation of Ameri.b: Americans with ancestral heritage in Eastern and Central Europe, cultural preservation efforts

Commission of Fine Arts.d: An independent federal agency that advises the President, Congress, and federal agencies on matters of architecture, sculpture, painting, and other fine arts as they relate to the appearance of Washington, D.C. Commission of Fine Arts.b: Washington D.C. residents, visitors, federal agencies, the public interest in the national capital’s aesthetic character

Commission on Civil Rights.d: An independent, bipartisan federal agency that investigates complaints of discrimination, studies federal civil rights policy, and issues reports and recommendations to the President and Congress. Monitors federal civil rights enforcement.
Commission on Civil Rights.b: Individuals subject to discrimination, civil rights advocacy, policymakers

Committee for Purchase from People Who A.d: Oversees the AbilityOne Program, which directs federal agencies to purchase designated products and services from nonprofit agencies employing people who are blind or have significant disabilities. Committee for Purchase from People Who A.b: People who are blind or severely disabled, nonprofit agencies employing disabled workers

Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.d: Collects filing fees from parties submitting transactions for national security review by CFIUS, established under the Foreign Investment Risk Review Modernization Act (FIRRMA).
