Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.b: National security interests, federal agencies participating in CFIUS reviews

Commodity Credit Corporation Direct Loan.d: Manages the legacy portfolio of direct loans made by the Commodity Credit Corporation prior to credit reform, including commodity price support loans and farm storage facility loans. Collects repayments on pre-1992 loans.
Commodity Credit Corporation Direct Loan.b: Farmers with outstanding pre-reform CCC loans

Commodity Credit Corporation Export Loan.d: Provides export credit guarantees to promote U.S. agricultural exports by reducing financial risk for U.S. exporters and foreign buyers. Supports programs like the GSM-102 Export Credit Guarantee Program.
Commodity Credit Corporation Export Loan.b: U.S. agricultural exporters, foreign buyers of U.S. farm products, the U.S. agricultural sector

Commodity Futures Trading Commission Cus.d: Receives monetary sanctions and penalties collected by the CFTC from enforcement actions and uses them to fund whistleblower awards and customer education initiatives. Established by the Dodd-Frank Act.
Commodity Futures Trading Commission Cus.b: Whistleblowers reporting commodities fraud, market participants, investors through customer education

Community Development Financial Institut.d: Provided emergency financial support to Community Development Financial Institutions during the COVID-19 pandemic to maintain lending capacity in underserved communities. A temporary emergency supplement to the regular CDFI Fund program.
Community Development Financial Institut.b: CDFIs, low-income and minority communities served by CDFIs during the pandemic

Community Development Revolving Loan Fun.d: Provides loans and technical assistance to low-income designated credit unions to increase the availability of financial services to underserved communities. Helps credit unions build capacity to serve low-income members.
Community Development Revolving Loan Fun.b: Low-income credit unions, underserved communities lacking access to financial services

Community Oriented Policing Services, Of.d: Provides grants to state, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies to advance community policing strategies through hiring of officers, training, and technology. Known as the COPS program, established under the Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act.
Community Oriented Policing Services, Of.b: State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies, communities through improved public safety

Community Planning and Development, Prog.d: Funds the salaries and operational expenses of HUD’s Office of Community Planning and Development, which administers community development and housing programs including CDBG, HOME, and homeless assistance. Supports program oversight and technical assistance.
Community Planning and Development, Prog.b: Communities receiving CPD grants, homeless populations, low-income residents

Compact of Free Association, Insular Aff.d: Provides economic assistance and federal services to the Freely Associated States (Marshall Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, and Palau) under Compacts of Free Association. Supports education, health, infrastructure, and governance in these Pacific island nations.
Compact of Free Association, Insular Aff.b: Citizens of the Marshall Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, and Palau

Compensation of the President, Executive.d: Provides for the salary and compensation of the President of the United States as established by Article II of the Constitution. The current annual salary is $400,000, set by Congress in 2001.
Compensation of the President, Executive.b: The President of the United States

Complex Crises Fund, Funds Appropriated.d: Provides flexible, rapid-response funding for emerging or unforeseen complex crises abroad, including political transitions, conflict situations, and humanitarian emergencies. Allows USAID to respond quickly without waiting for new appropriations.
Complex Crises Fund, Funds Appropriated.b: Populations in countries experiencing complex crises, conflict, or political transitions

Concessioner Improvement Accounts, Natio.d: Receives a portion of franchise fees paid by concession operators in national parks and uses funds for infrastructure improvements at concession facilities. Established by the National Parks Omnibus Management Act.
Concessioner Improvement Accounts, Natio.b: National park visitors, park concession facility users, concession operators

Concessions Fees and Volunteer Services,.d: Collects fees from concession activities on Agricultural Research Service properties and supports volunteer service programs at ARS research facilities. Funds minor improvements and visitor services at ARS sites.
Concessions Fees and Volunteer Services,.b: ARS research facilities, volunteers, visitors to agricultural research sites

Conditional Gift Fund, General, State.d: Receives conditional gifts and donations made to the Department of State for specific purposes designated by the donors. Allows the State Department to accept and expend private donations with conditions on their use.
Conditional Gift Fund, General, State.b: State Department programs and activities designated by donors

Conditional Gift Fund, Health Resources.d: Receives conditional gifts and donations to HRSA for health-related purposes specified by donors. Allows the agency to accept private funding for public health programs, research, and services.
Conditional Gift Fund, Health Resources.b: HRSA programs, underserved communities benefiting from donor-designated health initiatives

Connecting Minority Communities Fund, Na.d: Provides grants to Historically Black Colleges and Universities, Tribal Colleges and Universities, and Minority-Serving Institutions to purchase broadband internet access and equipment. Addresses the digital divide at minority-serving educational institutions.
Connecting Minority Communities Fund, Na.b: HBCUs, Tribal Colleges, Minority-Serving Institutions, students and faculty at these institutions

Construction, Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco.d: Funds the construction and major renovation of ATF laboratory facilities, offices, and other infrastructure. Supports ATF’s law enforcement mission through modernized physical facilities.
Construction, Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco.b: ATF personnel, law enforcement operations, public safety

Construction, Cemeterial Expenses, Army.d: Funds the construction, maintenance, and operation of Arlington National Cemetery and the Soldiers’ and Airmen’s Home National Cemetery. Supports burial operations, grave markers, and cemetery infrastructure.
Construction, Cemeterial Expenses, Army.b: Families of deceased veterans and military personnel, visitors to national cemeteries

Construction, Drug Enforcement Administr.d: Funds construction and major renovation projects for DEA laboratories, offices, and operational facilities. Supports the physical infrastructure for federal drug law enforcement operations.
Construction, Drug Enforcement Administr.b: DEA personnel, drug enforcement operations, public safety

Construction, Federal Bureau of Investig.d: Funds construction, acquisition, and major renovation of FBI facilities including field offices, laboratories, and the training academy at Quantico. Supports the physical infrastructure for federal law enforcement and counterintelligence.
Construction, Federal Bureau of Investig.b: FBI personnel, federal law enforcement operations, national security

Construction, Rehabilitation, Operation,.d: Funds the construction, rehabilitation, operation, and maintenance of high-voltage transmission systems operated by the Western Area Power Administration across 15 western states. Delivers hydroelectric power from federal dams to wholesale customers.
Construction, Rehabilitation, Operation,.b: Electric utilities, municipalities, cooperatives, and tribal entities purchasing federal hydropower in the western U.S.

Construction, United States Fish and Wil.d: Funds construction and major maintenance projects at national wildlife refuges, fish hatcheries, and other Fish and Wildlife Service facilities. Supports visitor centers, habitat restoration infrastructure, and operational facilities.
Construction, United States Fish and Wil.b: Wildlife refuge visitors, fish and wildlife conservation, local communities near FWS facilities

Construction, United States Marshals Ser.d: Funds construction and major renovation of U.S. Marshals Service facilities including courthouses, prisoner transport infrastructure, and witness security program facilities. Supports the operational needs of the nation’s oldest federal law enforcement agency.
Construction, United States Marshals Ser.b: U.S. Marshals Service personnel, the federal judicial system, protected witnesses

Consumer Financial Civil Penalty Fund, B.d: Receives civil penalties collected by the CFPB from enforcement actions against financial institutions for consumer protection violations. Funds are used to compensate victims of those violations or, if victims cannot be located, for consumer education and financial literacy.
Consumer Financial Civil Penalty Fund, B.b: Victims of consumer financial protection violations, financial literacy programs, consumers

Consumer Operated and Oriented Plan Prog.d: Provided loans to establish consumer-governed, nonprofit health insurance cooperatives (CO-OPs) under the Affordable Care Act. Most CO-OPs have since closed; the account manages remaining loan recoveries.
Consumer Operated and Oriented Plan Prog.b: Health insurance consumers in markets served by CO-OP plans (program largely wound down)

Contingency Fund for State Welfare Progr.d: Provides additional TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families) funding to states experiencing economic downturns or increased welfare caseloads. Acts as a countercyclical buffer for state welfare programs during recessions.
Contingency Fund for State Welfare Progr.b: States with increased welfare needs, low-income families receiving TANF benefits

Continued Dumping and Subsidy Offset, Un.d: Distributed antidumping and countervailing duties collected on unfairly traded imports directly to affected domestic producers under the Byrd Amendment. The program was repealed in 2006 but the account managed remaining distributions.
Continued Dumping and Subsidy Offset, Un.b: Domestic manufacturers harmed by dumped or subsidized imports (program repealed 2006)

Contract Support Costs, Indian Health Se.d: Reimburses Indian tribes and tribal organizations for administrative and overhead costs associated with operating healthcare programs under Indian Self-Determination Act contracts with the Indian Health Service.
Contract Support Costs, Indian Health Se.b: Indian tribes and tribal health organizations operating IHS programs under self-determination contracts

Contributed Funds, Geological Survey, In.d: Receives contributions from state, local, and other federal agencies and non-federal entities for cooperative scientific research and investigations conducted by the U.S. Geological Survey. Enables collaborative geological, hydrological, and biological research.
Contributed Funds, Geological Survey, In.b: Contributing agencies and organizations, scientific research community, public through improved geological knowledge

Contributed Funds, United States Fish an.d: Receives contributions from non-federal sources for fish and wildlife conservation activities conducted cooperatively with the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. Supports research, habitat management, and species conservation projects.
Contributed Funds, United States Fish an.b: Fish and wildlife conservation, contributing partners, the public through enhanced species protection

Contribution for Annuity Benefits, Natio.d: Funds the federal government’s contribution toward retirement annuity benefits for eligible National Park Service employees, particularly those in law enforcement and firefighting positions with enhanced retirement provisions.
Contribution for Annuity Benefits, Natio.b: National Park Service employees, particularly law enforcement rangers and firefighters

Contribution to the African Development.d: Funds the U.S. capital subscription and periodic replenishments to the African Development Bank, a multilateral development bank that finances economic and social development projects in Africa.
Contribution to the African Development.b: African nations receiving development financing, U.S. interests in African economic development

Contribution to the Asian Development Fu.d: Provides the U.S. contribution to the Asian Development Fund, the concessional lending facility of the Asian Development Bank that provides low-interest loans and grants to the poorest countries in Asia and the Pacific.
Contribution to the Asian Development Fu.b: Low-income countries in Asia and the Pacific, vulnerable populations in developing Asian nations

Contribution to the Clean Technology Fun.d: Provides the U.S. contribution to the Clean Technology Fund, one of the Climate Investment Funds that finances large-scale clean energy projects in developing countries to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
Contribution to the Clean Technology Fun.b: Developing countries investing in clean energy, global climate mitigation efforts

Contribution to the European Bank for Re.d: Funds the U.S. capital subscription to the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, established to support the transition of former communist countries in Central and Eastern Europe to market economies.
Contribution to the European Bank for Re.b: Transition economies in Central and Eastern Europe, private sector development in former communist states

Contribution to the International Fund f.d: Provides the U.S. contribution to IFAD, a specialized UN agency that finances agricultural development projects in the poorest rural areas of developing countries. Supports smallholder farmers, food security, and rural livelihoods.
Contribution to the International Fund f.b: Rural poor in developing countries, smallholder farmers, food-insecure populations

Contribution to the Multilateral Investm.d: Funds the U.S. capital subscription to MIGA, a member of the World Bank Group that provides political risk insurance and credit enhancement to foreign investors in developing countries.
Contribution to the Multilateral Investm.b: Foreign investors in developing countries, developing nations seeking to attract foreign investment

Contributions for Highway Research Progr.d: Receives contributions from state highway agencies and other entities for cooperative highway research conducted by the Federal Highway Administration. Supports the Strategic Highway Research Program and related activities.
Contributions for Highway Research Progr.b: State highway departments, transportation researchers, road users through improved highway technology

Contributions for Renewable Energy Impac.d: Receives contributions from renewable energy developers to fund assessments of potential impacts from renewable energy projects on military operations, including radar interference, and mitigation measures. Ensures military readiness while supporting clean energy development.
Contributions for Renewable Energy Impac.b: Military operations potentially affected by renewable energy installations, renewable energy developers

Contributions to International Organizat.d: Funds U.S. assessed contributions to international organizations of which the United States is a member, including the United Nations, NATO, WHO, and dozens of other multilateral bodies. Covers mandatory membership dues.
Contributions to International Organizat.b: International organizations, global governance and cooperation, U.S. diplomatic interests

Contributions to the Cooperative Threat.d: Receives contributions from foreign governments and international organizations to supplement the Nunn-Lugar Cooperative Threat Reduction Program, which secures and dismantles weapons of mass destruction in former Soviet states.
Contributions to the Cooperative Threat.b: Global nonproliferation and security, former Soviet states, international security cooperation

Contributions to the International Monet.d: Funds U.S. contributions to IMF lending facilities and trust funds that provide financial assistance to member countries experiencing balance of payments difficulties. Supports global financial stability.
Contributions to the International Monet.b: IMF member countries facing economic crises, global financial stability, the international monetary system

Contributions, Education.d: Receives voluntary contributions and donations from non-federal sources for educational purposes administered by the Department of Education. Supports specific educational programs and initiatives designated by donors.
Contributions, Education.b: Educational programs receiving donated funds, students and educators

Contributions, American Battle Monuments.d: Receives private contributions and donations to the American Battle Monuments Commission for the construction, maintenance, and improvement of overseas military memorials and cemeteries. Supplements federal appropriations.
Contributions, American Battle Monuments.b: Overseas military memorials and cemeteries, families of fallen service members

Contributions, Indian Health Facilities,.d: Receives contributions from tribal governments and other sources for the construction, maintenance, and improvement of Indian health facilities. Supplements federal funding for healthcare infrastructure serving Native American communities.
Contributions, Indian Health Facilities,.b: Indian tribes, Alaska Natives, Native American communities receiving health services

Cooperative Endangered Species Conservat.d: Provides additional funding from the Land and Water Conservation Fund for cooperative endangered species conservation grants to states and territories. Supplements the primary Cooperative Endangered Species Conservation Fund for land acquisition and habitat conservation.
Cooperative Endangered Species Conservat.b: Endangered species, state conservation programs, landowners participating in habitat conservation

Cooperative Research and Development Agr.d: Manages cooperative research partnerships between CDC and private sector entities to develop and commercialize public health technologies, diagnostics, and treatments. Facilitates technology transfer from CDC laboratories.
Cooperative Research and Development Agr.b: Private sector research partners, public health through commercialized CDC innovations

Cooperative Research and Development Agr.d: Manages cooperative research partnerships between FDA and private sector entities to advance regulatory science, develop new analytical methods, and improve food and drug safety. Facilitates technology transfer and collaborative research.
Cooperative Research and Development Agr.b: Pharmaceutical companies, food safety researchers, consumers through improved regulatory science

Cooperative Research and Development Agr.d: Manages cooperative research partnerships between NIH intramural scientists and private sector entities to advance biomedical research and bring discoveries to market. A key mechanism for NIH technology transfer.
Cooperative Research and Development Agr.b: Biotechnology and pharmaceutical companies, patients through accelerated drug development, NIH researchers

Cooperative Work, Forest Service, Agricu.d: Receives deposits from cooperating entities for work performed by the Forest Service on behalf of states, counties, municipalities, and other organizations on National Forest System lands. Supports collaborative land management projects.
Cooperative Work, Forest Service, Agricu.b: Cooperating state and local agencies, forest management, communities near national forests

Coronavirus Capital Projects Fund, Depar.d: Provides payments to states, territories, and tribal governments to carry out critical capital projects that enable work, education, and health monitoring in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. Focuses on broadband infrastructure and digital connectivity.
Coronavirus Capital Projects Fund, Depar.b: State, territorial, and tribal governments, communities lacking broadband and digital infrastructure

Coronavirus Relief Fund, Departmental Of.d: Provided $150 billion in direct payments to state, local, and tribal governments under the CARES Act to cover expenditures incurred due to the COVID-19 public health emergency. One of the largest pandemic relief programs.
Coronavirus Relief Fund, Departmental Of.b: State, local, and tribal governments, residents affected by COVID-19 pandemic

Corporation for National and Community S.d: A federal agency (also known as AmeriCorps) that supports community service and volunteerism programs including AmeriCorps, Senior Corps, and the Social Innovation Fund. Engages Americans in service to address critical community needs.
Corporation for National and Community S.b: AmeriCorps members, community organizations, communities receiving volunteer services, seniors in Senior Corps

Council of the Inspectors General on Int.d: A coordinating body for the federal Inspectors General community, established by the Inspector General Reform Act. Promotes integrity, economy, and effectiveness across the federal government through coordination of IG activities.
Council of the Inspectors General on Int.b: Federal Inspectors General, government accountability and oversight, taxpayers

Council on Environmental Quality and Off.d: Established by the National Environmental Policy Act, advises the President on environmental policy and oversees federal agency compliance with NEPA, including environmental impact assessments. Coordinates the federal government’s environmental efforts.
Council on Environmental Quality and Off.b: The environment, federal agencies implementing NEPA, the public through environmental protection oversight

Covered Countermeasure Process Fund, Hea.d: Funds the Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program, which provides benefits to individuals injured by covered countermeasures (vaccines, medications, devices) administered during a declared public health emergency. Established under the Public Readiness and Emergency Preparedness Act.
Covered Countermeasure Process Fund, Hea.b: Individuals injured by medical countermeasures during public health emergencies

Crash Data, National Highway Traffic Saf.d: Funds the collection, analysis, and dissemination of motor vehicle crash data through systems such as the Fatality Analysis Reporting System (FARS) and the National Automotive Sampling System. Supports evidence-based highway safety policy.
Crash Data, National Highway Traffic Saf.b: Highway safety researchers, policymakers, vehicle manufacturers, the driving public

Creating Helpful Incentives to Produce S.d: Provides substantial direct funding to incentivize the construction, expansion, and modernization of semiconductor manufacturing facilities in the United States under the CHIPS and Science Act. Includes funding for manufacturing incentives and R&D programs.
Creating Helpful Incentives to Produce S.b: Semiconductor manufacturers, U.S. technology sector, national security, manufacturing workers

Crime Victims Fund, Justice.d: Collects fines, penalties, and forfeited bonds from convicted federal offenders and distributes funds to state victim compensation and assistance programs under the Victims of Crime Act (VOCA). One of the largest funding sources for crime victim services.
Crime Victims Fund, Justice.b: Crime victims, state victim compensation programs, domestic violence shelters, child advocacy centers

Customs and Border Protection Services a.d: Provides customs, immigration, and agricultural inspection services at designated user fee facilities (airports, seaports, and land ports of entry) that reimburse CBP for the cost of services. Funded through user fees from facility operators.
Customs and Border Protection Services a.b: International travelers, importers, facility operators at user-fee ports of entry

Customs and Border Protection, Customs U.d: Collects user fees from commercial merchandise processing at U.S. ports of entry to fund customs inspections, trade enforcement, and border security operations. Known as the Merchandise Processing Fee account.
Customs and Border Protection, Customs U.b: Importers and exporters through trade facilitation, CBP operations, national security

Customs and Border Protection, Immigrati.d: Collects inspection fees from international air and sea passengers and commercial vessels to fund immigration inspection services at U.S. ports of entry. Supports CBP immigration officer staffing and operations.
Customs and Border Protection, Immigrati.b: International travelers, CBP immigration inspection operations

Customs and Border Protection, Land Bord.d: Collects fees for dedicated commuter lane and SENTRI/NEXUS trusted traveler programs at land border ports of entry with Mexico and Canada. Funds expedited processing for pre-approved low-risk travelers.
Customs and Border Protection, Land Bord.b: Frequent border crossers, trusted traveler program participants, land border communities

Customs and Border Protection, Transfer.d: Manages customs operations and the transfer of customs duties collected in Puerto Rico, which are returned to the Puerto Rican treasury under federal law. Funds CBP operations on the island.
Customs and Border Protection, Transfer.b: Puerto Rico treasury (receiving customs revenue), businesses and travelers in Puerto Rico

Cyber Security Initiatives, Office of th.d: Funds cybersecurity programs and initiatives to protect the Department of Transportation’s information systems and the broader transportation infrastructure from cyber threats. Supports network defense, vulnerability assessment, and incident response.
Cyber Security Initiatives, Office of th.b: Transportation systems and infrastructure, DOT information systems, the traveling public

Cybersecurity Enhancement Account, Depar.d: Funds cybersecurity improvements and modernization of information technology security at the Department of the Treasury. Protects financial systems, taxpayer data, and critical Treasury infrastructure from cyber threats.
Cybersecurity Enhancement Account, Depar.b: Treasury Department operations, taxpayers whose data is protected, the financial system

DOD-VA Health Care Sharing Incentive Fun.d: Provides incentive funding to encourage health care resource sharing between the Department of Defense and Department of Veterans Affairs. Supports joint ventures, shared medical facilities, and coordinated care delivery.
DOD-VA Health Care Sharing Incentive Fun.b: Veterans and active-duty military receiving healthcare, VA and DoD medical facilities

Dairy Indemnity Program, Farm Service Ag.d: Provides indemnity payments to dairy farmers and manufacturers of dairy products whose milk has been contaminated by pesticides, chemicals, or nuclear radiation through no fault of their own. Protects dairy farmers from losses due to contamination events.
Dairy Indemnity Program, Farm Service Ag.b: Dairy farmers and dairy product manufacturers affected by contamination, consumers through food safety protection

Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolv.d: Funds the assessment of natural resource damages and restoration of resources on USDA-managed lands injured by hazardous substance releases, oil spills, or other environmental incidents. Recovers costs from responsible parties.
Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolv.b: Natural resources on agricultural lands, communities affected by environmental contamination

Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolv.d: Funds EPA’s natural resource damage assessment activities and environmental restoration following hazardous substance releases or oil spills. Recovered funds from responsible parties are recycled for additional restoration work.
Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolv.b: Damaged ecosystems, communities affected by hazardous releases, environmental restoration

Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolv.d: Funds NOAA’s assessment and restoration of natural resources injured by oil spills, hazardous substance releases, and vessel groundings in coastal and marine environments. Recovers costs from responsible parties for reinvestment in restoration.
Damage Assessment and Restoration Revolv.b: Coastal and marine ecosystems, fisheries, coastal communities, marine wildlife

Data-Driven Innovation, Funds Appropriat.d: Funded cross-agency initiatives to leverage government data and analytics to improve decision-making, service delivery, and operational efficiency across the federal government. Supported open data and evidence-based policymaking.
Data-Driven Innovation, Funds Appropriat.b: Federal agencies, the public through improved government services, data-driven policymaking

Debt Collection Fund, Office of the Secr.d: Funds debt collection activities for debts owed to the Department of Health and Human Services, including overpayments from Medicare, Medicaid, and other HHS programs. Supports recovery of improperly spent federal health funds.
Debt Collection Fund, Office of the Secr.b: HHS programs through recovered funds, taxpayers through reduced improper payments

Debt Restructuring, Treasury.d: Manages the restructuring of bilateral official debt owed by developing countries to the United States, including debt reduction and rescheduling agreements negotiated through the Paris Club. Supports international development through debt relief.
Debt Restructuring, Treasury.b: Heavily indebted developing countries, international debt relief efforts

Defense Emergency Response Fund, Defense.d: Provides rapid-response funding for the Department of Defense to address unforeseen military emergencies and contingency operations. Allows DoD to fund urgent operational needs before regular appropriations are available.
Defense Emergency Response Fund, Defense.b: U.S. military operations, troops deployed in emergency situations, national defense

Defense Nuclear Facilities Safety Board.d: An independent federal agency that provides technical oversight of nuclear weapons facilities operated by the Department of Energy. Reviews safety standards and practices at DOE defense nuclear facilities to protect public health and safety.
Defense Nuclear Facilities Safety Board.b: Workers at DOE nuclear facilities, communities near defense nuclear sites, public safety

Defense Production Act Program Account,.d: Funds the Defense Production Act Title III program, which provides loans, loan guarantees, purchase commitments, and other incentives to develop and maintain domestic industrial capabilities critical to national defense.
Defense Production Act Program Account,.b: Defense industrial base, manufacturers of defense-critical materials and components, national security

Delaware Water Gap Route 209 Operations,.d: Funds the operation and maintenance of Route 209 through the Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area in Pennsylvania and New Jersey. Covers road maintenance, safety improvements, and traffic management on this federally managed highway.
Delaware Water Gap Route 209 Operations,.b: Motorists using Route 209, visitors to Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area, local communities

Delta Regional Authority.d: A federal-state partnership that promotes economic development in the Mississippi River Delta region spanning 252 counties across eight states from Illinois to Louisiana. Invests in infrastructure, workforce development, and community improvement in distressed areas.
Delta Regional Authority.b: Residents of the Delta region, particularly economically distressed communities in the lower Mississippi River area

Denali Commission.d: An independent federal agency that provides critical infrastructure and economic development support to remote communities in Alaska, particularly Alaska Native villages. Addresses the unique infrastructure challenges of rural Alaska.
Denali Commission.b: Remote Alaska communities, Alaska Native villages, rural Alaskans

Denali Commission Trust Fund.d: The trust fund that finances the Denali Commission’s operations and grants for infrastructure, energy, health care, and economic development in rural Alaska. Receives deposits from federal agencies and other sources.
Denali Commission Trust Fund.b: Rural Alaska communities, Alaska Native villages, the Denali Commission’s grant programs

Department of Defense Acquisition Workfo.d: Funds the recruitment, training, and retention of the Department of Defense acquisition workforce to improve management of defense contracts and procurement. Finances education, training, and strategic human capital initiatives.
Department of Defense Acquisition Workfo.b: DoD acquisition professionals, defense contracting operations, taxpayers through improved procurement

Department of Defense Base Closure Accou.d: Funds the implementation of military base closure and realignment decisions from the 2005 Base Realignment and Closure (BRAC) round. Covers environmental cleanup, facility construction, and property disposal costs.
Department of Defense Base Closure Accou.b: Military communities affected by BRAC 2005, receiving communities, environmental cleanup

Department of Defense Base Closure Accou.d: Funds the implementation of military base closures and realignments from the 1988, 1991, 1993, and 1995 BRAC rounds. Covers ongoing environmental remediation, caretaker costs, and property conveyance at closed bases.
Department of Defense Base Closure Accou.b: Communities near closed military bases, environmental remediation, redevelopment authorities

Department of Defense Family Housing Imp.d: Provides alternative financing mechanisms for military family housing through public-private partnerships under the Military Housing Privatization Initiative. Allows DoD to leverage private capital to improve on-base family housing.
Department of Defense Family Housing Imp.b: Military families living in on-base housing, private housing partners

Department of Defense General Gift Fund,.d: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations from individuals and organizations for the benefit of the Department of Defense, its personnel, and their dependents. Used for morale, welfare, and recreation purposes.
Department of Defense General Gift Fund,.b: Military personnel and their families, DoD morale, welfare, and recreation programs

Department of Defense Military Unaccompa.d: Provides funding for public-private partnerships to construct, renovate, and maintain unaccompanied military housing (barracks and dormitories) for service members without dependents. Mirrors the family housing privatization initiative.
Department of Defense Military Unaccompa.b: Single military service members and those stationed without families

Department of Defense Rapid Prototyping.d: Funds the rapid prototyping and fielding of innovative technologies to address emerging military threats. Accelerates the transition of promising technologies from development to operational use.
Department of Defense Rapid Prototyping.b: U.S. military forces, defense technology developers, national defense capabilities

Department of Defense Vietnam War Commem.d: Receives donations to support the 50th Anniversary Vietnam War Commemoration program honoring Vietnam veterans and their families. Funds commemorative events, outreach, and recognition activities.
Department of Defense Vietnam War Commem.b: Vietnam War veterans and their families, the public through historical remembrance

Department of Defense, Forest Products P.d: Manages timber sales and forest products on military installations, with proceeds used for reforestation, road maintenance, and natural resource management on Army lands. Supports sustainable forestry on military bases.
Department of Defense, Forest Products P.b: Military installations, timber purchasers, natural resource management on Army lands

Department of Education Nonrecurring Exp.d: Receives unobligated balances from expired Department of Education appropriations and repurposes them for information technology infrastructure, acquisition of capital equipment, and facility improvements. Reduces waste from expired but unspent funds.
Department of Education Nonrecurring Exp.b: Department of Education operations, IT modernization, administrative efficiency

Department of Veterans Affairs Cemetery.d: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations for the benefit of VA national cemeteries, including cemetery beautification, memorial programs, and historic preservation. Supplements appropriated funds for cemetery operations.
Department of Veterans Affairs Cemetery.b: Veterans’ families, national cemetery visitors, cemetery beautification and preservation

Department of the Air Force General Gift.d: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations from individuals and organizations for the benefit of Air Force and Space Force personnel, their dependents, and morale, welfare, and recreation programs.
Department of the Air Force General Gift.b: Air Force and Space Force personnel and their families, morale and welfare programs

Department of the Army General Gift Fund.d: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations from individuals and organizations for the benefit of Army personnel, their dependents, and morale, welfare, and recreation programs.
Department of the Army General Gift Fund.b: Army personnel and their families, morale, welfare, and recreation programs

Department of the Navy General Gift Fund.d: Receives gifts, bequests, and donations from individuals and organizations for the benefit of Navy and Marine Corps personnel, their dependents, and morale, welfare, and recreation programs.
Department of the Navy General Gift Fund.b: Navy and Marine Corps personnel and their families, morale and welfare programs

Department-Wide Systems and Capital Inve.d: Funds major information technology investments and capital improvement projects that serve multiple bureaus and offices across the Department of the Treasury. Supports enterprise-wide IT modernization and infrastructure.
Department-Wide Systems and Capital Inve.b: Treasury Department bureaus, taxpayers through improved government efficiency and services

Department-Wide Technology Investments,.d: Funds enterprise-wide information technology investments across the Department of Homeland Security, including shared IT infrastructure, cybersecurity, and data analytics capabilities. Supports departmental integration and information sharing.
Department-Wide Technology Investments,.b: DHS component agencies, national security and homeland security operations

Departmental Operations, Land and Water.d: Funds the Department of the Interior’s administration of the Land and Water Conservation Fund, including land acquisition for federal conservation purposes and grants to states for outdoor recreation. Supported by offshore oil and gas revenues.
Departmental Operations, Land and Water.b: Public lands, state and local parks, outdoor recreation, conservation

Detention and Removal Operations, U.S. I.d: Funds ICE’s detention of aliens in removal proceedings and the execution of removal orders for those found to be unlawfully present or deportable. Covers detention facility operations, transportation, and removal flights.
Detention and Removal Operations, U.S. I.b: Immigration enforcement operations, public safety through removal of deportable individuals

Digital Equity, National Telecommunicati.d: Funds programs to promote digital equity and inclusion by ensuring all individuals and communities have the skills, technology, and capacity needed to benefit from broadband internet. Established under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
Digital Equity, National Telecommunicati.b: Underserved communities, digital literacy programs, populations on the wrong side of the digital divide

Disaster Loan Fund, Liquidating Account,.d: Manages the legacy portfolio of SBA disaster loans made before the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990. Collects repayments and handles defaults on pre-reform disaster loans to homeowners, renters, and businesses.
Disaster Loan Fund, Liquidating Account,.b: Disaster-affected homeowners, renters, and businesses with pre-1992 SBA disaster loans

Disposal of Department of Defense Real P.d: Manages the disposal and conveyance of excess and surplus military real property, including land, buildings, and facilities no longer needed by the Department of Defense. Facilitates property transfer for community redevelopment.
Disposal of Department of Defense Real P.b: Communities receiving former military properties, redevelopment authorities, DoD through cost avoidance

Distance Learning, Telemedicine and Broa.d: Provides grants and loans to rural communities for distance learning and telemedicine technologies, and broadband infrastructure. Connects rural schools, libraries, and healthcare facilities with advanced telecommunications.
Distance Learning, Telemedicine and Broa.b: Rural students, rural healthcare patients, rural communities lacking broadband access

District of Columbia Crime Victims Compe.d: Provides financial compensation to victims of violent crime in the District of Columbia for unreimbursed medical expenses, lost wages, and other costs resulting from criminal victimization. Administered by the D.C. courts.
District of Columbia Crime Victims Compe.b: Victims of violent crime in the District of Columbia

District of Columbia Federal Pension Fun.d: Manages the federal government’s assumption of pension liabilities for District of Columbia police, firefighters, teachers, and judges hired before July 1, 1997. Established when the federal government took over pre-existing D.C. pension obligations.
District of Columbia Federal Pension Fun.b: Retired D.C. police, firefighters, teachers, and judges hired before 1997

District of Columbia Judicial Retirement.d: Funds retirement annuities and survivor benefits for judges of the District of Columbia courts. Managed by Treasury as part of the federal government’s financial responsibilities for D.C. governance.
District of Columbia Judicial Retirement.b: Retired D.C. judges and their survivors

Domestic Trafficking Victims’ Fund, Offi.d: Provides funding for services and assistance to domestic victims of human trafficking, including housing, legal aid, mental health services, and case management. Funded by special assessments on convicted sex traffickers and purchasers of commercial sex.
Domestic Trafficking Victims’ Fund, Offi.b: Victims of domestic human trafficking, organizations providing anti-trafficking services

Donations for Forest and Rangeland Resea.d: Receives donations from individuals, corporations, and other entities for forest and rangeland research conducted by the Forest Service’s Research and Development branch. Supplements federal research funding with private contributions.
Donations for Forest and Rangeland Resea.b: Forest and rangeland research, Forest Service scientists, forest management practices

Donations, Advisory Council on Historic.d: Receives voluntary donations to the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation for the promotion of historic preservation activities. Supplements appropriated funds for the council’s operations and programs.
Donations, Advisory Council on Historic.b: Historic preservation programs, the Advisory Council’s mission

Donations, National Park Service.d: Receives and manages donations of money, land, and other assets given to the National Park Service for the benefit of the national park system. Supports specific park units, programs, and conservation activities as designated by donors.
Donations, National Park Service.b: National parks, park visitors, conservation and preservation efforts

Donations, National Science Foundation.d: Receives donations from individuals, corporations, and foundations to support scientific research and education programs administered by the National Science Foundation. Supplements federal research funding.
Donations, National Science Foundation.b: Scientific research, NSF programs, researchers, students

Dual Benefits Payments Account, Railroad.d: Funds the "windfall" dual benefit payments to retired railroad workers who qualified for both railroad retirement and Social Security benefits based on work in both the railroad industry and covered employment. A legacy obligation being paid down.
Dual Benefits Payments Account, Railroad.b: Retired railroad workers eligible for both railroad retirement and Social Security benefits

EEOC Education, Technical Assistance and.d: A revolving fund that provides education, technical assistance, and training programs on equal employment opportunity laws on a fee-for-service basis. Supports employer compliance training and anti-discrimination education.
EEOC Education, Technical Assistance and.b: Employers seeking EEO compliance training, employees, HR professionals

Early Retiree Reinsurance Program, Offic.d: Provided temporary reinsurance to employer-sponsored health plans covering early retirees (aged 55-64) to help reduce premium costs before ACA health insurance exchanges became operational. The program is fully expended.
Early Retiree Reinsurance Program, Offic.b: Early retirees aged 55-64, employer-sponsored health plans covering retirees (program exhausted)

East-West Center, State.d: Funds the East-West Center in Honolulu, Hawaii, which promotes understanding and cooperation among the peoples and nations of Asia, the Pacific, and the United States through research, education, and dialogue programs.
East-West Center, State.b: Scholars, students, and policymakers from the U.S. and Asia-Pacific region

Economic Development Assistance Programs.d: Provides grants for public works, economic adjustment, planning, and technical assistance to economically distressed communities through the Economic Development Administration. Supports infrastructure, innovation, and regional economic diversification.
Economic Development Assistance Programs.b: Economically distressed communities, regional development organizations, local governments

Economic Research Service, Agriculture.d: Funds the operations of the USDA’s Economic Research Service, which conducts economic analysis and research on agriculture, food, natural resources, and rural development. Provides data and analysis to inform farm and food policy.
Economic Research Service, Agriculture.b: Agricultural policymakers, farmers, food industry, researchers, rural communities

Economic Stabilization Program Account,.d: Managed the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) and related financial stabilization programs during the 2008 financial crisis. Authorized under the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act to purchase troubled assets and inject capital into financial institutions.
Economic Stabilization Program Account,.b: Financial system stability, banks and financial institutions during the crisis, the broader economy

Election Security Grants, Election Assis.d: Provides federal grants to states to improve the administration and security of federal elections, including upgrading voting equipment, implementing post-election audits, and enhancing cybersecurity of election infrastructure.
Election Security Grants, Election Assis.b: State and local election administrators, voters, election infrastructure security

Elective Payment for Energy Property and.d: Provides direct elective payments (direct pay) to tax-exempt entities, state and local governments, and other eligible organizations for clean energy tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act. Allows entities without tax liability to benefit from energy credits.
Elective Payment for Energy Property and.b: Tax-exempt organizations, state and local governments, tribal entities investing in clean energy

Electric or Low-Emitting Ferry Program,.d: Provides competitive grants for the purchase of electric or low-emitting ferry vessels and related charging or fueling infrastructure. Established by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to reduce emissions from ferry operations.
Electric or Low-Emitting Ferry Program,.b: Ferry operators, coastal and island communities, the environment through reduced maritime emissions

Electronic System for Travel Authorizati.d: Operates the ESTA system that screens Visa Waiver Program travelers before they board U.S.-bound flights or vessels. Collects travel promotion fees to fund the system and Brand USA tourism marketing.
Electronic System for Travel Authorizati.b: Visa Waiver Program travelers, U.S. border security, tourism promotion

Emergencies in the Diplomatic and Consul.d: Provides emergency funding for unforeseen diplomatic and consular emergencies, including evacuation of U.S. citizens abroad, emergency security at diplomatic posts, and emergency assistance to Americans overseas.
Emergencies in the Diplomatic and Consul.b: U.S. citizens abroad needing emergency assistance, diplomatic personnel, consular operations

Emergency Capital Investment Fund, Depar.d: Provides capital investments in Community Development Financial Institutions and Minority Depository Institutions to support lending in low- and moderate-income and minority communities. Established under the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021.
Emergency Capital Investment Fund, Depar.b: CDFIs, Minority Depository Institutions, low-income and minority communities, small businesses

Emergency Citrus Disease Research and De.d: Funds emergency research to combat citrus greening disease (Huanglongbing), which threatens the U.S. citrus industry. Supports development of disease-resistant citrus varieties, treatments, and management strategies.
Emergency Citrus Disease Research and De.b: Citrus growers, the citrus industry, agricultural researchers, consumers

Emergency EIDL Grants, Small Business Ad.d: Provided emergency Economic Injury Disaster Loan (EIDL) advance grants to small businesses and nonprofits experiencing economic harm from the COVID-19 pandemic. Grants of up to $10,000 did not need to be repaid.
Emergency EIDL Grants, Small Business Ad.b: Small businesses, nonprofits, and sole proprietors affected by the COVID-19 pandemic

Emergency Forest Restoration Program, Fa.d: Provides cost-share payments to private forest landowners for emergency restoration of forest resources damaged by natural disasters such as wildfires, hurricanes, and ice storms. Helps restore non-industrial private forest lands.
Emergency Forest Restoration Program, Fa.b: Private forest landowners whose forests were damaged by natural disasters

Emergency Homeowner’s Relief Fund, Housi.d: Provided emergency assistance to homeowners at risk of foreclosure due to involuntary unemployment or underemployment, covering mortgage payments, insurance, and taxes. Established under the Dodd-Frank Act.
Emergency Homeowner’s Relief Fund, Housi.b: Homeowners at risk of foreclosure due to unemployment (program largely expired)

Emergency Preparedness Grant, Pipeline a.d: Provides grants to states, territories, and tribal governments to develop and improve emergency response plans and training for hazardous materials transportation incidents. Supports first responder preparedness for hazmat emergencies.
Emergency Preparedness Grant, Pipeline a.b: State and local emergency responders, communities along hazardous materials transportation routes

Emergency Relief Program, Federal Highwa.d: Provides federal funding for the repair and reconstruction of federal-aid highways and roads on federal lands that have been damaged by natural disasters or catastrophic failures. Supplements regular highway funding after declared emergencies.
Emergency Relief Program, Federal Highwa.b: State and local transportation agencies, motorists, communities with disaster-damaged roads
